title: Type narrowing in conditionals (instanceof/is/match)
description:
When the inference engine encounters instanceof (Java/TS), is (C#/Kotlin), or pattern match with type checks (Scala/Rust), it should narrow the variable's type within the true branch.

Example: if (x instanceof Animal) { x.speak(); } — inside the branch, x should be narrowed from Object to Animal. is_subtype_expr() is needed to validate that the narrowing is sound (e.g., Dog narrows to Animal because Dog <: Animal).

Requires:
1. Wire extend_with_interfaces() into infer_types() to build the augmented TypeGraph
2. Add type narrowing logic to _infer_branch_if or a new narrowing pass
3. Track narrowed types per-scope (similar to TypeScript's control flow narrowing)

This is the most likely first consumer of is_subtype_expr(). Intersects heavily with red-dragon-jn0 (pattern matching).

title: TypeGraph interface subtyping: production consumers
description:
extend_with_interfaces() adds class→interface edges to the TypeGraph so is_subtype_expr() works across interface hierarchies. Currently no production code consumes this. This epic tracks the three scenarios that would require it, each as a child story.

Prerequisite: extend_with_interfaces() itself works (tested in test_type_graph.py). The work here is wiring it into the inference/execution pipeline AND building the consumers.

Intersects with red-dragon-jn0 (pattern matching) — type narrowing from instanceof/is checks is the most likely first consumer.

title: COBOL: SEARCH ALL (binary search) lowered as linear SEARCH; no binary/KEY semantics
description:
SEARCH ALL is not distinguished from a plain serial SEARCH. The bridge serializeSearch() emits the same SEARCH shape for both, so the SEARCH-ALL form is lowered as a linear scan. Behaviorally a linear scan still finds an equality match, but the true SEARCH ALL contract (sorted table, KEY-only equality conditions ANDed, O(log n), index left at the matching occurrence or AT END) is not modeled.

EVIDENCE:
- proleap-bridge serializeSearch() reads getDataCall / getVaryingPhrase / getWhenPhrases / getAtEndPhrase but never an ALL/binary indicator.
- ProLeap SearchStatement interface does not surface an ALL flag in the basic getters; the ALL vs serial distinction must be recovered (grammar context or a separate metamodel path) before the bridge can mark it.
- CobolFeature.SEARCH_BINARY is uncovered (feature_coverage_audit.py).

WHY IT MATTERS: SEARCH ALL requires the table be sorted on its KEY and only permits equality (and ANDed equality) on KEY fields; programs that rely on binary semantics (or on the index landing exactly) can misbehave if treated as linear with arbitrary WHEN conditions. Also a perf/semantics fidelity gap.

REMEDIATION:
1. Determine how ProLeap exposes SEARCH ALL (grammar context flag) and serialize an 'all: true' marker + the KEY operands.
2. Python: lower SEARCH ALL as a binary search over the table's OCCURS KEY, setting the index and running the WHEN/AT END branches.
3. Add a @covers test for SEARCH_BINARY through run() (found case + AT END case).

Found in COBOL language-coverage audit 2026-06-22.

title: COBOL: USAGE IS INDEX data items and INDEXED BY not modeled
description:
USAGE IS INDEX index data items and the INDEXED BY clause on OCCURS are not modeled in the data layout. SEARCH/SET against named indices therefore have no backing storage of the correct (index) kind.

EVIDENCE:
- No handling of USAGE INDEX in interpreter/cobol/data_layout.py or asg_types.py (grep for index/INDEXED BY finds nothing structural).
- CobolFeature.USAGE_INDEX exists but is uncovered (feature_coverage_audit.py).
- INDEXED BY idx on '05 T OCCURS n INDEXED BY IDX' declares IDX as an index-name (not an ordinary numeric field); SET IDX TO k / SET IDX UP BY k and SEARCH ... VARYING IDX rely on it.

SCOPE:
- USAGE IS INDEX elementary items (standalone index data items).
- INDEXED BY clause creating implicit index-names tied to a table.
- SET semantics on index-names (occurrence number, 1-based) vs ordinary fields.

REMEDIATION:
1. Parse/serialize INDEXED BY names + USAGE INDEX in the bridge data-division extraction.
2. Model index-names in the layout (a 1-based occurrence counter; commonly a binary fullword).
3. Wire SET <index> TO / UP BY / DOWN BY and SEARCH VARYING <index> to the modeled storage.
4. Integration tests through run(): SET IDX TO 3, read T(IDX); SEARCH VARYING IDX.

NOTE: related to SEARCH coverage; SEARCH currently lowers VARYING but the index backing is the open question here.

Found in COBOL language-coverage audit 2026-06-22.

title: COBOL: most intrinsic FUNCTIONs silently fall back to first argument (~10 of ~40 supported)
description:
Only 10 COBOL intrinsic functions are implemented; any other FUNCTION call silently returns its FIRST ARGUMENT instead of computing the function — a quiet wrong-result gap (no error, no NotImplementedError).

EVIDENCE:
- interpreter/cobol/lower_arithmetic.py _INTRINSIC_FUNCTIONS maps only: UPPER-CASE, LOWER-CASE, TRIM, CURRENT-DATE, LENGTH, NUMVAL, NUMVAL-C, TEST-NUMVAL, TEST-NUMVAL-C, INTEGER-OF-DATE.
- Fallback at lower_arithmetic.py:277 and condition_lowering.py:1014: 'Unsupported COBOL intrinsic FUNCTION %r — falling back to first argument'.
- Enum: features.py has a single coarse INTRINSIC_FUNCTION member ('UPPER-CASE / LOWER-CASE / TRIM / CURRENT-DATE'), so the coverage gate cannot see the missing ones.

MISSING (common, non-exhaustive): REVERSE, MAX, MIN, MOD, REM, SUM, MEAN, MEDIAN, RANGE, INTEGER, INTEGER-PART, ABS/SIGN, SQRT, FACTORIAL, ORD, ORD-MAX/ORD-MIN, CHAR, NUMVAL already done, DATE-OF-INTEGER (inverse of INTEGER-OF-DATE), WHEN-COMPILED, DATE-TO-YYYYMMDD, DAY-OF-INTEGER, the trig/log family (SIN/COS/TAN/ASIN/ACOS/ATAN/LOG/LOG10/EXP), STORED-CHAR-LENGTH, LENGTH already done.

RISK: falling back to arg-1 makes e.g. FUNCTION MAX(A B C) return A, FUNCTION REVERSE(S) return S unchanged — wrong but silent.

REMEDIATION:
1. Decide enumeration granularity (per-intrinsic enum members vs grouped) so coverage reflects reality without gaming.
2. Implement the high-frequency set first: REVERSE, MAX, MIN, MOD, INTEGER, ABS, SUM, MEAN, ORD, CHAR.
3. Replace the silent arg-1 fallback with a loud unsupported path (or implement) so future gaps are visible.
4. Integration tests per intrinsic through run().

Found in COBOL language-coverage audit 2026-06-22.

title: COBOL: GO TO ... DEPENDING ON (computed GOTO) silently dropped
description:
GO TO p1 p2 p3 ... DEPENDING ON idx is not lowered — the bridge GOTO serializer only handles the simple form and silently drops the computed form, producing an empty operands array (so the statement becomes a no-op at runtime: control falls through instead of branching to the idx-th target).

EVIDENCE:
- proleap-bridge StatementSerializer.java serializeGoTo() only reads stmt.getSimple().getProcedureCall(); it never inspects the DEPENDING-ON (getDepending()) form. On a computed GOTO getSimple() is null, so operands stays empty.
- No 'depending' key is emitted for GOTO; GotoStatement in interpreter/cobol/cobol_statements.py has no depending-index field.
- Enum: features.py has no computed-GOTO member (OCCURS_DEPENDING_ON is unrelated).

SEMANTICS: GO TO p1 p2 ... pN DEPENDING ON idx transfers control to the idx-th paragraph (1-based) when 1<=idx<=N; otherwise falls through. Used in older dispatch/state-machine COBOL.

REMEDIATION:
1. Bridge: serialize the depending form — emit the ordered list of target paragraph names + the depending data-name.
2. Python: extend GotoStatement with optional targets list + depending field; lower to a branch table (decode idx, bounds-check, BRANCH to the idx-th label, else fall through).
3. Add a CobolFeature member (GOTO_DEPENDING_ON).
4. Integration test through run(): idx selecting 1st/2nd/3rd target + out-of-range fall-through.

Found in COBOL language-coverage audit 2026-06-22.
close_reason: Implemented + merged to main locally (commits 16fb50ef, d68b717d, e2568f4e; fast-forward). GO TO ... DEPENDING ON now lowers as a 1-based chained-BranchIf branch table with silent out-of-range fall-through. Sum-type data model (SimpleGoto|ComputedGoto|AlteredGoto in GotoStatement.form; new ProcedureRef; index reuses RefModOperand); bridge emits explicit form discriminator + serializeProcedureRef. Added CobolFeature.GOTO_DEPENDING_ON. 7 integration tests (index matrix incl. both fall-through cases, qualified index, section-qualified target) + 3 dataclass round-trip unit tests + 1 bridge JSON test; full suite green. Spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-23-cobol-computed-goto-design.md; plan docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-23-cobol-computed-goto.md. Two clean per-task reviews + MERGE-READY final review. Deferred follow-up (non-blocking): a dedicated subscripted-index integration test (the subscript path delegates to the shared, already-tested resolve_field_ref, which itself supports only a single subscript per red-dragon-cqwx). Future work captured in spec: section/paragraph symbol cache for static qualified-name resolution (ProcedureRef.section carried but resolved flat as para_{paragraph} today). NOT pushed to origin (merged locally per user).

title: COBOL: ADD CORRESPONDING / SUBTRACT CORRESPONDING not lowered
description:
SYMPTOM: ADD CORRESPONDING and SUBTRACT CORRESPONDING are not serialised by the ProLeap bridge and have no lowering handler. Programs using them will produce wrong results or raise errors.

ROOT CAUSE:
- interpreter/cobol/cobol_statements.py has MoveCorrespondingStatement but no AddCorrespondingStatement / SubtractCorrespondingStatement
- The ProLeap bridge StatementSerializer has no serializeAddCorresponding() / serializeSubtractCorresponding() methods
- The bridge falls through to serializeUnknown() for these statement types

COBOL semantics:
  ADD CORRESPONDING src TO dst
Equivalent to: for each field name that exists in both src and dst at the same level, emit ADD src.field TO dst.field.

  SUBTRACT CORRESPONDING src FROM dst
Equivalent to: for each matching field name, emit SUBTRACT src.field FROM dst.field.

SCOPE:
1. proleap-bridge StatementSerializer.java — add serializeAddCorresponding() and serializeSubtractCorresponding() following the same pattern as serializeMoveCorresponding()
2. interpreter/cobol/cobol_statements.py — add AddCorrespondingStatement, SubtractCorrespondingStatement dataclasses matching MoveCorrespondingStatement shape
3. interpreter/cobol/lower_procedure.py — add lowering handlers matching lower_move_corresponding() but using arithmetic

RELATED: MoveCorrespondingStatement at cobol_statements.py:207 is the model to follow.
close_reason: Implemented: bridge serializes ADD_CORRESPONDING/SUBTRACT_CORRESPONDING; ArithmeticCorrespondingStatement + lower_arithmetic_corresponding operate on matching leaf names. 2 e2e tests in TestAddCorresponding (commit ef21e719). Pushed.

title: make test does not export PROLEAP_BRIDGE_JAR to the pytest subprocess
description: The Makefile sets 'PROLEAP_BRIDGE_JAR ?= $(CURDIR)/...' as a make VARIABLE but never 'export's it, so 'make test' runs pytest WITHOUT PROLEAP_BRIDGE_JAR in the environment unless the caller's shell already exported it. Most COBOL tests resolve the JAR via a fallback, but tests/integration/test_bridge_structured_subscripts.py reads os.environ['PROLEAP_BRIDGE_JAR'] directly (bare KeyError, no fallback), so it ERRORs at setup under 'make test' (and thus the pre-commit hook) whenever the committer's shell didn't export the var. Symptom: pre-commit 'make test' aborts with '1 error' at a random point (xdist -x + random order), looking like a flaky test, when it's actually a deterministic missing-env-var setup error. Cost ~30min of false 'flaky parallel race' diagnosis. Fix options: (1) add 'export PROLEAP_BRIDGE_JAR' in the Makefile so the recipe subprocess always gets it; and/or (2) give the bridge_jar fixture in test_bridge_structured_subscripts.py the same fail-loud-with-clear-message or default-path fallback the other COBOL tests use. Prefer (1) (robust for the hook regardless of caller shell).
acceptance_criteria: make test (and the pre-commit hook) runs the bridge tests with PROLEAP_BRIDGE_JAR available regardless of the caller's shell env; test_bridge_structured_subscripts no longer errors at setup under make test.

title: Signal COBOL ABEND from the VM (abnormal termination), distinct from STOP RUN
description: An ABEND (abnormal end: S0Cx, a USER abend, or a program CALLing an LE abend service like CEE3ABD) is distinct from a normal STOP RUN/GOBACK and from a high RETURN-CODE. On z/OS an unhandled abend flushes all remaining job steps. Today red-dragon has no abend concept: CEE3ABD is an unresolved CALL that goes symbolic (the CBACT01C spike confirmed it neither halts nor signals), and VMState has no abend/halt-reason field. Needed so a JCL orchestrator (jackal) can implement standard abend-flush (stop running remaining steps on abend) and distinguish abnormal from normal completion. Design: model abend as a VM halt-with-reason — recognize the LE abend services (CEE3ABD/CEE3AB2) and/or runtime traps (e.g. S0C7 on bad packed data) as an abnormal-termination signal carrying an abend/condition code, surfaced on the returned VMState (e.g. VMState.abend_code: int | None or a halt-reason enum). Separate from RETURN-CODE (red-dragon special-register ticket). Surfaced by jackal v1 (2026-06-20).
acceptance_criteria: A program that CALLs CEE3ABD terminates with an observable abend signal on the returned VMState distinct from normal completion; a normal GOBACK shows no abend; integration test for both paths.

title: Model RETURN-CODE special register: store writes + expose on VMState
description: COBOL RETURN-CODE is a special register (compiler-synthesized, implicit PIC S9(4) COMP, not in the DATA DIVISION) whose value at GOBACK/STOP RUN becomes the step's OS return/COND code. red-dragon currently DROPS writes to it: lower_arithmetic.py lists RETURN-CODE in _SPECIAL_REGISTER_NAMES and treats 'MOVE n TO RETURN-CODE' as a no-op (comment: dropping the write is behaviour-preserving because callers read the LINKAGE result, not the register). Result: RETURN-CODE is unobservable. Needed so an external orchestrator (jackal, the JCL runner) can read each step's return code. Design: give RETURN-CODE a real addressable home as a COBOL-runtime special register (NOT a JCL-specific copybook — every COBOL program has RETURN-CODE regardless of JCL; cf. how CICS injects DFHEIBLK, but RETURN-CODE is COBOL-universal). Allocate a synthetic special-register field/region (implicit S9(4) COMP) when a program references RETURN-CODE (or universally), route MOVEs to it, and expose its final value on the returned VMState (e.g. VMState.return_code). Other special registers (SORT-RETURN, TALLY) share this shape; consider a general special-registers area. Surfaced by jackal v1 (2026-06-20); jackal v2 will consume it.
acceptance_criteria: MOVE n TO RETURN-CODE is observable; after run(), the program's final RETURN-CODE value is readable from the returned VMState; integration test: a program that MOVEs 8 TO RETURN-CODE then GOBACKs exposes 8.
notes:
DESIGN REFINEMENT (2026-06-20): model RETURN-CODE as ordinary storage, NOT VM-level special handling. Inject a synthetic special-registers group into the materialised layout at build time: a known 01 field RETURN-CODE PIC S9(4) COMP (2-byte binary, init 0) at a fixed region/offset (same injection pattern as CICS DFHEIBLK, but COBOL-universal). Then REMOVE 'RETURN-CODE' from _SPECIAL_REGISTER_NAMES in lower_arithmetic.py so resolve_field_ref finds the real field and 'MOVE n TO RETURN-CODE' lowers through the existing MOVE->encode->WRITE_REGION path — no new opcode, no executor branch, no VMState.return_code field. Consumers read the value by decoding that known field from the returned VMState's region (same as how tests decode WS fields). Generalizes to SORT-RETURN/TALLY (same group). Caveat: field must carry implicit S9(4) COMP type for correct binary encode/decode; RETURN-CODE is run-unit-global (single WS-level slot). This makes the lowering uniform and the VM untouched.
DONE 2026-06-20, merged to red-dragon main (merge da4b111e, feature a94d75da). Dedicated isolated SR region; MOVE via ordinary encode/WRITE_REGION (no VM changes); read_return_code(vm) reads it back; bridge recovers real names for name=[null] operands (broad/fail-loud per user decision). Reviewed (opus) Approved. Follow-up filed for broad-recovery regression coverage + brittle-test cleanup. JAR must be rebuilt (mvn -DskipTests package) to pick up the bridge change.

title: Resolve COBOL files by ASSIGN name (DDname), not SELECT name
description: Today red-dragon's RealFileIOProvider / lower_io resolve a file by its SELECT name (e.g. ACCTFILE-FILE): lower_open passes the OPEN statement's file (SELECT) names, and _resolve_path checks path_overrides by that SELECT name. The correct COBOL external-binding contract is the ASSIGN clause: SELECT <sel> ASSIGN TO <name> binds the file to external name <name>, which on z/OS is the JCL DDname. To let an external orchestrator (jackal, the JCL runner) wire datasets by DDname, red-dragon should resolve files by their ASSIGN name. Approach: run()/run_linked injects the parsed file_control (FileControlEntry.assign_to) into the io_provider after parse; RealFileIOProvider resolves a SELECT file to its assign_to and looks up path_overrides / paths by assign_to (DDname). Keep SELECT-name fallback for programs run without a JCL layer. Not needed where SELECT name == ASSIGN name. Surfaced during jackal v1 design (2026-06-20); jackal v1 sidesteps it by using matching names in synthetic programs, but jackal v3 (real batch corpus, e.g. ACCTFILE-FILE vs ACCTFILE) requires it.
acceptance_criteria: A COBOL program with SELECT <s> ASSIGN TO <dd> (s != dd) run via run(io_provider=RealFileIOProvider(path_overrides={<dd>: path})) opens/reads/writes the file at path; integration test with non-matching SELECT/ASSIGN names; SELECT-name resolution still works when no file_control injected.
notes: DONE 2026-06-20 (red-dragon main 64588713). RealFileIOProvider._resolve_path now matches path_overrides by the ASSIGN name (DDname) in addition to the SELECT name — additive, backward-compatible (SELECT-name still takes precedence). Unit test test_override_resolves_by_assign_name (SELECT IN-FILE ASSIGN TO INDD, override keyed by INDD). Full suite green via pre-commit. Unblocks jackal v1 DDname-keyed dataset wiring (jackal Task 1).

title: Figurative constant (ZERO) as arithmetic operand in GIVING path crashes lowering
description: Found by spike running real batch program CBACT01C. 'ADD 8 TO ZERO GIVING APPL-RESULT' (and 'ADD 12 TO ZERO GIVING ...') crash in interpreter/cobol/lower_arithmetic.py lower_arithmetic_giving._decode_operand (~line 796): operand 'ZERO' falls through to ctx.const_to_reg(float(field_name)) -> ValueError: could not convert string to float: 'ZERO'. Figurative constants (ZERO/ZEROS/ZEROES, and likely SPACE/HIGH-VALUE/LOW-VALUE) are not resolved in this path. condition_lowering.py already has _FIGURATIVE_FILL; reuse the canonical resolver. Fix: resolve figurative constants to their numeric/byte value before the float() fallback in _decode_operand.
acceptance_criteria: ADD/SUBTRACT/MULTIPLY/DIVIDE ... GIVING with a figurative-constant operand lowers and executes; ZERO->0; integration test on a GIVING-with-ZERO program.
notes: DONE 2026-06-21, merged to main. _decode_operand (GIVING) + lower_arithmetic plain-ADD source now translate_cobol_figurative before float(): ZERO/ZEROS/ZEROES -> 0; non-numeric figuratives still fail loudly. Integration test test_cobol_figurative_arithmetic.py. Full suite + CICS e2e green.

title: Sign conditions (IF x IS POSITIVE/NEGATIVE/ZERO) crash with KeyError 'left' in _lower_relation_node
description:
Found 2026-06-18 while auditing red-dragon-9cxh (unrelated to subscripts).

SYMPTOM: any COBOL sign condition aborts the whole run with KeyError: 'left'.
  IF N IS POSITIVE ... / IS NEGATIVE / IS ZERO  (also the abbreviated 'IF N POSITIVE')
all raise at interpreter/cobol/condition_lowering.py:586 in _lower_relation_node (rel['left']).

REPRO:
  01 N PIC S9(3). 01 RESULT PIC 9(1).
  MOVE 5 TO N.
  IF N IS POSITIVE MOVE 1 TO RESULT ELSE MOVE 9 TO RESULT.
  STOP RUN.
-> KeyError: 'left'.

ROOT CAUSE: the ProLeap bridge serializes a sign condition as a TEXT-only relation node:
  {'relation': {'text': 'NISPOSITIVE'}}
_lower_condition_node (condition_lowering.py ~L295) sees the 'relation' key and calls
_lower_relation_node, which unconditionally reads rel['left'] / rel['right'] (L584-585).
A sign-condition relation has neither -> KeyError 'left'.

Two layers of breakage:
1. Routing: _lower_relation_node assumes structured {left, op, right}; a text-only relation
   (sign condition) must instead be routed to a sign-condition handler (or _lower_condition_str).
2. Serialization: the 'text' is space-stripped to 'NISPOSITIVE' (operand + 'IS' + sign keyword
   concatenated, spaces removed), so even a text fallback cannot recover the operand name 'N' or
   the sign. The bridge should emit a structured sign condition, e.g.
   {'sign': 'POSITIVE'|'NEGATIVE'|'ZERO', 'operand': <expr>, 'not': bool}.

IMPACT: crashes (hard abort, not wrong-result) any program using a sign condition — a common
COBOL construct; contributes to NIST-85 failures (red-dragon-m0oa). Sign test is also a documented
COBOL class/sign condition category.

FIX DIRECTION:
- Bridge: serialize sign conditions structurally (operand expr + sign + not), like class conditions.
- condition_lowering: add a sign-condition branch (compare decoded operand value >0 / <0 / =0);
  and make _lower_relation_node defensive only insofar as routing a text-only relation to the
  text path rather than KeyError (but the real fix is structured serialization).
- Add regression tests: IF N IS POSITIVE/NEGATIVE/ZERO and NOT POSITIVE select the correct branch
  for positive/negative/zero N (currently the program cannot even run).

Related: red-dragon-9cxh (audit that surfaced it), red-dragon-m0oa (NIST).

comments.author: avishek-sen-gupta
comments.text:
FIXED + merged to main (commit 5540aab0, real hooks incl. full suite + CardDemo CICS green).

Bridge (StatementSerializer.java): added structured sign-condition serialization in the RELATION_CONDITION dispatch — serializeSignCondition emits {'not': bool, 'sign': 'POSITIVE'|'NEGATIVE'|'ZERO', 'operand': <expr>} (mirrors serializeClassCondition; effective-not = ccNot XOR signNot). JAR rebuilt.
Python (condition_lowering.py): _lower_condition_node gains a 'sign' branch -> _lower_sign_condition decodes the operand and compares to 0 (>0 / <0 / ==0); enclosing 'not' applied by the wrapper.
Tests: tests/integration/test_cobol_sign_condition.py — 10 cases (POSITIVE/NEGATIVE/ZERO x +/-/0 operands x NOT), all green; signed S9 decode verified (-5 IS NEGATIVE true). 286 COBOL tests green, no regressions.

The earlier KeyError 'left' crash on any sign condition is gone.

title: Migrate php frontend to typed Const
description:
## PHP Frontend Typed-Const Migration Analysis

Branch: `typed-const-literals`. The `Const` dataclass requires a keyword-only `type_expr: TypeExpr` and typed factory classmethods: `Const.int_`, `Const.float_`, `Const.string`, `Const.bool_`, `Const.null_`, `Const.func_ref`, `Const.class_ref`. Currently 136 PHP unit tests fail, 26 pass — all failures are `TypeError: Const.__init__() missing 1 required keyword-only argument: 'type_expr'`.

---

### 1. Every `Const(...)` Site (19 direct sites in PHP-specific files)

#### `interpreter/frontends/php/expressions.py`

| Line | Method | value | Intended Type | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|---------------|-------|
| 95 | `_lower_interpolated_child` | `ctx.node_text(child)` | `Const.string` | Fragment of double-quoted string; already unquoted STRING_CONTENT |
| 109 | `_lower_interpolated_string_parts` | `""` | `Const.string` | Empty string when interpolated string has no parts |
| 309 | `lower_php_store_target` | `i` (Python int) | `Const.int_` | Index into list-literal destructuring |
| 376 | `lower_php_throw_expr` | `ctx.constants.none_literal` | `Const.null_` | Synthetic placeholder after throw in expression context |
| 547 | `_lower_php_associative_array` | `"0"` | `Const.int_` | Fallback index when assoc-array element has no explicit key |
| 560 | `_lower_php_indexed_array` | `str(len(elements))` | `Const.int_` | Array size |
| 572 | `_lower_php_indexed_array` | `str(i)` | `Const.int_` | Loop index into indexed array |
| 683 | `lower_php_arrow_function` | `ctx.constants.default_return_value` | `Const.null_` | Fall-through return after arrow function body |
| 749 | `lower_php_anonymous_function` | `ctx.constants.default_return_value` | `Const.null_` | Fall-through return after anonymous function body |

#### `interpreter/frontends/php/control_flow.py`

| Line | Method | value | Intended Type | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|---------------|-------|
| 57 | `lower_php_return` | `ctx.constants.default_return_value` | `Const.null_` | Bare `return;` with no value |
| 182 | `lower_php_foreach` | `"0"` | `Const.int_` | Initializes loop index to 0 |
| 224 | `lower_php_foreach` | `"1"` | `Const.int_` | Increment step in foreach update |

#### `interpreter/frontends/php/declarations.py`

| Line | Method | value | Intended Type | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|---------------|-------|
| 157 | `lower_php_func_def` | `ctx.constants.default_return_value` | `Const.null_` | Fall-through return after function body |
| 195 | `lower_php_method_decl` | `ctx.constants.default_return_value` | `Const.null_` | Fall-through return after method body |
| 265 | `_emit_php_synthetic_constructor` | `ctx.constants.default_return_value` | `Const.null_` | Fall-through return in synthetic constructor |
| 407 | `lower_php_property_declaration` | `ctx.constants.none_literal` | `Const.null_` | Default value for property with no initializer |
| 466 | `_lower_php_constructor_with_field_inits` | `ctx.constants.default_return_value` | `Const.null_` | Fall-through return in explicit constructor |
| 537 | `lower_php_function_static` | `ctx.constants.none_literal` | `Const.null_` | `static $x;` without initializer |
| 587 | `lower_php_enum_case` | `case_name` (string) | `Const.string` | Unvalued enum case: name used as string value |

---

### 2. Shared Common Emitters (also broken, used by PHP dispatch)

In `interpreter/frontends/common/expressions.py`:
- Line 42–53 `lower_const_literal`: polymorphic — used for `INTEGER`, `FLOAT`, `STRING`, `NOWDOC` nodes via PHP dispatch table
- Line 56–68 `lower_canonical_none`: `Const(value=ctx.constants.none_literal)` → `Const.null_`
- Line 71–82 `lower_canonical_true`: → `Const.bool_(value=True)`
- Line 85–96 `lower_canonical_false`: → `Const.bool_(value=False)`
- Line 470 `lower_update_expr`: `Const(value="1")` → `Const.int_(value=1)`

In `interpreter/frontends/context.py`:
- `emit_func_ref` (line 250): `Const(value=str(func_label))` → `Const.func_ref(...)`
- `emit_class_ref` (line 273): `Const(value=str(class_label))` → `Const.class_ref(...)`
These are shared across ALL frontends; fixing them fixes PHP as a side-effect.

---

### 3. Literal Dispatch in PHP Frontend (`frontend.py` lines 61–108)

| Node Type | Lowerer | Kind | Shared vs Direct |
|-----------|---------|------|-----------------|
| `integer` | `common_expr.lower_const_literal` | int (decimal/hex/octal/binary/underscore) | Shared (broken) |
| `float` | `common_expr.lower_const_literal` | float | Shared (broken) |
| `string` | `common_expr.lower_const_literal` | single-quoted string | Shared (broken) |
| `encapsed_string` | `php_expr.lower_php_encapsed_string` | double-quoted, with/without interpolation | Direct PHP |
| `boolean` | `common_expr.lower_canonical_bool` | true/false case-insensitive | Shared (broken) |
| `null` | `common_expr.lower_canonical_none` | null case-insensitive | Shared (broken) |
| `heredoc` | `php_expr.lower_php_heredoc` | interpolated heredoc | Direct PHP |
| `nowdoc` | `common_expr.lower_const_literal` | non-interpolated nowdoc | Shared (broken) |

---

### 4. String Handling

**Single-quoted strings (`string` node):** tree-sitter text includes surrounding quotes: `'hello'` → raw text `'hello'`. Must strip outer `'..'` AND unescape `\\'→'`, `\\\\→\\`. No other escapes.

**Double-quoted strings (`encapsed_string` node):**
- No interpolation: falls back to `lower_const_literal(ctx, node)` — same quoting problem
- With interpolation: decomposes into `string_content` and `variable_name` children; STRING_CONTENT nodes (line 95) are already raw unquoted fragments — no stripping needed
- Full escapes: `\\n`, `\\r`, `\\t`, `\\$`, `\\e`, `\\f`, `\\v`, `\\0`, `\\x{HH}`, `\\u{HHHH}`
- ESCAPE_SEQUENCE nodes are filtered out of interpolation concatenation via `_NON_INTERPOLATION_TYPES` — silently dropped (pre-existing gap)

**Heredoc (`heredoc` node):**
- Non-interpolated fallback: `lower_const_literal(ctx, node)` — raw text includes `<<<EOT\n...\nEOT` markers (pre-existing gap)
- With interpolation: same fragment logic as `encapsed_string`

**Nowdoc (`nowdoc` node):** `lower_const_literal` — raw text includes `<<<'EOT'\n...\nEOT'` markers (pre-existing gap)

---

### 5. func/class refs

`emit_func_ref` and `emit_class_ref` in `interpreter/frontends/context.py` are shared. Changing them fixes PHP plus all other frontends. `Const.class_ref` needs a `class_type` argument (e.g. `scalar(TypeName(class_name))`) — requires importing `scalar` and `TypeName` into `context.py`.

---

### 6. Numeric Nuances

`INTEGER` node covers: decimal `42`, hex `0x1A`, octal `0777`, binary `0b1010`, underscore-separated `1_000_000`, `0x1A_2B`. Parse with `int(text.replace("_", ""), 0)` or `int(text, 0)` (Python 3.6+ handles underscores). `FLOAT` covers `1.5`, `1.5e3`, `1_500.0` — parse with `float(text.replace("_", ""))`.

---

### 7. Exact Ordered Change List

**Step 1 — `interpreter/frontends/context.py` (shared):**
- `emit_func_ref` line 250: → `Const.func_ref(...)`
- `emit_class_ref` line 273: → `Const.class_ref(...)`

**Step 2 — `interpreter/frontends/common/expressions.py` (shared):**
- `lower_canonical_none` line 62: → `Const.null_`
- `lower_canonical_true` line 76: → `Const.bool_(value=True)`
- `lower_canonical_false` line 90: → `Const.bool_(value=False)`
- `lower_update_expr` line 470: → `Const.int_(value=1)`
- `lower_const_literal`: replace PHP dispatch entries with typed PHP-specific lowerers (see Step 3); this function remains for non-PHP frontends but PHP must no longer call it

**Step 3 — `interpreter/frontends/php/frontend.py` (dispatch table):**
- Replace `PHPNodeType.INTEGER → common_expr.lower_const_literal` with new `lower_php_integer`: `Const.int_(value=int(text.replace("_",""), 0))`
- Replace `PHPNodeType.FLOAT → common_expr.lower_const_literal` with new `lower_php_float`: `Const.float_(value=float(text.replace("_","")))`
- Replace `PHPNodeType.STRING → common_expr.lower_const_literal` with new `lower_php_single_quoted_string`: strip `'..'` and unescape, then `Const.string`
- Replace `PHPNodeType.NOWDOC → common_expr.lower_const_literal` with new `lower_php_nowdoc`: strip markers, then `Const.string`

**Step 4 — `interpreter/frontends/php/expressions.py` (9 sites):**
- Line 95: → `Const.string` (STRING_CONTENT already unquoted)
- Line 109: → `Const.string(value="")`
- Line 309: → `Const.int_(value=i)` (i already Python int)
- Line 376: → `Const.null_`
- Line 547: → `Const.int_(value=0)`
- Line 560: → `Const.int_(value=len(elements))`
- Line 572: → `Const.int_(value=i)`
- Line 683: → `Const.null_`
- Line 749: → `Const.null_`

**Step 5 — `interpreter/frontends/php/control_flow.py` (3 sites):**
- Line 57: → `Const.null_`
- Line 182: → `Const.int_(value=0)`
- Line 224: → `Const.int_(value=1)`

**Step 6 — `interpreter/frontends/php/declarations.py` (7 sites):**
- Lines 157, 195, 265, 466: → `Const.null_` (fall-through returns)
- Lines 407, 537: → `Const.null_` (no-initializer defaults)
- Line 587: → `Const.string(value=case_name)` (enum case name)

---

### 8. Test Command + PHP Integration Files

```bash
poetry run python -m pytest tests/unit/test_php_frontend.py tests/integration/test_php_frontend_execution.py tests/integration/test_php_print_clone_const_execution.py -q --tb=short
```

PHP fixture files require `<?php` tag — 18 exercism exercises under `tests/unit/exercism/exercises/*/solutions/php.php`.

Current state: **136 failed, 26 passed, 1 xfailed** (all failures: `TypeError: missing type_expr`).

---

### 9. Risks

**Risk 1 (HIGH): Single vs double quote stripping.** `lower_const_literal` passes raw node text including quotes to `Const(value=...)`. New typed lowerers must strip `'..'` and apply single-quote-only escape rules (`\\'→'`, `\\\\→\\`); double-quote lowerers must strip `".."` and apply the full PHP double-quote escape set. Wrong stripping produces corrupt string values.

**Risk 2 (HIGH): Interpolation fragment handling.** STRING_CONTENT nodes in interpolated strings are already unquoted raw text — do NOT strip them again. ESCAPE_SEQUENCE nodes are silently dropped by the current `_NON_INTERPOLATION_TYPES` filter; this is a pre-existing gap that the migration must not worsen.

**Risk 3 (MEDIUM): Heredoc/nowdoc raw text includes delimiters.** Both `nowdoc` and non-interpolated `heredoc` fall back to `lower_const_literal` which passes the entire node text including `<<<'EOT'\n...\nEOT` markers. New typed lowerers must extract just the body text via `heredoc_body` child node or string parsing.

**Risk 4 (MEDIUM): `emit_func_ref`/`emit_class_ref` are cross-frontend shared.** Changing `context.py` fixes PHP but simultaneously affects all 15 other frontends. `Const.class_ref` needs a `class_type` TypeExpr argument — verify `scalar`/`TypeName` are importable in `context.py` without circular imports.

**Risk 5 (LOW): Case-insensitive true/false/null.** Already handled by `lower_canonical_bool` (uses `.strip().lower()`) and `lower_canonical_none`. Migration preserves this behavior.

**Risk 6 (LOW): Integer base prefixes and underscores.** `int(text, 0)` in Python 3.6+ handles all forms including underscores. No separate handling needed.
close_reason: PHP migrated (ec7756ef): single-quote (\' \\ only) vs double-quote (full escapes+interpolation) handling, nowdoc/heredoc, interpolation fragments verbatim, case-insensitive true/false/null. Frontend tests green (166).

title: Migrate pascal frontend to typed Const
description:
## Pascal Frontend Typed-Const Migration Analysis

### Branch context
Branch: `typed-const-literals`. The `Const` dataclass now has `type_expr: TypeExpr` as a mandatory keyword-only argument, breaking all legacy `Const(result_reg=..., value=...)` calls. Current baseline: **108 failed, 10 passed** in Pascal unit tests.

---

### All `Const(...)` Call Sites (19 total across 3 files)

#### `interpreter/frontends/pascal/expressions.py`
| Line | Method | Literal kind | Factory |
|------|--------|-------------|---------|
| 227 | `lower_pascal_brackets` | Integer — set element count | `Const.int_(result_reg=size_reg, value=len(elems))` |
| 237 | `lower_pascal_brackets` | Integer — 0-based element index | `Const.int_(result_reg=idx_reg, value=i)` |

#### `interpreter/frontends/pascal/control_flow.py`
| Line | Method | Literal kind | Factory |
|------|--------|-------------|---------|
| 238 | `lower_pascal_for` | Integer — step constant 1 | `Const.int_(result_reg=one_reg, value=1)` |
| 520 | `lower_pascal_raise` | Null sentinel | `Const.null_(result_reg=val_reg)` |
| 565 | `lower_pascal_foreach` | Integer — initial index 0 | `Const.int_(result_reg=zero_reg, value=0)` |
| 606 | `lower_pascal_foreach` | Integer — step constant 1 | `Const.int_(result_reg=one_reg, value=1)` |

#### `interpreter/frontends/pascal/declarations.py`
| Line | Method | Literal kind | Factory |
|------|--------|-------------|---------|
| 161 | `lower_pascal_decl_var` | Integer — array size | `Const.int_(result_reg=size_reg, value=array_size)` |
| 191 | `lower_pascal_decl_var` | Null sentinel | `Const.null_(result_reg=val_reg)` |
| 210 | `_populate_array_with_records` | Integer — 0-based slot index | `Const.int_(result_reg=idx_reg, value=i)` |
| 348 | `lower_pascal_proc` | Null sentinel | `Const.null_(result_reg=none_reg)` |
| 462 | `lower_pascal_decl_const` | Null sentinel — fallback inner is None | `Const.null_(result_reg=val_reg)` |
| 465 | `lower_pascal_decl_const` | Null sentinel — no value_node | `Const.null_(result_reg=val_reg)` |
| 573 | `_emit_synthetic_init_for_fields` | Null sentinel — field init | `Const.null_(result_reg=val_reg)` |
| 581 | `_emit_synthetic_init_for_fields` | Null sentinel — synthetic return | `Const.null_(result_reg=none_reg)` |
| 640 | `_lower_pascal_method` | Null sentinel — forward-decl stub | `Const.null_(result_reg=none_reg)` |
| 793 | `_emit_property_setter` | Null sentinel — setter return | `Const.null_(result_reg=none_reg)` |
| 826 | `_lower_pascal_enum` | String — enum member name key | `Const.string(result_reg=key_reg, value=member_name)` |
| 828 | `_lower_pascal_enum` | Integer — enum ordinal | `Const.int_(result_reg=val_reg, value=i)` |
| 832 | `_lower_pascal_enum` | Integer — enum ordinal again | `Const.int_(result_reg=ord_reg, value=i)` |

---

### Literal Dispatch

Pascal node types → literal kind:

| Pascal node type | Dispatched to | Kind |
|---|---|---|
| `literalNumber` | `lower_const_literal` (common) | int or float (text-parsed) |
| `literalString` | `lower_const_literal` (common) | string (raw Pascal-quoted text) |
| `kTrue` | `lower_canonical_true` (common) | bool True |
| `kFalse` | `lower_canonical_false` (common) | bool False |
| `kNil` | `lower_canonical_none` (common) | null |

---

### String Handling

Pascal single-quoted strings: `'hello'`. The shared `lower_const_literal` in `common/expressions.py` currently passes raw node text verbatim (including surrounding single quotes).

- **Quote escape**: `''` inside a string = literal single quote (`'it''s'` → `it's`). Current code does NOT unescape.
- **Char codes**: `#65` = `chr(65)` = `'A'`. May appear as distinct expressions or concatenated with `+`.
- **Char literals**: `'A'` is a single-char string — Pascal distinguishes Char from String by declaration, not literal syntax.

After migration, if `Const.string` holds a real Python `str`, `lower_const_literal` must strip outer quotes and unescape `''` → `'` and `#NN` → `chr(NN)`.

---

### Numeric Nuances

- **Hex**: `$FF` (Pascal uses `$` prefix). Naive `int(text)` raises `ValueError`. Must detect `text.startswith('$')` → `int(text[1:], 16)`.
- **Float**: text containing `.` or `e`/`E` → `Const.float_(value=float(text))`.
- **Integer**: decimal digits only → `Const.int_(value=int(text))`.

---

### func_ref / class_ref

The Pascal frontend uses shared helpers `ctx.emit_func_ref(...)` and `ctx.emit_class_ref(...)` defined in `interpreter/frontends/context.py`. These emit bare `Const(...)` at lines 250 and 273 respectively. Migration of those helpers affects all frontends (shared infrastructure, not Pascal-specific).

---

### Ordered Change List

1. **`interpreter/frontends/common/expressions.py`** — Migrate 7 `Const(...)` calls:
   - `lower_const_literal` (line 47): detect numeric/string → typed factory + Pascal literal parsing
   - `lower_canonical_none` (line 62): → `Const.null_(...)`
   - `lower_canonical_true` (line 76): → `Const.bool_(..., value=True)`
   - `lower_canonical_false` (line 90): → `Const.bool_(..., value=False)`
   - Array index helpers (lines 470, 500, 512): → `Const.int_(...)`
2. **`interpreter/frontends/context.py`** — Migrate `emit_func_ref` (line 250) and `emit_class_ref` (line 273) to `Const.func_ref` / `Const.class_ref`.
3. **`interpreter/frontends/pascal/declarations.py`** — Migrate 13 call sites (9× null, 3× int, 1× string).
4. **`interpreter/frontends/pascal/control_flow.py`** — Migrate 4 call sites (3× int, 1× null).
5. **`interpreter/frontends/pascal/expressions.py`** — Migrate 2 call sites (2× int).

---

### Risks

**P0 — `lower_const_literal` (shared common)**: Handles ALL Pascal number and string literals. Currently stores raw node text verbatim. Migration must correctly parse Pascal's `$FF` hex integers, `3.14` floats, and `'`-delimited strings. Wrong parsing silently produces incorrect values rather than errors.

**P0 — Pascal string unescaping (`''` → `'`) and char codes (`#65`)**: If `Const.string` holds a Python `str` value (not raw Pascal text), the migration must strip outer quotes and unescape both `''` and `#NN`/`#$NN` sequences. Any string containing an apostrophe or char code will be wrong if omitted.

**P1 — Hex integer literals (`$FF`)**: Pascal uses `$` prefix. Must be explicitly handled in `lower_const_literal` — naive `int(text)` raises `ValueError`.

**P1 — `emit_class_ref` in `context.py`**: `Const.class_ref` requires a `class_type: TypeExpr` argument. The context helper does not currently compute one; a suitable `TypeExpr` must be chosen.

**P1 — nil/null downstream consumers**: Any code matching on the string `"None"` as nil value (e.g., `vm.py`, `type_inference.py`) must be updated when `kNil` → `Const.null_()` with `value=None` payload.

---

### Test command

```bash
poetry run python -m pytest tests/unit/test_pascal_frontend.py tests/integration/test_pascal_frontend_execution.py tests/integration/test_pascal_p0_gaps_execution.py tests/integration/test_pascal_array_of_records.py -q
```

Current baseline before migration: **108 failed, 10 passed** in Pascal unit tests.
close_reason: Pascal migrated (57bce9d0): $FF hex ints, '' quote-escaping, #65/#$41 char codes->1-char string, concatenated char-code sequences. Frontend tests green (124). LAST frontend.

title: Migrate rust frontend to typed Const
description:
Prerequisite: red-dragon-gjoy.1 (shared typed helpers in _base.py / context.py / common) must land first.

## Summary

Total untyped Const( sites: **28 direct** across 3 files + **7 dispatch entries** in frontend.py that route through untyped lower_const_literal. All must be migrated.

---

## 1. Exhaustive Const( site catalogue

### expressions.py (17 direct sites)

| Line | Method | value= | Kind | Factory |
|------|--------|--------|------|---------|
| 369 | lower_expr_stmt_as_expr | ctx.constants.none_literal ("None") | null | Const.null_ |
| 381 | lower_else_clause | ctx.constants.none_literal | null | Const.null_ |
| 395 | lower_return_expr (empty return) | ctx.constants.default_return_value ("()") | null/unit | Const.null_ |
| 485 | lower_block_expr (empty block) | ctx.constants.none_literal | null | Const.null_ |
| 515 | lower_closure_expr (no body) | ctx.constants.default_return_value | null/unit | Const.null_ |
| 620 | _lower_vec_macro | str(len(elems)) | int | Const.int_ |
| 630 | _lower_vec_macro | str(i) | int | Const.int_ |
| 705 | _make_rust_const | value (str, varies) | varies | see note below |
| 721 | lower_tuple_expr | str(len(elems)) | int | Const.int_ |
| 731 | lower_tuple_expr | str(i) | int | Const.int_ |
| 845 | lower_loop_as_expr | ctx.constants.none_literal | null | Const.null_ |
| 857 | lower_continue_as_expr | ctx.constants.none_literal | null | Const.null_ |
| 869 | lower_break_as_expr | ctx.constants.none_literal | null | Const.null_ |
| 927 | lower_tuple_struct_pattern | variant_name (str) | string | Const.string |
| 943 | lower_tuple_struct_pattern | str(i) | int | Const.int_ |
| 1008 | lower_struct_pattern_expr | field_name (str) | string | Const.string |
| 1010 | lower_struct_pattern_expr | field_name (str) | string | Const.string |

**Note on _make_rust_const (line 705):** Called with either str(int-like) or ctx.constants.none_literal. Callers:
- L620/630/721/731/943: str(int) → already handled above
- L667: none_literal (range start default) → Const.null_
- L684: none_literal (range step/None) → Const.null_
- L705 itself needs to become two helpers: _make_rust_const_int(ctx, n: int) and _make_rust_const_null(ctx), or inline all call sites.

### declarations.py (9 direct sites)

| Line | Method | value= | Kind | Factory |
|------|--------|--------|------|---------|
| 191 | lower_function_def (body no implicit return) | ctx.constants.default_return_value ("()") | null/unit | Const.null_ |
| 197 | lower_function_def (no body) | ctx.constants.default_return_value | null/unit | Const.null_ |
| 223 | lower_let_decl (no initializer) | ctx.constants.none_literal | null | Const.null_ |
| 249 | _lower_tuple_destructure | str(i) | int | Const.int_ |
| 396 | lower_enum_item | variant_name (str) | string | Const.string |
| 425 | lower_const_item (no value) | ctx.constants.none_literal | null | Const.null_ |
| 447 | lower_static_item (no value) | ctx.constants.none_literal | null | Const.null_ |
| 465 | lower_type_item | type_text (str, e.g. "i32") | string | Const.string |
| 520 | lower_function_signature | ctx.constants.default_return_value | null/unit | Const.null_ |

### control_flow.py (2 direct sites)

| Line | Method | value= | Kind | Factory |
|------|--------|--------|------|---------|
| 117 | lower_for | "0" | int | Const.int_ |
| 158 | lower_for | "1" | int | Const.int_ |

---

## 2. Dispatch table (frontend.py) — 7 literal node types routed through lower_const_literal

These are not direct Const( sites but become typed once gjoy.1 replaces lower_const_literal with typed per-kind helpers. Each must be re-pointed to a typed helper:

| Node type | Line | Raw text | Kind | New helper |
|-----------|------|----------|------|------------|
| INTEGER_LITERAL | 86 | "42", "0xFF", "100u64", "1_000" | int | lower_rust_integer_literal |
| FLOAT_LITERAL | 87 | "3.14", "1.0f32", "2.5e-3" | float | lower_rust_float_literal |
| STRING_LITERAL | 88 | '"hello"' (with quotes) | string | lower_rust_string_literal |
| CHAR_LITERAL | 89 | "'x'", "'\n'" | int (ord) | lower_rust_char_literal |
| RAW_STRING_LITERAL | 90 | 'r#"raw"#' | string | lower_rust_raw_string_literal |
| NEGATIVE_LITERAL | 91 | "-42", "-1.5" | int/float | lower_rust_negative_literal |
| UNIT_EXPRESSION | 93 | "()" | null | lower_rust_unit_expr |

BOOLEAN_LITERAL (line 92) already uses lower_canonical_bool → will become Const.bool_ after gjoy.1. TRUE (94) / FALSE (95) use lower_canonical_true/false → Const.bool_ after gjoy.1. No action needed in Rust-specific code for these.

---

## 3. String handling

Raw text from tree-sitter for each literal kind:
- STRING_LITERAL: includes surrounding double-quotes `"hello world"`. Must strip first and last character. Escape processing: `\"`, `\\`, `\n`, `\t`, `\r`, `\0`, `\x41`, `\u{1F600}` — tree-sitter delivers raw text with escape sequences intact; the VM/runtime is responsible for escape interpretation, but the value stored in Const.string should be the unquoted content with escape sequences preserved (consistent with other frontends).
- RAW_STRING_LITERAL: `r#"content"#`, `r##"content"##` — strip `r`, strip leading `#`s + `"`, strip trailing `"` + `#`s. The content is verbatim (no escape sequences). Nesting level = count `#` chars.
- CHAR_LITERAL: `'x'`, `'\n'`, `'\u{263A}'`. For Rust chars → Const.int_(reg, ord(char_value)) to match VM's char-as-int convention. Strip surrounding quotes, decode escape if needed.
- BYTE_LITERAL: b'x' → Const.int_ (0–255). Not currently in dispatch table; if encountered (tree-sitter may surface as char_literal or separate node), treat as int.
- BYTE_STRING_LITERAL: b"..." → treat as Const.string (raw bytes as str). Not currently dispatched; add if needed.

---

## 4. func_ref / class_ref

Not done inside rust/expressions.py or declarations.py directly. They are called via:
- `ctx.emit_func_ref(...)` — defined in context.py/base.py, will be migrated in gjoy.1 to use Const.func_ref
- `ctx.emit_class_ref(...)` — same, Const.class_ref

No changes needed in rust-specific files for func_ref/class_ref; they inherit from gjoy.1.

---

## 5. Numeric nuances

### Integer literals (INTEGER_LITERAL)
Rust allows:
- Decimal: `42`, `1_000_000`
- Hex: `0xFF`, `0x1A_2B`
- Octal: `0o77`
- Binary: `0b1010_1010`
- Suffixed: `42u64`, `1_000i32`, `0xFFusize`

Parsing: strip suffix (trailing `[uiUsU][0-9]*` or `size`), strip underscores in the numeric body, then `int(text, 0)` to handle 0x/0o/0b prefixes. Result → `Const.int_(reg, parsed_int)`.

### Float literals (FLOAT_LITERAL)
Rust allows:
- `3.14`, `1.0`, `2.5e-3`, `1.0E6`
- Suffixed: `1.0f32`, `2.5f64`

Parsing: strip `f32`/`f64` suffix, strip underscores, then `float(text)`. Result → `Const.float_(reg, parsed_float)`.

### NEGATIVE_LITERAL
Tree-sitter wraps negative literals as a separate node type containing `-` + a numeric literal child. Must recurse: parse the child as int or float depending on its type, negate, emit Const.int_ or Const.float_.

### Char literals
`'x'` → ord('x'). Escape sequences: `'\n'` → 10, `'\t'` → 9, etc. Unicode: `'\u{263A}'` → parse hex. Result → `Const.int_(reg, ord_value)`.

---

## 6. Ordered change list

1. **Prerequisite check**: Confirm gjoy.1 has landed (lower_rust_integer_literal etc. exist in common/expressions.py or _base.py; Const.null_/int_/float_/string/bool_ are available).

2. **Write FAILING tests first (TDD)**:
   - Unit: integers with suffixes (u64, i32, usize), hex, octal, binary, underscores
   - Unit: float with f32/f64 suffix
   - Unit: string literal (with quotes stripped), raw string (r#"..."# unwrapped)
   - Unit: char literal → int (ord)
   - Unit: negative integer/float
   - Unit: unit expression () → null
   - Integration: run VM on snippet with each literal kind, assert result

3. **frontend.py dispatch table** — replace 7 entries:
   - INTEGER_LITERAL → lower_rust_integer_literal (new, in rust/expressions.py)
   - FLOAT_LITERAL → lower_rust_float_literal (new)
   - STRING_LITERAL → lower_rust_string_literal (new)
   - CHAR_LITERAL → lower_rust_char_literal (new)
   - RAW_STRING_LITERAL → lower_rust_raw_string_literal (new)
   - NEGATIVE_LITERAL → lower_rust_negative_literal (new)
   - UNIT_EXPRESSION → lower_rust_unit_expr (new)

4. **rust/expressions.py** — new typed literal helpers (step 3 above), then:
   - All `Const(result_reg=reg, value=ctx.constants.none_literal)` → `Const.null_(reg)` (7 sites: 369, 381, 485, 845, 857, 869, + _make_rust_const call sites)
   - All `Const(result_reg=val_reg, value=ctx.constants.default_return_value)` → `Const.null_(val_reg)` (2 sites: 395, 515)
   - `Const(result_reg=size_reg, value=str(len(elems)))` → `Const.int_(size_reg, len(elems))` (2 sites: 620, 721)
   - `Const(result_reg=idx_reg, value=str(i))` → `Const.int_(idx_reg, i)` (2 sites: 630, 731, 943)
   - `Const(result_reg=variant_reg, value=variant_name)` → `Const.string(variant_reg, variant_name)` (line 927)
   - `Const(result_reg=key_reg/val_reg, value=field_name)` → `Const.string(key_reg/val_reg, field_name)` (lines 1008, 1010)
   - **_make_rust_const**: split into _make_rust_const_int(ctx, n: int) → Const.int_ and _make_rust_const_null(ctx) → Const.null_; update all 4 call sites

5. **rust/declarations.py** — migrate 9 sites:
   - Lines 191, 197, 520: default_return_value → Const.null_
   - Lines 223, 425, 447: none_literal → Const.null_
   - Line 249: str(i) → Const.int_(idx_reg, i)
   - Line 396: variant_name → Const.string(variant_reg, variant_name)
   - Line 465: type_text → Const.string(val_reg, type_text)

6. **rust/control_flow.py** — migrate 2 sites:
   - Line 117: "0" → Const.int_(init_idx, 0)
   - Line 158: "1" → Const.int_(one_reg, 1)

7. **Run tests**: `poetry run python -m pytest tests/ -k rust -q` — all should be green (56 passing pre-migration; errors are pre-existing infrastructure issues unrelated to Const typing).

---

## 7. Test command and integration files

```
poetry run python -m pytest tests/ -k rust -q
```

Pre-migration baseline (on typed-const-literals branch): 56 passed, 236 failed, 671 errors (errors are pre-existing, unrelated to Const typing — likely missing bridge JAR or similar infra).

Integration test files:
- tests/integration/test_rust_frontend_execution.py
- tests/integration/test_rust_unit_expression_execution.py
- tests/integration/test_rust_box_deref.py
- tests/integration/test_rust_pattern_matching.py
- tests/integration/test_rust_or_pattern_execution.py
- tests/integration/test_rust_string_concat_execution.py

Unit test files:
- tests/unit/test_rust_frontend.py
- tests/unit/test_rust_prelude.py
- tests/unit/test_rust_patterns.py
- tests/unit/test_rust_box_option_lowering.py

---

## 8. Risks and ambiguities

### Risk 1 (HIGH): RAW STRING LITERAL stripping logic
`r#"content"#`, `r##"..."##` — the number of `#` delimiters must be counted and matched. The raw text from tree-sitter is the full literal text. Nesting level can be 0–N. Algorithm: count leading `#`s after `r`, strip `r"..."`, `r#"..."#`, `r##"..."##` etc. Edge case: content containing `"#` sequences that are not the closing delimiter. Must count the `#` run at the end and match against opening count.

### Risk 2 (HIGH): NEGATIVE_LITERAL node structure
Tree-sitter Rust grammar represents `-42` as a `negative_literal` node. But the internal structure matters: does tree-sitter deliver `-42` as a single text node, or as a unary `-` + an `integer_literal` child? If the latter, the `lower_rust_negative_literal` must recurse into the child to determine int vs float. If the former, simply parse the text with `-` prefix. Needs verification by printing node structure in a test before implementing.

### Risk 3 (MEDIUM): CHAR_LITERAL → int convention
Converting `'x'` to `ord('x')` (int) means char comparisons and arithmetic work naturally. But if any existing VM builtins or test fixtures expect a string value for chars, this will break. Audit tests that use char-returning Rust code before committing to int convention.

### Risk 4 (LOW): _make_rust_const caller audit
The helper at expressions.py:702 takes a raw string. All 4 call sites must be audited to determine correct typed replacement (int vs null vs string). Done above in section 1, but validate against actual source before editing.
close_reason: Rust migrated (2e30ad70): raw strings via string_content child, negative literals via unary_expression, char->ord w/ escapes, int/float suffixes. Frontend tests green (243).

title: Migrate ruby frontend to typed Const
description:
## Summary

Migrate `interpreter/frontends/ruby/` from untyped `Const(result_reg=..., value=...)` calls to the typed factory classmethods (`Const.int_`, `Const.float_`, `Const.string`, `Const.bool_`, `Const.null_`, `Const.func_ref`, `Const.class_ref`).

This task is a child of red-dragon-gjoy.1 (shared keystone). The shared `lower_const_literal` and friends in `common/expressions.py` must be migrated under red-dragon-gjoy.1 first, as ruby delegates to them for INTEGER, FLOAT, SIMPLE_SYMBOL, HASH_KEY_SYMBOL, REGEX, HEREDOC_BEGINNING, DELIMITED_SYMBOL, IN, TRUE, FALSE, NIL nodes.

---

## Exhaustive Const( site inventory (16 direct sites)

### expressions.py

| Line | Method | Intended type | Notes |
|------|--------|---------------|-------|
| 104 | `lower_ruby_string` (interpolation, STRING_CONTENT fragment) | `Const.string` | Raw text of a string content fragment — strip surrounding quotes not needed (content node has no quotes) |
| 128 | `lower_ruby_heredoc_body` (HEREDOC_CONTENT fragment) | `Const.string` | Same as above — raw heredoc text chunk |
| 235 | `lower_ruby_argument_list` (empty arg list fallback) | `Const.null_` | `ctx.constants.default_return_value` == `"None"` → nil |
| 335 | `lower_ruby_lambda` (implicit nil return) | `Const.null_` | `ctx.constants.default_return_value` → nil |
| 362 | `lower_ruby_word_array` (array size) | `Const.int_` | `str(len(elems))` — integer array size |
| 371 | `lower_ruby_word_array` (element value) | `Const.string` | `ctx.node_text(elem)` for each `%w[]` / `%i[]` element — string or symbol text |
| 373 | `lower_ruby_word_array` (element index) | `Const.int_` | `str(i)` — integer index |
| 488 | `_make_const` helper | mixed | Called with `"0"`, `"1"`, `ctx.constants.none_literal` — three distinct types (int, int, null); must be split into typed variants or caller must pass typed value |
| 633 | `lower_ruby_block` (implicit nil return) | `Const.null_` | `ctx.constants.default_return_value` |
| 639 | `lower_ruby_block` (func ref) | `Const.func_ref` | `f"func:{block_label}"` — already routed through `emit_func_ref` in lambda but here is a raw direct emit; should use `ctx.emit_func_ref(...)` or `Const.func_ref(...)` |

### control_flow.py

| Line | Method | Intended type | Notes |
|------|--------|---------------|-------|
| 140 | `lower_ruby_for` (init loop index) | `Const.int_` | `"0"` — integer |
| 178 | `lower_ruby_for` (increment by 1) | `Const.int_` | `"1"` — integer |

### assignments.py

| Line | Method | Intended type | Notes |
|------|--------|---------------|-------|
| 33 | `lower_ruby_return` (empty return) | `Const.null_` | `ctx.constants.default_return_value` |

### declarations.py

| Line | Method | Intended type | Notes |
|------|--------|---------------|-------|
| 100 | `lower_ruby_method` (implicit nil return) | `Const.null_` | `ctx.constants.default_return_value` |
| 215 | `lower_ruby_singleton_method` (implicit nil return) | `Const.null_` | `ctx.constants.default_return_value` |

**Total: 15 direct Const( sites across 4 files.** (Plus the shared `lower_const_literal` / `lower_canonical_none/true/false` in `common/expressions.py` delegated to via dispatch — those are migrated under red-dragon-gjoy.1.)

---

## Literal dispatch: node type → kind → handler

| RubyNodeType | Kind | Handler |
|---|---|---|
| `INTEGER` | int | `lower_const_literal` (shared, via gjoy.1) → `Const.int_` after parse |
| `FLOAT` | float | `lower_const_literal` (shared) → `Const.float_` after parse |
| `STRING` | string / interpolated string | `lower_ruby_string` — delegates to `lower_const_literal` for simple strings, or emits fragments for interpolation |
| `TRUE` | bool | `lower_canonical_true` (shared) → `Const.bool_(True)` |
| `FALSE` | bool | `lower_canonical_false` (shared) → `Const.bool_(False)` |
| `NIL` | null | `lower_canonical_none` (shared) → `Const.null_()` |
| `SIMPLE_SYMBOL` | string (symbol as string) | `lower_const_literal` (shared) — value is e.g. `:foo`; treated as string |
| `HASH_KEY_SYMBOL` | string (symbol) | `lower_const_literal` (shared) |
| `DELIMITED_SYMBOL` | string | `lower_const_literal` (shared) |
| `HEREDOC_BODY` | string / interpolated string | `lower_ruby_heredoc_body` — same pattern as `lower_ruby_string` |
| `HEREDOC_BEGINNING` | string | `lower_const_literal` (shared) |
| `REGEX` | string | `lower_const_literal` (shared) |
| `STRING_ARRAY` / `SYMBOL_ARRAY` | array of strings | `lower_ruby_word_array` — emits `Const.int_` for size/index, `Const.string` for element |
| `IN` | string (keyword used as literal) | `lower_const_literal` (shared) |

---

## String handling specifics

- **Single-quoted strings** (`'foo'`): tree-sitter gives `string > string_content`. No interpolation child. `lower_ruby_string` calls `lower_const_literal` (shared). After gjoy.1 migration `lower_const_literal` will parse and emit `Const.string`. The text returned by `node_text(string_node)` is the whole `'...'` — the shared migrated emitter must strip outer quotes, OR the node dispatched is the `string_content` inner child (confirm in tree-sitter parse). Currently `lower_const_literal` receives the whole `string` node; `node_text` returns the raw text with quotes. The caller must strip delimiters — this needs verification.
- **Double-quoted strings** (`"foo"`): same dispatch; same stripping requirement.
- **Interpolated strings** (`"#{expr}"`): `lower_ruby_string` walks children: `string_content` nodes → `Const.string` fragments; `interpolation` nodes → `lower_expr`; concatenated via `lower_interpolated_string_parts`. Direct `Const(...)` at line 104 needs → `Const.string(frag_reg, ctx.node_text(child))`.
- **Heredocs** (`<<~HEREDOC`): `lower_ruby_heredoc_body` mirrors `lower_ruby_string`; `heredoc_content` fragments at line 128 → `Const.string`.
- **%-literals** (`%w[a b c]`, `%i[a b c]`): `lower_ruby_word_array`; each element at line 371 → `Const.string`; size (362) and index (373) → `Const.int_`.
- **Symbols** (`:foo`, `%i`, hash key symbols): treated as strings throughout. `lower_const_literal` will emit `Const.string`. The raw text from tree-sitter includes the colon for `simple_symbol` (`:foo`) and no colon for `hash_key_symbol` (`foo:`). There is no distinct symbol type in the IR — the mapping to string is intentional but must be documented.

---

## func/class refs

- `emit_func_ref` and `emit_class_ref` in `context.py` (lines 234–275) still call the bare `Const(result_reg=..., value=str(...))` — those must be migrated to `Const.func_ref(...)` / `Const.class_ref(...)` under gjoy.1 (context.py is shared).
- In `expressions.py` line 639: `Const(result_reg=ref_reg, value=f"func:{block_label}")` is a **direct** raw func-ref emit that bypasses `emit_func_ref`. After context.py migration the correct fix is `ctx.emit_func_ref(block_name, block_label, result_reg=ref_reg, node=node)` (same pattern as `lower_ruby_lambda` line 340). This is a ruby-specific fix.

---

## `_make_const` helper (expressions.py:485–488)

Called with three value types:
1. `"0"` → integer → `Const.int_(reg, 0)`
2. `"1"` → integer → `Const.int_(reg, 1)`
3. `ctx.constants.none_literal` (`"None"`) → null → `Const.null_(reg)`

Resolution: replace `_make_const(ctx, value)` with three typed helpers: `_make_int_const`, `_make_null_const`, or inline the typed calls at each call site.

---

## Ordered change list

1. **Prerequisite**: red-dragon-gjoy.1 must land first — it migrates `lower_const_literal`, `lower_canonical_none/true/false`, `emit_func_ref`, `emit_class_ref` (context.py), and `lower_interpolated_string_parts` in `common/expressions.py`.
2. **`expressions.py` line 104** (`lower_ruby_string`, string content fragment): `Const(...)` → `Const.string(frag_reg, ctx.node_text(child))`.
3. **`expressions.py` line 128** (`lower_ruby_heredoc_body`, heredoc content fragment): same → `Const.string`.
4. **`expressions.py` line 235** (`lower_ruby_argument_list`, empty fallback): `Const(...)` → `Const.null_(reg)`.
5. **`expressions.py` line 335** (`lower_ruby_lambda`, implicit nil): → `Const.null_(nil_reg)`.
6. **`expressions.py` line 362** (`lower_ruby_word_array`, size): → `Const.int_(size_reg, len(elems))`.
7. **`expressions.py` line 371** (`lower_ruby_word_array`, element string): → `Const.string(val_reg, ctx.node_text(elem))`.
8. **`expressions.py` line 373** (`lower_ruby_word_array`, index): → `Const.int_(idx_reg, i)`.
9. **`expressions.py` line 488** (`_make_const`): split into typed helpers; update all call sites (lines 412, 416, 421, 433, 467).
10. **`expressions.py` line 633** (`lower_ruby_block`, implicit nil): → `Const.null_(nil_reg)`.
11. **`expressions.py` line 639** (`lower_ruby_block`, func ref): replace raw `Const(result_reg=ref_reg, value=f"func:{block_label}")` with `ctx.emit_func_ref(<block_name>, block_label, result_reg=ref_reg, node=node)`.
12. **`control_flow.py` line 140** (`lower_ruby_for`, init index `"0"`): → `Const.int_(init_idx, 0)`.
13. **`control_flow.py` line 178** (`lower_ruby_for`, increment `"1"`): → `Const.int_(one_reg, 1)`.
14. **`assignments.py` line 33** (`lower_ruby_return`, empty return nil): → `Const.null_(val_reg)`.
15. **`declarations.py` line 100** (`lower_ruby_method`, implicit nil): → `Const.null_(none_reg)`.
16. **`declarations.py` line 215** (`lower_ruby_singleton_method`, implicit nil): → `Const.null_(none_reg)`.

---

## Test command

```
poetry run python -m pytest tests/ -k ruby -q
```

Current ruby test run: 54 passed, 197 failed, 674 errors (failures are pre-existing; this migration must not introduce new failures).

Integration test files: `tests/integration/` (ruby-tagged fixtures), `tests/unit/rosetta/`, `tests/unit/exercism/` (ruby-parametrized cases).

---

## Risks

### Risk 1 (HIGH): Symbol node text includes colon (`:foo`) — Const.string value must be canonical

`simple_symbol` node text from tree-sitter is `:foo` (with colon). `hash_key_symbol` text for `foo:` syntax has no colon. `lower_const_literal` (shared) passes the raw node text straight to `Const.string(value=...)`. Downstream symbol comparison and dispatch will see `:foo` as the string value. This is intentional but the migration must not inadvertently strip the colon. Care is needed when gjoy.1 implements quote-stripping logic in `lower_const_literal` — it must NOT strip the leading colon from symbol nodes.

### Risk 2 (HIGH): `lower_ruby_block` line 639 emits raw `func:` string bypassing `emit_func_ref`

`lower_ruby_block` emits `Const(result_reg=ref_reg, value=f"func:{block_label}")` directly (line 639), unlike `lower_ruby_lambda` (line 340) which correctly calls `ctx.emit_func_ref(...)`. This means blocks are NOT registered in the `func_symbol_table`, so their labels cannot be resolved downstream. The fix (`ctx.emit_func_ref(...)`) is correct but may change existing test behaviour for blocks — all block-related tests should be run and reviewed.

### Risk 3 (MEDIUM): `_make_const` is an untyped helper called with heterogeneous values

`_make_const(ctx, "0")`, `_make_const(ctx, "1")`, and `_make_const(ctx, ctx.constants.none_literal)` all flow through the same untyped path. Splitting them into `Const.int_` vs `Const.null_` correctly is straightforward, but if any call site passes a value not in these three shapes, the migration will fail silently. All 5 call sites of `_make_const` must be reviewed individually.
close_reason: Ruby migrated (61eecf15): symbol :foo colon preserved as string, blocks via emit_func_ref (register in func_symbol_table), interpolation fragments verbatim, nil->null_. Frontend tests green (191).

title: Migrate lua frontend to typed Const
description:
## Exhaustive Typed-Const Migration Analysis — Lua Frontend

### 1. Direct Const(...) Sites (11 total across 3 files)

**interpreter/frontends/lua/expressions.py**

- Line 194 — `lower_table_constructor`: `Const(result_reg=key_reg, value=ctx.node_text(name_node))`
  Table field name keys (identifiers from AST). Type: **string** → `Const.string(key_reg, ctx.node_text(name_node))`

- Line 202 — `lower_table_constructor`: `Const(result_reg=idx_reg, value=str(positional_idx))`
  Synthetic integer index for positional table entries. Type: **int** → `Const.int_(idx_reg, positional_idx)`

- Line 219 — `lower_expression_list`: `Const(result_reg=reg, value=ctx.constants.default_return_value)`
  Fallback nil for empty expression list. Type: **null** → `Const.null_(reg)`

- Line 250 — `lower_lua_function_definition`: `Const(result_reg=none_reg, value=ctx.constants.default_return_value)`
  Default return nil at end of anonymous function body. Type: **null** → `Const.null_(none_reg)`

**interpreter/frontends/lua/control_flow.py**

- Line 172 — `_lower_lua_for_numeric`: `Const(result_reg=step_reg, value="1")`
  Default step value for numeric for loop when step omitted. Type: **int** → `Const.int_(step_reg, 1)`

- Line 268 — `_lower_lua_for_generic`: `Const(result_reg=init_idx, value="0")`
  Initial index for generic for (ipairs/pairs emulation). Type: **int** → `Const.int_(init_idx, 0)`

- Line 312 — `_lower_lua_for_generic`: `Const(result_reg=one_reg, value="1")`
  Increment constant in generic for update step. Type: **int** → `Const.int_(one_reg, 1)`

**interpreter/frontends/lua/declarations.py**

- Line 43 — `lower_lua_variable_declaration`: `Const(result_reg=val_reg, value=ctx.constants.none_literal)`
  Default nil for `local x` without assignment. Type: **null** → `Const.null_(val_reg)`

- Line 81 — `lower_lua_assignment`: `Const(result_reg=val_reg, value=ctx.constants.none_literal)`
  Nil fill when assignment target count exceeds expression count. Type: **null** → `Const.null_(val_reg)`

- Line 162 — `lower_lua_function_declaration`: `Const(result_reg=none_reg, value=ctx.constants.default_return_value)`
  Default return nil at end of named function body. Type: **null** → `Const.null_(none_reg)`

- Line 192 — `lower_lua_return`: `Const(result_reg=val_reg, value=ctx.constants.default_return_value)`
  Bare `return` with no value emits nil. Type: **null** → `Const.null_(val_reg)`

### 2. Literal Dispatch via lower_const_literal (shared emitter, NOT yet typed)

Registered in **frontend.py** dispatch table (lines 39-56):

- `LuaNodeType.NUMBER` → `common_expr.lower_const_literal` — needs int/float dispatch
- `LuaNodeType.STRING` → `common_expr.lower_const_literal` — needs string dispatch
- `LuaNodeType.TRUE` → `common_expr.lower_canonical_true` — already dispatches to `Const(..., value=ctx.constants.true_literal)` — not yet typed
- `LuaNodeType.FALSE` → `common_expr.lower_canonical_false` — same
- `LuaNodeType.NIL` → `common_expr.lower_canonical_none` — same
- `LuaNodeType.STRING_CONTENT` → `common_expr.lower_const_literal` — string content within delimiters
- `LuaNodeType.ESCAPE_SEQUENCE` → `common_expr.lower_const_literal` — escape chars within strings

The shared emitters (`lower_const_literal`, `lower_canonical_true/false/none`) are migrated under red-dragon-gjoy.1 (the keystone). The lua-specific task is to (a) fix lua's 11 direct Const() sites, and (b) ensure lua-specific NUMBER dispatch splits integer vs float correctly after the shared emitter is upgraded.

**Lua number literal dispatch**: `lower_const_literal` uses `ctx.node_text(node)` raw. For Lua, tree-sitter NUMBER nodes carry the raw text (e.g., `42`, `3.14`, `0xFF`, `0x1p4`). Post-gjoy.1, the shared emitter will need to parse the text and call `Const.int_` or `Const.float_`. Lua 5.3+ distinguishes integers from floats; the parser needs `'.' in text or 'e'/'E'/'p'/'P' in text` to select float.

### 3. String Handling

Lua strings: single quotes, double quotes, long-bracket strings `[[` ... `]]` (level 0) or `[=[` ... `]=]` (level N). The tree-sitter Lua grammar parses strings as a `string` node containing `string_content` and `escape_sequence` children. Long-bracket strings have no escape sequences; their content is literal. Both STRING and STRING_CONTENT map to `lower_const_literal`, which calls `ctx.node_text(node)` — this returns raw text including delimiters for STRING nodes, or content-only for STRING_CONTENT children, depending on what tree-sitter hands us.

The migration must strip surrounding quote characters (', ", [[ ]], [=[ ]=]) before calling `Const.string`. This is a lua-specific string normalizer: check for long-bracket prefix `[=*[` and strip the matching close; otherwise strip matching first/last char.

### 4. func_ref / class_ref

No direct `Const.func_ref` or `Const.class_ref` calls in lua frontend. `ctx.emit_func_ref()` (in context.py) emits the FUNC_REF constant and is handled by the shared keystone migration (red-dragon-gjoy.1). Lua uses table-based OOP; `extract_lua_symbols` returns `SymbolTable.empty()` — no class refs.

### 5. Exact Ordered Change List

1. **After red-dragon-gjoy.1 lands**: Update imports in expressions.py, control_flow.py, declarations.py to use typed factories (remove bare `Const` constructor calls).
2. **control_flow.py:172** — `Const(step_reg, value="1")` → `Const.int_(step_reg, 1)`
3. **control_flow.py:268** — `Const(init_idx, value="0")` → `Const.int_(init_idx, 0)`
4. **control_flow.py:312** — `Const(one_reg, value="1")` → `Const.int_(one_reg, 1)`
5. **expressions.py:194** — table key_reg → `Const.string(key_reg, ctx.node_text(name_node))`
6. **expressions.py:202** — positional idx → `Const.int_(idx_reg, positional_idx)`
7. **expressions.py:219** — empty expression_list nil → `Const.null_(reg)`
8. **expressions.py:250** — anon fn default return → `Const.null_(none_reg)`
9. **declarations.py:43** — local x nil init → `Const.null_(val_reg)`
10. **declarations.py:81** — assignment nil fill → `Const.null_(val_reg)`
11. **declarations.py:162** — named fn default return → `Const.null_(none_reg)`
12. **declarations.py:192** — bare return nil → `Const.null_(val_reg)`
13. **Verify** NUMBER dispatch in shared `lower_const_literal` handles Lua int vs float correctly (dot/e/p suffix detection). If shared emitter is language-agnostic, add lua-specific NUMBER lowerer in lua/expressions.py.

### 6. Test Command

`poetry run python -m pytest tests/ -k lua -q`

Lua integration files: tests/integration/test_lua_generic_for_execution.py, test_lua_method_index_execution.py, test_lua_table_oop_execution.py. Exercism: ~18 lua.lua fixtures under tests/unit/exercism/exercises/*/solutions/. Current pre-migration state: **25 passed, 86 failed, 678 errors** (errors are pre-existing fixture/setup issues unrelated to Const typing).

### 7. Risks

**Risk 1 (HIGH) — int vs float ambiguity in NUMBER dispatch**: Lua 5.3+ has a hard semantic integer/float split. A raw `ctx.node_text(NUMBER_node)` returns text like `0xFF` (int), `0x1p4` (hex float), `3.` (float), `42` (int). If the shared `lower_const_literal` uses Python `int()` and `float()` heuristics, hex floats (`0x1p4`) or trailing-dot floats (`3.`) may be misclassified or throw. Need explicit pattern: `is_float = '.' in text or any(c in text.lower() for c in 'ep')`.

**Risk 2 (MEDIUM) — Long-bracket string content stripping**: Lua long-bracket strings `[[` ... `]]` can span multiple lines and contain no escape sequences. The tree-sitter STRING node's text includes the delimiters. If `lower_const_literal` is upgraded to strip quotes naively (strip first/last char), long-bracket strings with level markers `[=[` ... `]=]` will produce corrupt values. Need explicit long-bracket detector and level-aware stripping before passing to `Const.string`.
close_reason: Lua migrated (48bf89c8): int-vs-float detection (hex floats via float.fromhex, trailing-dot), long-bracket [[]]/[=[]=] level-aware stripping, nil->null_. Frontend tests green (80 unit + 13 integration).

title: Migrate cpp frontend to typed Const
description:
## Exhaustive Typed-Const Migration Analysis: cpp frontend

Branch: typed-const-literals
Analysis date: 2026-06-17

---

## 1. Every Const(...) site in the cpp frontend

**Total untyped Const( sites: 10** (all using raw string `value=...` without a typed factory)

### expressions.py

| Line | Method | Value | Intended type |
|------|--------|-------|---------------|
| 110 | `lower_lambda` | `ctx.constants.default_return_value` ("0") | `Const.int_(…, 0)` |
| 210 | `lower_throw_expr` | `ctx.constants.default_return_value` ("0") | `Const.int_(…, 0)` |

### control_flow.py

| Line | Method | Value | Intended type |
|------|--------|-------|---------------|
| 168 | `_lower_structured_binding` | `str(i)` (loop index) | `Const.int_(…, i)` |
| 203 | `lower_range_for` | `"0"` (initial loop index) | `Const.int_(…, 0)` |
| 248 | `lower_range_for` | `"1"` (loop increment) | `Const.int_(…, 1)` |

### declarations.py

| Line | Method | Value | Intended type |
|------|--------|-------|---------------|
| 74 | `lower_cpp_declaration` | `ctx.constants.none_literal` ("None") | `Const.null_(…)` |
| 210 | `_lower_cpp_constructor_with_field_inits` | `ctx.constants.default_return_value` ("0") | `Const.int_(…, 0)` |
| 389 | `lower_cpp_method` | `ctx.constants.default_return_value` ("0") | `Const.int_(…, 0)` |
| 428 | `lower_field_initializer_list` | `ctx.constants.default_return_value` ("0") | `Const.int_(…, 0)` |
| 497 | `lower_cpp_function_def` | `ctx.constants.default_return_value` ("0") | `Const.int_(…, 0)` |

---

## 2. Literal dispatch: node types → Const kind

The cpp frontend **inherits from CFrontend**, which maps these literal node types to `lower_const_literal` (untyped, emits raw source text):

### Inherited from CFrontend (c/frontend.py)

| Node type | C NodeType constant | What's emitted | Should become |
|-----------|-------------------|----------------|---------------|
| `number_literal` | `CNodeType.NUMBER_LITERAL` | raw text e.g. `"42"`, `"3.14f"`, `"0xFF"`, `"1'000"` | `Const.int_` or `Const.float_` after suffix/base parsing |
| `string_literal` | `CNodeType.STRING_LITERAL` | raw text e.g. `'"hello"'` | `Const.string` with quotes stripped + escapes decoded |
| `char_literal` | `CNodeType.CHAR_LITERAL` | raw text e.g. `"'a'"` | `Const.int_(ord('a'))` — C/C++ char is an int |
| `true` | `CNodeType.TRUE` | → `lower_canonical_true` → `"True"` | → `Const.bool_(…, True)` |
| `false` | `CNodeType.FALSE` | → `lower_canonical_false` → `"False"` | → `Const.bool_(…, False)` |
| `null` | `CNodeType.NULL` | → `lower_canonical_none` → `"None"` | → `Const.null_(…)` |
| `concatenated_string` | `CNodeType.CONCATENATED_STRING` | raw text | `Const.string` (joined, unquoted) |

### Overridden / added in CppFrontend (cpp/frontend.py)

| Node type | CppNodeType constant | Handler | Should become |
|-----------|---------------------|---------|---------------|
| `nullptr` | `CppNodeType.NULLPTR` | `lower_canonical_none` | → `Const.null_(…)` |
| `user_defined_literal` | `CppNodeType.USER_DEFINED_LITERAL` | `lower_const_literal` | `Const.string` (raw text, UDL suffix preserved) |
| `raw_string_literal` | `CppNodeType.RAW_STRING_LITERAL` | `lower_const_literal` | `Const.string` (delimiter-stripped, unescaped content) |

### Indirect uses in expressions.py (fallback/placeholder)

| Line | Context | Handler | Should become |
|------|---------|---------|---------------|
| 226 | `lower_cpp_cast` (no value_node fallback) | `lower_const_literal` | `Const.string` (or a Symbolic — currently uses node text as placeholder) |
| 243 | `lower_condition_clause` (empty fallback) | `lower_const_literal` | `Const.bool_(…, True)` (a condition default-true makes semantic sense) |
| 260 | `lower_cpp_subscript_expr` (no named children) | `lower_const_literal` | `Const.int_(…, 0)` (index placeholder) |

---

## 3. String handling

### Regular string literals (`string_literal`)
- Inherited via CFrontend → `lower_const_literal` → emits raw source text including quotes: `'"hello"'`
- After migration: call `Const.string(reg, unquoted_text)` where unquoted_text strips outer `"..."` and decodes C escape sequences (`\n`, `\t`, `\\`, `\"`, `\xNN`, `\uNNNN` etc.)
- Wide strings (`L"..."`, `u"..."`, `U"..."`, `u8"..."`) — prefixes must be stripped; content is same otherwise

### Raw string literals (`raw_string_literal`) — C++ only
- Tree-sitter node type: `raw_string_literal`; dispatched in CppFrontend to `lower_const_literal`
- Source form: `R"delimiter(actual content)delimiter"` — the delimiter can be up to 16 chars
- Current code emits the ENTIRE source text including `R"...(...)"` wrapper
- After migration: strip `R"`, find the delimiter (chars between `"` and `(`), extract content between `(delimiter` and `delimiter)"`, call `Const.string(reg, content)` — no escape processing needed (raw strings have no escapes by design)

### Concatenated strings (`concatenated_string`)
- Adjacent string literals: `"hello" " world"` → single logical string
- Currently emits full source text as string value
- After migration: strip quotes from each component and join, call `Const.string`

### char literals (`char_literal`)
- Form: `'a'`, `'\n'`, `'\x41'`
- In C/C++, char is an integer; the typed Const should be `Const.int_(reg, ord_value)` after unquoting and escape decode
- Multi-character literals (e.g. `'ab'`) are implementation-defined — emit as `Const.int_` with `0` fallback or `Symbolic`

---

## 4. func/class refs

The cpp frontend uses `ctx.emit_func_ref(...)` and `ctx.emit_class_ref(...)` inherited from the shared context/base layer. These are:
- `emit_func_ref` in `interpreter/frontends/context.py` (or `_base.py`) — currently emits `Const(value=func_label_string)`
- `emit_class_ref` similarly
