title: Migrate non-handler Const consumers: type_inference, memory, registry, linker, calls, executor
description:
Migrate the code that READS Const.value / calls _parse_const (other than
_handle_const, already done):
- interpreter/types/type_inference.py: infer a literal's type from `Const.type_expr`
  instead of from `value`. Add unit tests: INT const -> Int, STRING const -> String.
- interpreter/handlers/memory.py (~424): replace `_parse_const(raw)` index/figurative
  resolution with the operand register's typed value. Test indexed load/store with an
  INT const index.
- interpreter/registry.py, interpreter/project/linker.py: audit Const.value reads
  (func/class label collection); key behavior on type_expr being FunctionType/metatype.
  Behavior unchanged; add a linking test if missing.
- interpreter/handlers/calls.py + interpreter/vm/executor.py: drop unused _parse_const
  import / re-export.

Acceptance: no consumer relies on string re-parsing; their tests green. Do NOT delete
_parse_const yet (that's the final story). Parent: red-dragon-gjoy.
notes: ADD TO SCOPE: interpreter/interprocedural/types.py:223 has module-level _SENTINEL_INSTRUCTION = IRInstruction(opcode=Opcode.CONST, operands=[]) which now breaks _const conversion (no literal_type) → ~200 test COLLECTION errors across the suite. Migrate it to a typed sentinel (e.g. literal_type='Null' or a typed Const). This blocks full-suite green, so do it as part of consumers.
close_reason: Consumers migrated (39b41f99): type_inference uses inst.type_expr; memory drops _parse_const for class consts; interprocedural sentinel typed (literal_type=Null, clears collection errors); _parse_const imports removed from memory/calls/executor; test_type_inference _make_inst derives literal_type. null->UNKNOWN preserved (null->Null is option-style x78r). Consumers no longer rely on _parse_const.

title: Const type round-trips through _parse_const re-guessing — numeric-looking string literals silently become ints
description:
DESIGN SMELL discovered 2026-06-16 while fixing red-dragon-m0oa.7 (READ AT END
infinite loop).

`Const.value` is typed Any and `Const.operands` returns [self.value] WITHOUT
stringifying (interpreter/instructions.py ~202-214). So the producer's Python
type is preserved at the creation site. But `_handle_const`
(interpreter/handlers/variables.py:49-50) discards it:

    raw = inst.operands[0]
    val = _parse_const(raw)

and `_parse_const` (interpreter/vm/vm.py:378-397) RE-GUESSES the type:

    try: return int(raw)      # "10" (a STRING) -> int 10  <-- silent corruption
    except: pass
    try: return float(raw)
    except: pass
    # quote-strip -> string

CONSEQUENCE: a Const whose value is the Python string "10" (a 2-char COBOL
alphanumeric, e.g. a file-status code) is silently converted to the integer 10.
Comparisons like `String "10" == Int 10` then fail. This was the root cause of
the READ AT END infinite loop (m0oa.7) and affects every numeric-looking string
literal that isn't defensively quoted.

WHY IT EXISTS: legacy of the stringly-typed/LLM-oriented IR — text producers emit
Const as a string ("42" meaning int 42) and _parse_const recovers the type.
Frontends that DO know the type (COBOL) must currently disambiguate by emitting
alphanumeric literals quoted ('"10"' survives as a string; bare "10" reparses to
int). The COBOL MOVE path already quotes; the file-status path did not (fixed in
m0oa.7 via _status_const_reg).

PROPOSED FIX (principled, cross-cutting — needs care):
- Make Const carry type unambiguously: either (a) `_handle_const` trusts
  inst.value's Python type when it is already a non-string (and treats str as
  str), only invoking _parse_const for genuinely stringly-typed producers; or
  (b) add an explicit literal-type tag to Const set at emit time.
- BLAST RADIUS: all 15 frontends + the LLM interpretation path (which relies on
  the string re-guess) + type inference. Must audit each producer: does it emit
  numeric Consts as Python numbers or as strings? Add tests per frontend.

ACCEPTANCE:
- A Const created with a Python str value "10" resolves to the string "10" at
  runtime (not int 10), without needing defensive quoting.
- No regression across the 15 frontends (full suite green).
- Once fixed, the _status_const_reg quoting workaround in lower_io.py can be
  simplified/removed.

Related: red-dragon-m0oa.7 (READ AT END fix that exposed this).
notes:
INVESTIGATION (2026-06-16): the int/float re-parse in _parse_const is LOAD-BEARING,
not a removable bug. ~114 sites across ALL 15 frontends emit numeric literals as
STRINGS and depend on the reparse:
  - Python frontend: Const(value="0"), Const(value="1"), Const(value=str(i)) for
    array indices, sizes, loop counters.
  - Every frontend has similar sites (python 10, js/go/csharp 4 each, ... ; plus
    ~? Const(value=str(...)) sites: java 9, common 9, csharp 7, rust 6, python 6...).
  - _base.py itself emits Const(value="1").

So the IR Const is effectively "stringly typed" with an IMPLICIT convention:
  * numeric literal  -> bare string ("0","1",str(n))  -> _parse_const -> number
  * string literal   -> QUOTED string ('"10"')        -> _parse_const -> string
The COBOL file-status bug (m0oa.7) was a violation of this convention (unquoted
string literal). The fix there (quote it) is consistent with the convention.

CONCLUSION: removing the reparse requires migrating all ~114 numeric sites to emit
real Python numbers (Const(value=0)) AND auditing the LLM/text interpretation path
(which emits instructions as text and relies on reparse). This is a genuine
cross-cutting refactor with high blast radius, NOT a quick fix.

RECOMMENDED APPROACH (own effort, brainstorm+plan):
  Phase 1: introduce explicit emit helpers (emit_int_const / emit_str_const) and/or
           a typed literal tag on Const; keep _parse_const as a fallback.
  Phase 2: migrate frontends language-by-language to the typed helpers, with the
           full per-language suite green at each step.
  Phase 3: once all producers are typed, drop _parse_const's string coercion;
           remove the _status_const_reg quoting workaround.
Lower-risk interim: add a lint/guard so new string-literal Consts must use the
explicit string helper, preventing new convention violations.
Execution underway on branch 'typed-const-literals'. Core DONE: Const.type_expr required + typed factories + typed _handle_const (Task 2), LLM wire literal_type (Task 3), all COBOL producers + parse_literal bug fix (Task 4), NULL scalar (Task 1). Remaining frontend/consumer/deletion work tracked under EPIC red-dragon-gjoy with exhaustively-analyzed per-frontend child stories (gjoy.1 keystone, gjoy.2 consumers, gjoy.3 delete+merge, gjoy.4-17 + gjoy.1.1 = 15 frontends). Branch suite intentionally RED until the epic completes (atomic required-field design); main untouched.

comments.author: avishek-sen-gupta
comments.text: FIXED via epic red-dragon-gjoy. Const now carries a REQUIRED TypeExpr; _parse_const (the string-type-guessing function where '10' silently became int 10) is deleted. Null literals are the canonical NULL scalar. Follow-up option-style nullability tracked in red-dragon-x78r.

title: Handle COBOL DECLARATIVES: skip in normal flow, set entry after END DECLARATIVES (9 programs)
description:
ROOT CAUSE of 9 programs originally mis-labeled "step-cap" (verified 2026-06-16 they are
NOT step-capped — identical behavior at max_steps 50k/200k/500k):
  SQ212A, IX104A, IX108A, IX204A, IX216A, RL104A, RL111A, RL112A, RL119A

The COBOL frontend has NO DECLARATIVES handling (grep -i declarative on interpreter/cobol/
and proleap-bridge/src/ → nothing). ProLeap returns DECLARATIVES sections via
pd.getSections() with no flag; AsgSerializer.java (serializeProcedureDivision /
serializeSections) emits them as ordinary sections, FIRST in order. So the first
DECLARATIVES section becomes the de-facto program entry point.

Concrete failure (SQ212A):
  PROCEDURE DIVISION. (line 293)
  DECLARATIVES. (294)
  SECT-SQ212A-0001 SECTION. (295)
      USE AFTER STANDARD EXCEPTION PROCEDURE ON SQ-VS7. (296)   <-- USE error handler
      ... body with WRITEs ... STOP RUN (432)
  END DECLARATIVES. (433)
  CCVS1 SECTION. (434)
  OPEN-FILES. (435)
      OPEN OUTPUT PRINT-FILE. (446)                              <-- never reached

Runtime evidence: IR contains 4 __cobol_open_file but runtime executes 0 opens
(passing programs execute 3). First runtime I/O call is write_record, not open_file.
Program runs the USE procedure body (17 phantom WRITEs to an unopened PRINT-FILE),
hits the in-declaratives STOP RUN, never opens PRINT-FILE → no output file → skip.
SQ103A passes only by luck: its declaratives section is a bare USE with no body, so
control falls through into OPEN-FILES.

FIX (minimal, recovers all 9 — none of these tests intentionally trigger the error path):
1. proleap-bridge AsgSerializer.java: mark sections that live inside
   DECLARATIVES ... END DECLARATIVES (ProLeap exposes declaratives; add e.g.
   "declarative": true on each such section). Rebuild bridge (mvn package -DskipTests).
2. interpreter/cobol asg_types.py + cobol_frontend.py lowering: exclude declaratives
   sections from normal control flow; set the program entry point to the first
   paragraph/section AFTER END DECLARATIVES.

FOLLOW-UP (separate child red-dragon-m0oa.4): wire USE AFTER STANDARD ERROR/EXCEPTION
procedures to actually fire on matching I/O errors. Not required to pass these 9.

Acceptance:
- All 9 programs above pass in tests/nist/test_{sq,ix,rl}.py (they currently skip).
- Add a focused integration test: a COBOL program with a non-empty DECLARATIVES USE
  section whose body would corrupt output if run as entry; assert normal flow starts
  after END DECLARATIVES.

Related: red-dragon-m0oa (epic). Memory: project_nist85_probe.md.
notes: Planning discovered a 10th affected program: RL204A (also DECLARATIVES; makes zero I/O calls). Target set is now 10 programs: SQ212A, IX104A, IX108A, IX204A, IX216A, RL104A, RL111A, RL112A, RL119A, RL204A. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-16-cobol-declaratives.md
close_reason: DECLARATIVES recognized: bridge emits declaratives sections separately (excluded from standalone paragraphs), lowering emits them after real flow so entry point lands after END DECLARATIVES. 10 programs (incl RL204A) move skip->pass; entry-point behavior covered by falsifiable unit + integration tests (verified to FAIL when fix reverted). NIST 153 pass/9 skip (M-stubs only). Caveat: NIST PASS/FAIL content still vacuous pending m0oa.6 (WRITE-without-FROM). Pushed.

title: Parse bare 'PIC P' scaling clause (IX110A)
description:
NIST program IX110A has the declaration:
    01 STATUS-TEST-10 PIC P VALUE ZERO.
A bare 'P' (assumed-decimal scaling position with zero significant digits).

Current behavior: ValueError: Cannot parse PIC clause: 'P' — the Lark PIC grammar
requires digit symbols (9/X/etc.) and rejects a PIC made of only scaling 'P's.

Fix location: interpreter/cobol/pic_parser.py — allow a PIC composed solely of P
scaling characters (zero significant digits). Determine the resulting category/size
per COBOL rules (numeric, value scaled by power of ten; storage is zero digit positions).

Acceptance:
- tests/nist/test_ix.py::test_ix_program[IX110A] passes (remove xfail mark).
- Add a focused unit test for pic_parser parsing 'P', 'PP', '9PPP', 'PPP9'.

Currently marked xfail in tests/nist/test_ix.py.
Related: red-dragon-m0oa (epic).
close_reason: Added scaling_only grammar alternative; bare PIC P (and PPP, SPP) now parse to zero-digit numeric. IX110A passes (xfail removed). Unit + regression tests added. Pushed to main.

title: INPUT-mode WRITE must return file status 48, not raise io.UnsupportedOperation (SQ152A, SQ155A)
description:
NIST programs SQ152A and SQ155A are error-recovery tests that deliberately attempt
a WRITE to a file opened in INPUT mode, expecting the runtime to return file status
"48" (WRITE attempted on file not open for output) which the program then checks.

Current behavior: the file driver calls fh.write() on a read-only file handle,
raising io.UnsupportedOperation: write — the run errors out (ERROR, not FAIL/skip).

Expected: detect the open mode before writing; if the file is not open in OUTPUT/EXTEND/I-O
mode, return IOResult("48", None) instead of attempting the OS-level write.

Fix location: interpreter/cobol/file_drivers.py — the write() path(s). Guard on the
tracked open mode and return status 48. Check SequentialDriver (and any shared base)
since SQ152A/SQ155A are sequential.

Acceptance:
- tests/nist/test_sq.py::test_sq_program[SQ152A] and [SQ155A] pass (remove xfail marks
  added in commit expanding parametrize lists).
- Add a focused unit/integration test asserting status 48 on INPUT-mode write.

Currently marked xfail in tests/nist/test_sq.py.
Related: red-dragon-m0oa (epic). Overlaps conceptually with USE-procedure error
dispatch (DECLARATIVES child) — these tests check status directly, no USE needed.
close_reason: WRITE in non-writable open mode now returns status 48 across all 3 drivers. SQ152A/SQ155A pass (xfail removed). Unit tests added. Commit pushed to main.

title: ProLeap EXEC SQL preprocessor reports 'missing END_EXEC' for certain SQL bodies
description:
ProLeap's preprocessor (CobolPreprocessorParser.execSqlStatement) intermittently fails with 'syntax error … missing END_EXEC at <EOF>' for certain EXEC SQL bodies, consuming past the real END-EXEC. Surfaced building squall's WHENEVER e2e.

REPRO (each is EXEC SQL <body> END-EXEC inside a minimal program):
  FAILS: SELECT 1 INTO :WS-VAL FROM NO_SUCH_TABLE_XYZ        (multi-underscore table, no WHERE)
  FAILS: SELECT 1 INTO :WS-VAL FROM ACCOUNT WHERE ACCT_ID = 1  (underscore col = integer literal)
  OK:    SELECT 1 INTO :WS-VAL FROM A_B                       (single-underscore table)
  OK:    SELECT 1 INTO :WS-VAL FROM ABC
  OK:    SELECT ACCT_ID INTO :WS-VAL FROM ACCOUNT            (underscore col in SELECT list)
  OK:    SELECT ACCT_BAL INTO :WS-BAL FROM ACCOUNT WHERE ACCT_ID = :ACCT-ID  (host-var compare; test_crud passes)

So underscores alone aren't the rule — host-var comparisons work but an integer-literal comparison against an underscore column fails, and a long multi-underscore table name fails. Looks like ProLeap's embedded SQL token rule mis-scans some identifier/literal patterns. squall worked around it by avoiding the pattern in test_db2_whenever_e2e.py (table NOSUCHTABLEXYZ). Investigate the ProLeap execSqlStatement / SQL token grammar in proleap-bridge.

title: ProLeap: adjacent EXEC SQL blocks (no period) merge into one statement — mirror EXEC CICS adjacency fix
description:
Two EXEC SQL ... END-EXEC blocks with no period and no intervening COBOL statement are parsed as a SINGLE execSqlStatement whose text spans both, e.g. 'INSERT ... END-EXEC EXEC SQL SELECT ...'. Downstream (squall) then sends malformed SQL ('near END: syntax error').

EXEC CICS already handles this: proleap-cobol-parser has src/test/resources/io/proleap/cobol/asg/procedure/execcics/ExecCicsAdjacent.cbl (two adjacent EXEC CICS ... END-EXEC, no periods) and parses them as separate statements. There is NO equivalent execsql adjacency test, and the behaviour diverges.

Grammar: CobolPreprocessor.g4 — execCicsStatement: EXEC CICS charData END_EXEC DOT?  vs  execSqlStatement: EXEC SQL charDataSql END_EXEC DOT?. charData stops at the first END_EXEC (CICS adjacency works); charDataSql (which additionally allows COPY|REPLACE) over-consumes past the first END_EXEC. 

Fix: make charDataSql terminate at the first END_EXEC the way charData does (and/or align the bridge's getExecSqlText() span extraction), and add an ExecSqlAdjacent.cbl test mirroring ExecCicsAdjacent.cbl. 

Workaround in callers: period-terminate each EXEC SQL ... END-EXEC. (squall's CRUD capstone does this; real mainframe COBOL usually does too, which is why it wasn't caught earlier.)
close_reason: Grammar fix (EXECSQLLINE* EXECSQLENDLINE) + ExecSqlAdjacentTest; 829 parser tests green; squall no-period CRUD e2e passes

title: PERFORM VARYING AFTER not supported (multi-index nested loop)
description:
COBOL's most general PERFORM VARYING form allows multiple nested loop variables via AFTER clauses:

```cobol
PERFORM VARYING I FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL I > 10
    AFTER J FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL J > 10
        MOVE ZERO TO TABLE(I, J)
END-PERFORM
```

This is semantically a nested loop where the outermost variable is `I` and the innermost is `J` — J's loop runs to completion for every increment of I.

## What works today

- `PERFORM ... VARYING var FROM x BY y UNTIL cond` (single variable, both inline and out-of-line) ✅
- TEST BEFORE / TEST AFTER ✅
- FROM as literal, field ref, or structured expr (LENGTH OF) ✅

## The gap

The ProLeap ASG models AFTER clauses via `VaryingClause.getAfters()` (returns `List<After>`), but the bridge serializer in `StatementSerializer.java` ignores them entirely.

**Bridge gap** — `StatementSerializer.java` line ~645:
```java
VaryingClause vc = varying.getVaryingClause();
if (vc != null && vc.getVaryingPhrase() != null) {
    // ... serializes primary VARYING phrase only
    // vc.getAfters() is never called
}
```
The AFTER clauses carry the same structure as the primary phrase (var, FROM, BY, UNTIL), but `getAfters()` is never iterated.

**Python gap** — `interpreter/cobol/cobol_statements.py::PerformVaryingSpec` has no field for nested specs:
```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PerformVaryingSpec:
    varying_var: str
    varying_from: str | dict
    varying_by: str
    condition: dict
    test_before: bool = True
    # no after_specs field
```

**Lowering gap** — `interpreter/cobol/lower_perform.py::lower_perform_varying` has no nested-loop emission logic.

## Fix plan

1. **Bridge**: In `StatementSerializer.java`, after serializing the primary phrase, iterate `vc.getAfters()` and emit them as a JSON array `"after_specs": [...]`, each with `varying_var`, `varying_from`, `varying_by`, `until` fields (same schema as the primary).

2. **Python dataclass**: Add `after_specs: list[PerformVaryingSpec] = field(default_factory=list)` to `PerformVaryingSpec`, and deserialize the array in the statement builder.

3. **Lowering**: In `lower_perform_varying`, after generating the outer loop structure, recursively emit inner loops for each AFTER spec. The correct semantics: AFTER variables are re-initialized and their loops run to completion on each increment of the outer variable. The UNTIL condition on the outer loop checks after the inner loops exhaust.

## Relevant files

- `proleap-bridge/src/main/java/org/reddragon/bridge/StatementSerializer.java` — bridge serializer (line ~641–663)
- `interpreter/cobol/cobol_statements.py` — `PerformVaryingSpec` dataclass (line ~42–49)
- `interpreter/cobol/lower_perform.py` — `lower_perform_varying` (line ~215–261), `emit_varying_increment` (line ~267–295)
- `tests/integration/test_cobol_programs.py` — `TestPerformVarying` (line ~473), `TestOccursLoop.test_perform_varying_with_occurs` (line ~937) — new test class needed here
- `proleap-bridge/proleap-cobol-parser/src/main/java/io/proleap/cobol/asg/metamodel/procedure/perform/VaryingClause.java` — confirms `getAfters()` exists in the ASG

title: COBOL: subscripted level-88 condition name in EVALUATE/IF never matches (e.g. SEL-OK(I))
description:
A level-88 condition name defined on an OCCURS element and referenced WITH a subscript never matches; the branch silently never fires.

Repro (EVALUATE TRUE WHEN <subscripted-88>):
  01 WS-FLAGS PIC X(3) VALUE 'SU '.
  01 WS-ARR REDEFINES WS-FLAGS.
     05 WS-SEL PIC X(1) OCCURS 3 TIMES.
        88 SEL-OK VALUES 'S','U'.
  01 WS-IDX PIC 9(1) VALUE 1.
  ...
  MOVE 1 TO WS-IDX.
  EVALUATE TRUE
    WHEN SEL-OK(WS-IDX)   *> WS-SEL(1)='S' should match
      ...                 *> but falls to WHEN OTHER
Logs: 'unparseable condition "SEL-OK(WS-IDX)" — never matching'.

Root cause: the ProLeap bridge serializes the WHEN condition as the PLAIN TEXT string 'SEL-OK(WS-IDX)' (verified via stmt.children dump) rather than a structured condition-name node. condition_lowering._lower_condition_str then splits on whitespace into one token 'SEL-OK(WS-IDX)', and condition_index.has_condition() misses because the index is keyed by the bare name 'SEL-OK' (the subscript is glued on). It is not a 3-part 'field OP value' either, so it hits the catch-all 'unparseable -> Const(False)'.

Fix has two layers:
1. Bridge (StatementSerializer): serialize a subscripted condition-name reference as a structured node carrying name + subscripts (like other subscripted refs), instead of flattening to text. Aligns with red-dragon-z6ad (kill structure->string round-trips).
2. Python: _lower_condition_node / _expand_condition_name must apply the subscript when expanding the 88 to its parent comparison (WS-SEL(idx) == 'S' OR == 'U'), threading subscripts through resolve_field_ref. (A Python-only stopgap could parse the '(subscript)' out of the token in _lower_condition_str, but still needs the subscript-threaded expansion.)

Impact: AWS CardDemo row-selection logic — COCRDLIC SELECT-OK(I)/SELECT-BLANK(I), and the analogous selection EVALUATEs in COTRN00C / COUSR00C. The list/browse DISPLAYS are unaffected (cicada test_carddemo_card_list passes); selecting a listed row to drill into detail/update would silently not register. Surfaced while implementing cicada COCRDLIC e2e (cicada-jn1).
close_reason: Fixed in ef8c1eab: _lower_condition_str splits a subscripted condition-name token (SEL-OK(I)) into name + subscript expr nodes, and _expand_condition_name/_emit_single_value_test thread the subscript through resolve_field_ref so the 88 tests the indexed element. Python-only; full suite green; COCRDLIC warning gone. Verified end-to-end in cicada test_carddemo_card_list.

title: Validate COBOL coverage using NIST-85 test suite (NC nucleus module first)
description:
Integrate the NIST-85 COBOL conformance test suite as a pytest-discoverable test layer to objectively measure red-dragon's COBOL execution correctness.

## Background

The NIST-85 suite (918 programs) is already present in the repo at proleap-bridge/proleap-cobol-parser/target/test-classes/gov/nist/. Programs are organised by module prefix: NC (Nucleus, 95 files), IF (Intrinsic Functions), ST (String), IC (Inter-program CALL), SQ/RL/IX (file I/O).

## Scope

Start with the NC (Nucleus) module — 95 fully self-contained programs that test non-IO COBOL features (arithmetic, conditionals, PERFORM, REDEFINES, signed types, ROUNDED, SIZE ERROR, etc.). NC programs have no CALL to external programs and no COPY directives. All other word-matches for CALL/COPY in NC files are paragraph names or string literals.

## Key design decisions (already brainstormed)

1. **Output mechanism**: Every NIST program writes pass/fail results to a sequential file (WRITE DUMMY-RECORD), not via DISPLAY. But each program also maintains WS counters: PASS-COUNTER PIC 999, ERROR-COUNTER PIC 999, INSPECT-COUNTER PIC 999, DELETE-COUNTER PIC 999. Read these from the WS region after STOP RUN — no file I/O needed.

2. **I/O stubs**: OPEN/CLOSE/WRITE against PRINT-FILE must not crash. Treat as no-ops or soft-fail with a warning. The XXXXX055/XXXXX082/XXXXX083 X-card placeholders are harmless — ProLeap parses them as identifiers and they never resolve.

3. **Test shape**: pytest parametrize over all NC*.CBL files. Each file = one test case (test_nist[NC101A] etc.). Add @pytest.mark.nist so they are excluded from the normal suite and CI until coverage is high enough.

4. **Success criterion**: ERROR-COUNTER == 0 at program end. Report PASS-COUNTER and DELETE-COUNTER as informational.

## WS counter extraction

After run(), read PASS-COUNTER and ERROR-COUNTER from the memory region using DataLayout. The CobolFrontend already retains self._layout after lower(); need a helper that maps field name → decoded value given the final VM state.

## Future extension

Once NC is green (or at a stable baseline), extend to IF (intrinsic functions) and ST (string manipulation) — both are likely self-contained. IC module requires running paired programs together. SQ/RL/IX require sequential file I/O implementation.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A pytest.mark.nist suite runs all 95 NC*.CBL programs.
2. Each test passes when ERROR-COUNTER == 0 after STOP RUN.
3. OPEN/CLOSE/WRITE against the print file do not crash the VM.
4. A baseline pass-rate report is produced showing which NC tests pass/fail.
5. The nist suite is excluded from default CI (run with -m nist explicitly).

title: COBOL: field-to-field numeric MOVE loses fractional digits (decode→str coercion)
description:
MOVE of a numeric-DISPLAY field into another numeric field drops the fractional part. E.g. WS-SRC PIC S9(9)V99 VALUE 12345.67; MOVE WS-SRC TO WS-DST (also S9(9)V99) stores 12345.00, losing .67.

Root cause: in lower_move, the source is decoded (emit_decode_field yields the correct float 12345.67 — verified by running build_decode_zoned_ir in isolation) then converted via emit_to_string. In the full pipeline the decoded register value is coerced from float to int before str() is called (no int() builtin call occurs; the runtime division 1234567/100 correctly yields 12345.67, yet str receives int 12345). The coercion comes from the VM type environment (_coerce_value / coerce_local_update in interpreter/vm/vm.py) inferring the decode-result register as Int.

Reproduces on clean code, independent of the numeric-editing / edit_picture work. A LITERAL source (MOVE 12345.67 TO WS-DST) is NOT affected — it preserves the fraction — because it skips the field decode path.

Impact: any field-to-field MOVE of fractional values; also blocks full fractional fidelity when MOVEing a numeric field into a numeric-edited item (edit_picture).

This is a specific manifestation of the broader float-based numeric pipeline tracked in red-dragon-4q25.1 (all numeric decode returns Python float for fixed-point categories). Acceptance criterion #4 there — 'No decode function returns Python float for fixed-point categories; all return exact integer-scaled values' — would resolve this. Linked as depends-on.

Originally filed in Cicada's Beads DB as cicada-hiv (now closed, moved here as a RedDragon-level VM bug).

Repro / coverage note: cicada tests/integration/test_cobol_numeric_edited.py uses a whole-number field source to avoid this bug.

title: Multi-dimensional OCCURS subscript offset arithmetic (currently silently wrong)
description: Multi-dimensional table refs TBL(I, J) are unsupported and silently incorrect end to end. Bridge: StatementSerializer.extractCallName does subscripts.get(0) -- keeps only the first subscript, drops the rest. Python: EmitContext.resolve_field_ref (interpreter/cobol/emit_context.py) consumes exactly one subscript and computes a single (idx-1)*stride displacement. So TBL(I,J) resolves to the address of TBL(I) with J ignored, no error. After the structured-subscript change, all subscripts are carried in the JSON and >=2 subscripts raises loudly instead of truncating silently; this issue is to implement real multi-dimensional offset arithmetic (per-dimension strides from the nested-OCCURS layout). Needs the layout builder to expose per-dimension element sizes/strides. Net-new capability.
close_reason: Superseded/done by red-dragon-1wy3 (merged this session). Multi-dimensional OCCURS subscript offset arithmetic is implemented: EmitContext.resolve_field_ref (emit_context.py:271-) has a multi-D branch collecting per-dimension OCCURS strides via MaterialisedSectionedLayout.subscript_strides / DataLayout.all_enclosing_occurs_strides, accumulating sum((idx_k-1)*stride_k); a subscript count exceeding the field's OCCURS dimensions raises ValueError (no longer silently truncates or NotImplementedError). Bridge carries all subscripts structurally. Covered by TestTwoDimensionalSubscript (3 tests) in test_cobol_subscript_occurrence.py. Stale docstring at emit_context.py that still cited NotImplementedError/cqwx corrected in the same change.

title: VSAM dump/inspect CLI: copybook-driven field decode of a flat-file dataset image
description: A low-tech CLI to inspect a persisted VSAM flat-file dataset image (see VSAM file persistence, docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-09-vsam-file-persistence-design.md). The raw fixed-length-record file is EBCDIC + binary (zoned/packed/COMP) — a hex+cp037 dump is barely useful; the inspector must decode NAMED FIELDS via the record copybook, reusing the same COBOL layout + field decoders the runtime uses (single source of truth). DEFERRED: brainstorm heavily before building — open design questions: copybook resolution (path/dir/member), output shape (field=value lines vs table vs JSON), pure-Python field decoder reuse (expose a bytes+FieldLayout->value decoder in the COBOL layer if the only path today is the VM builtin), record-length from the layout, multi-record-type datasets, hex+cp037 fallback when no copybook. Tracked separately from the persistence sub-project.

title: HIGH-VALUES/LOW-VALUES MOVE stores EBCDIC-translated bytes instead of raw 0xFF/0x00, breaking STARTBR(HIGH-VALUES)+READPREV id-generation (blocks CardDemo TRANSACTION-ADD WRITE)
description: MOVE HIGH-VALUES TO <alphanumeric> fills the receiver with EBCDIC-translated bytes (the figurative fill char \xff goes through encode_alphanumeric -> ascii encode(errors=replace) -> EBCDIC, yielding 0x6F...) instead of the raw byte 0xFF; LOW-VALUES likewise should be raw 0x00. Repro: 'MOVE HIGH-VALUES TO TRAN-ID' (PIC X(16)) yields 6f4040...40 (one 0x6F then spaces) — both the replication-to-width and the raw-byte semantics are wrong for MOVE (VALUE HIGH-VALUES replicates but still encodes 0xFF->0x6F). Impact: CardDemo COTRN02C ADD-TRANSACTION generates the next TRAN-ID via MOVE HIGH-VALUES TO TRAN-ID; STARTBR-TRANSACT-FILE RIDFLD(TRAN-ID); READPREV. With 0x6F (below the seeded EBCDIC digit keys 0xF0..) STARTBR positions at start, READPREV returns ENDFILE, COTRN02C does MOVE ZEROS TO TRAN-ID + ADD 1 = the seed id, and EXEC CICS WRITE returns DUPREC -> 'Unable to Add Transaction'. Everything upstream now works (red-dragon-p7qe closed: CSUTLDTC lowers, date validation + CONFIRM='Y' pass). Fix: figurative HIGH-VALUES/LOW-VALUES must store raw 0xFF/0x00 bytes replicated to the receiver width, bypassing ASCII->EBCDIC translation of the fill char, in the COBOL MOVE pipeline (figurative_constants / lower_arithmetic / alphanumeric.encode_alphanumeric). NOTE: encode happens at the cobol-frontend/VM string->region boundary; confirm whether a raw-byte representation can be threaded without a core-VM edit. Blocks tests/integration/cics/test_carddemo_signon_real.py::test_real_carddemo_transaction_add_write (xfail strict, updated reason). Layer: cobol frontend figurative/MOVE + alphanumeric encode.
close_reason: Fixed: MOVE HIGH-VALUES/LOW-VALUES now store raw 0xFF/0x00 bytes filling the receiver width, bypassing ASCII->EBCDIC translation. Added raw_figurative_byte() + emit_fill_raw_byte(); lower_move short-circuits raw figurative sources. CardDemo COTRN02C STARTBR(HIGH-VALUES)+READPREV id-generation now works; test_real_carddemo_transaction_add_write passes end to end (xfail removed) — third real CardDemo flow (CREATE) complete.

title: OCCURS ... DEPENDING ON variable-length subordinate fields not materialised in COBOL data-division layout (blocks CSUTLDTC date validation)
description: CSUTLDTC.cbl (CardDemo) declares variable-length groups for the CEEDAYS date API: 01 WS-DATE-TO-TEST / 01 WS-DATE-FORMAT each have 02 Vstring-length PIC S9(4) BINARY and 02 Vstring-text containing 03 Vstring-char PIC X OCCURS 0 TO 256 TIMES DEPENDING ON Vstring-length (CSUTLDTC.cbl:25-39). build_sectioned_layout (interpreter/cobol/sectioned_layout.py / data_layout) materialises the enclosing GROUP names (WS-DATE-TO-TEST, WS-DATE-FORMAT appear in layout.groups) but NOT their subordinate fields Vstring-length / Vstring-text. So the first PROCEDURE statement 'MOVE LENGTH OF LS-DATE TO VSTRING-LENGTH OF WS-DATE-TO-TEST' (CSUTLDTC.cbl:105) raises KeyError: Field 'VSTRING-LENGTH' not found in any DATA DIVISION section (interpreter/cobol/sectioned_layout.py:57, via lower_move -> _store_move_value -> resolve_field_ref). Blocks the CardDemo TRANSACTION-ADD WRITE flow: tests/integration/cics/test_carddemo_signon_real.py::test_real_carddemo_transaction_add_write (xfail strict). Region subprogram linking + the CEEDAYS LE-service stub are DONE and green (test_region_subprogram_link.py, test_ceedays_stub.py); this layout gap is the remaining blocker. Layer: COBOL frontend data-division layout. Fix: materialise OCCURS-DEPENDING-ON subordinate fields (fixed-max or current-occurrence) so qualified/bare references resolve.
close_reason: Fixed: root cause was case-sensitive COBOL field/group lookup in data_layout (CSUTLDTC declares mixed-case Vstring-length/Vstring-char, referenced upper-cased; the OCCURS DEPENDING ON layout already materialised correctly). Made DataLayout.lookup/lookup_group/_enclosing_occurs_element_size case-insensitive (verbatim names preserved). Also added MOVE LENGTH OF source support (bridge length_of node + lower_move) and OF/IN qualified-name resolution for MOVE operands + EVALUATE subject (bridge emits operand qualifiers; layout.lookup_qualified resolves the leaf within the named ancestor group) so CSUTLDTC's two duplicate Vstring groups no longer collide. CSUTLDTC now lowers and date validation passes end to end. Unit + JAR-gated integration tests added; full suite 14165 passed, 0 failed.

title: ProLeap bridge mangles LENGTH OF operand name for qualified references in PERFORM VARYING FROM
description: serializeFromValue() in proleap-bridge StatementSerializer.java emitted the LENGTH OF special-register operand name via id.getText(), which for a qualified operand like 'LENGTH OF OPTIONI OF COMEN1AI' glues tokens into 'OPTIONIOFCOMEN1AI'. The frontend can't resolve that name and lower_perform._eval_varying_from logs 'LENGTH OF unknown field ... using 0', so the PERFORM VARYING starts at index 0. In CardDemo COMEN01C PROCESS-ENTER-KEY this broke option parsing (the loop scanning OPTIONI for the last non-space char), making every menu option XCTL to garbage. Was masked until the qualified ref-mod fix (red-dragon-cfj9) made OPTIONI(1:WS-IDX) actually slice. Fix: use leafDataName(id) (already used by serializeBasisCtx's length_of path) so the leaf name 'OPTIONI' is emitted. Layer: proleap bridge (Java).

title: ProLeap bridge drops reference-modification (n:m) on qualified data references (X OF Y)
description: getRefMod() in proleap-bridge StatementSerializer.java only found the referenceModifier on a direct TableCallContext or via a tableCall() reflective call. For a QUALIFIED data reference like 'TRNAMTI OF COTRN2AI(2:8)', ProLeap wraps the tableCall+referenceModifier deeper in a qualifiedDataName context, so getRefMod returned null and the (2:8) ref-mod was silently dropped from the emitted operand JSON. Effect: TRNAMTI OF COTRN2AI(2:8) decoded to the WHOLE field from offset 0, breaking COTRN02C VALIDATE-INPUT-DATA-FIELDS amount/date format checks (Amount should be in format -99999999.99) and blocking ADD-TRANSACTION. Fix: getRefMod falls back to a depth-first firstReferenceModifierDescendant subtree search (mirrors firstFunctionCallDescendant). Layer: proleap bridge (Java). Added fixture MOVE WS-SUB OF WS-GRP(2:8) + RefModSerializerTest.testRefMod_qualifiedNameKeepsRefMod.

title: parse_exec_cics_text fails on inline COBOL comment (*>) inside EXEC CICS block (COTRN02C)
description: COTRN02C SEND-TRNADD-SCREEN has a commented-out LENGTH option line inside the EXEC CICS RETURN block (a column-7 '*' comment). ProLeap surfaces it in exec_cics_text as a free-format '*>' inline comment, so the collected text is 'EXEC CICS RETURN TRANSID (WS-TRANID) COMMAREA (CARDDEMO-COMMAREA) *>  LENGTH(LENGTH OF CARDDEMO-COMMAREA) END-EXEC'. The Lark CICS grammar cannot parse '*>' and parse_exec_cics_text raises ValueError. Fix: strip '*>'..end-of-line inline comments from the exec text before parsing in interpreter/cics/cics_parser.py (comments inside an EXEC block carry no semantics). Layer: cics_parser.

title: COACTUPC update-confirm blocked: unimplemented NUMVAL/NUMVAL-C/TEST-NUMVAL/TEST-NUMVAL-C/LENGTH/INTEGER-OF-DATE intrinsics
description: Follow-on to red-dragon-ge72 (now fixed). With function operands working in IF relations, COACTUPC 1205-COMPARE-OLD-NEW correctly detects field changes (verified: UPPER-CASE('N')!=UPPER-CASE('Y'), FUNCTION TRIM works). But the PF05-confirm -> 9600-WRITE-PROCESSING -> REWRITE path is blocked: 1200-EDIT-MAP-INPUTS field validation (1250-EDIT-SIGNED-9V2, EDIT-DATE-CCYYMMDD, 1100-RECEIVE-MAP credit-limit checks ~cbl 1078-1136) call FUNCTION NUMVAL / NUMVAL-C / TEST-NUMVAL / TEST-NUMVAL-C / LENGTH / INTEGER-OF-DATE. lower_function_operand stubs these to their first argument (logs 'Unsupported COBOL intrinsic FUNCTION'), so a signed-number/date validation spuriously sets INPUT-ERROR. 2000-DECIDE-ACTION WHEN ACUP-SHOW-DETAILS then sees INPUT-ERROR and stays in SHOW-DETAILS instead of ACUP-CHANGES-OK-NOT-CONFIRMED; PF05 never reaches REWRITE; ACCTDAT status stays 'Y'. FIX: add byte_builtins for NUMVAL (strip non-numeric, parse to number), NUMVAL-C (currency-aware), TEST-NUMVAL/TEST-NUMVAL-C (return 0 when valid numeric else error offset), LENGTH (string length), INTEGER-OF-DATE (CCYYMMDD->integer days); register in BuiltinName/BYTE_BUILTINS and map in lower_arithmetic._INTRINSIC_FUNCTIONS. SECONDARY: STRING sending source (StatementSerializer ~line 897 serializeMoveOperand) and MOVE-source drop FUNCTION nodes -> route through serializeFunctionNode so 'X must be supplied' error message renders the field name not bare 'TRIM'. Gated by tests/integration/cics/test_carddemo_signon_real.py::test_real_carddemo_account_update_rewrite (xfail strict). Layers: interpreter/cobol/byte_builtins.py, lower_arithmetic.py, proleap-bridge.
close_reason: Closed

title: ProLeap bridge: intrinsic FUNCTION operands dropped inside IF relational conditions
description: BLOCKER for CardDemo COACTUPC account-update REWRITE. Inside an IF relation, FUNCTION UPPER-CASE(A) = FUNCTION UPPER-CASE(B) is emitted by the Java bridge as {'left':{'kind':'ref','name':'UPPER-CASE'}, 'op':'==', 'right':{'kind':'ref','name':'UPPER-CASE'}} — the function call AND its arguments are DROPPED, leaving only the function NAME as a bare ref. Both sides then lower to the literal 'UPPER-CASE' and compare EQUAL regardless of operands. Repro: IF FUNCTION UPPER-CASE(A)=FUNCTION UPPER-CASE(B) with A='N' B='Y' takes the TRUE branch. Impact: COACTUPC 1205-COMPARE-OLD-NEW (40-term compound using UPPER-CASE/TRIM) never detects a field change, so Turn C stays in SHOW-DETAILS, PF05 confirm path (9600-WRITE-PROCESSING) is unreachable, and no REWRITE occurs. FUNCTION TRIM is also entirely unsupported (lower_arithmetic warns + falls back to first arg). FIX REQUIRES: Java bridge to emit function-call operands (with args) in condition relations — same FunctionCallOperand it already produces for MOVE/arithmetic — then condition_lowering._lower_expr_dict/_lower_relation_operand handles a 'function' kind via lower_function_operand. Bridge change + JAR rebuild needed; out of scope for a pure Python in-layer fix. Also implement FUNCTION TRIM. Files: proleap-bridge (Java), interpreter/cobol/condition_lowering.py, lower_arithmetic.py. Next loop input for the account-update flow.
close_reason: Fixed in ca08ab15: bridge now serializes intrinsic FUNCTION operands (incl. nested) in IF relations as structured function nodes; condition_lowering + cobol_expression lower them; FUNCTION TRIM implemented. Verified: UPPER-CASE/TRIM relation operands compare correctly; COACTUPC 1205 change detection works. Follow-on gap red-dragon-zuhj (NUMVAL/etc validation intrinsics) tracks the remaining REWRITE blocker.

title: COBOL: group byte length summed instead of placed-extent, overlaps next 01 (CardDemo WS-MISC overlapped WS-LITERALS)
description: _compute_group_length summed children's PARSER-computed lengths, which over-reach when a child's PIC is sized differently than the real compiler (e.g. edited PIC +ZZZ,ZZZ,ZZ9.99). In CardDemo COACTUPC, CICS-OUTPUT-EDIT-VARS over-counted by 9 bytes, making WS-MISC-STORAGE (offset 85, computed 2192) overlap WS-LITERALS@2268 by 9. INITIALIZE WS-MISC-STORAGE then sprayed spaces over LIT-THISPGM@2268, so MOVE LIT-THISPGM TO PGMNAMEO rendered blank PGMNAME. FIXED: group total_bytes now derived from children's PLACED extents (compiler-assigned offsets), each child clamped to the next non-REDEFINES sibling's offset. File: interpreter/cobol/data_layout.py (_placed_child_extent). Test: tests/unit/test_data_layout.py::TestGroupLengthFromPlacedExtent.
close_reason: Fix landed in data_layout.py (_placed_child_extent); 36 unit tests pass.

title: COBOL: level-88 figurative VALUE (LOW-VALUES/SPACES) written/compared as literal text
description: An 88-level whose VALUE is a figurative constant (e.g. ACUP-DETAILS-NOT-FETCHED VALUES LOW-VALUES, SPACES in CardDemo COACTUPC) was mis-handled on BOTH sides: SET <88> TO TRUE wrote the literal text 'LOW-VALUES' (truncated to 'L') into the X(1) parent, and IF <88> compared the parent against the text 'LOW-VALUES' instead of the figurative fill char. Result: the first-display EVALUATE branch never matched and fell into WHEN OTHER (RECEIVE timeout). FIXED: figurative expansion sized to parent byte_length on write (lower_arithmetic._set_condition_name) and read (condition_lowering._emit_88_value_reg). Test: tests/integration/test_cobol_e2e_features.py::TestLevel88FigurativeValues.
close_reason: Fix landed in lower_arithmetic._set_condition_name + condition_lowering._emit_88_value_reg; TestLevel88FigurativeValues passes.

title: COBOL: OCCURS-group leaf subscript strides by leaf width, not group element_size
description: Subscripting a leaf nested inside an OCCURS group (e.g. CardDemo COMEN02Y CDEMO-MENU-OPT-PGMNAME(WS-OPTION)) computed the offset using the leaf field's own byte_length as stride instead of the enclosing OCCURS element_size. For option 2, PGMNAME resolved to offset 45 ('U02Accou') instead of 83 ('COACTUPC'). FIXED: DataLayout.enclosing_occurs_element_size + MaterialisedSectionedLayout.subscript_stride; emit_context.resolve_field_ref now strides by the enclosing OCCURS element_size and accesses the leaf's own width. File: interpreter/cobol/data_layout.py, sectioned_layout.py, emit_context.py. Test: tests/unit/test_data_layout.py::TestEnclosingOccursElementSize. Found driving CardDemo COACTUPC menu option '02' XCTL.
close_reason: Fixed (data_layout.enclosing_occurs_element_size + sectioned_layout.subscript_stride/strides + emit_context stride by enclosing OCCURS element_size); unit TestEnclosingOccursElementSize + full data_layout suite (36) green. Added execution-level integration regression TestNestedLeafSubscriptStride (commit 64b9ab47, CardDemo COMEN02Y shape) — full suite green. Was stale-open; verified fixed end-to-end.

title: IF/EVALUATE level-88 with digit-character VALUE on PIC X parent never matched (read-side of 0sq2)
description: EXEC CICS COACTVWC turn-5 account-view: FOUND-ACCT-IN-MASTER / FOUND-CUST-IN-MASTER are level-88 conditions on PIC X(1) flags (WS-ACCOUNT-MASTER-READ-FLAG / WS-CUST-MASTER-READ-FLAG) whose 88 VALUE is the digit character '1'. SET <88> TO TRUE correctly wrote the character '1' (red-dragon-0sq2). But the READ side in interpreter/cobol/condition_lowering.py::_emit_single_value_test decoded the PIC X parent to the STRING '1' and compared it against ctx.parse_literal(cv.from_val), which coerced the digit-string 88 VALUE '1' to the INTEGER 1. The resulting compare ('1' == 1, str vs int) was always False, so IF FOUND-ACCT-IN-MASTER OR FOUND-CUST-IN-MASTER never fired and the gated output MOVEs (MOVE ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS TO ACSTTUSO, customer name MOVEs) were skipped — ACSTTUS/ACSFNAM/ACSLNAM rendered empty even though all three chained VSAM reads returned NORMAL and decoded their records correctly. Fix: _emit_88_value_reg() emits the 88 VALUE as a quoted string literal when the parent field category is ALPHANUMERIC (character), matching the decoded character form; numeric parents still parse normally. Covers THRU range bounds too. Test: tests/integration/test_cobol_programs.py::TestSetConditionNameToTrue::test_if_88_with_digit_char_value_on_pic_x_parent_is_true. Completes the CardDemo account-view end-to-end flow (durable test turn 5 now passes, xfail removed).

title: COBOL: FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE produces garbled date/time
description: FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE followed by reference-modification slicing (e.g. WS-CURDATE-YEAR(3:2)) yields nonsense values (observed CURDATE='55/30/49', CURTIME='41:35:00'). Either the intrinsic's output format/encoding or the ref-mod slicing over it is wrong. Cosmetic for sign-on but incorrect; general COBOL gap.
acceptance_criteria: FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE returns the standard 21-char format; ref-mod slices yield correct YYYY/MM/DD HH:MM:SS components.
close_reason: Duplicate root cause: folded into red-dragon-8eiq (intrinsic FUNCTION support). CURRENT-DATE garbling is the same missing-intrinsic mechanism as UPPER-CASE; track both there.

title: COBOL: integer fields (PIC 9(n), 0 decimals) decode/arithmetic to Python float — '7.0' instead of 7
description:
COBOL operations on integer fields (PIC 9(n) / S9(n) with zero decimal places) carry the value as a Python float, not an int. E.g. for '77 WX PIC 9(4) VALUE 5.', after 'ADD 1 TO WX' the decoded/computed value is 6.0 and 'MOVE WX TO LK-OUT' stringifies it via the str builtin to '6.0' (observed in VM logs). It currently round-trips correctly only because COBOL_PREPARE_DIGITS tolerates the trailing '.0' when decimal_digits=0.

Two distinct smells:
1. Float for an integer field. emit_decode_field + Binop (lower_arithmetic.py) produce float; emit_to_string -> str(6.0) -> '6.0'. An integer PIC field should carry int and stringify to '6'. This is a specific, easily-reproduced manifestation of the broader red-dragon-4q25.1 (all COBOL arithmetic uses Python float).
2. Spurious warning: _unwrap_builtin_result (interpreter/handlers/calls.py:65-75) flags the normal str-builtin result as a 'bare heap address' because _heap_addr (interpreter/vm/vm.py:338-339) returns Address(val) for ANY non-empty string, so _heap_addr('6.0') is truthy. Misleading log noise; the guard should only fire for address-shaped strings (obj_/arr_/mem_/rgn_ prefixes).
acceptance_criteria: Integer COBOL fields (decimal_digits=0) decode and compute as int, not float: str(value) yields '7' not '7.0' in the encode path. _unwrap_builtin_result no longer emits a 'bare heap address' warning for plain numeric strings returned by the str builtin (tighten the _heap_addr-based guard to known address prefixes). Add a regression test asserting no float contamination for an integer-field MOVE/ADD round-trip.
notes: Discovered 2026-06-06 while verifying red-dragon-4q25.34 (LOCAL-STORAGE). Related: red-dragon-4q25.1 (P0, broad float arithmetic). The '7.0' string sent an earlier debug session chasing a phantom copy-back bug (which turned out to be max_steps exhaustion).
close_reason: Implemented + verified on branch fix/cobol-integer-int-decode. Integer fields (decimal_digits==0) in all four decoders (zoned, zoned-separate, comp3, binary) now decode to int, not float. 18-digit PIC 9(18) MOVE round-trips exactly (was corrupting to 1 via float->scientific-notation->prepare_digits). _unwrap_builtin_result no longer emits spurious bare-heap-address warnings for plain numeric strings. Commits f4925a2c, 27d6d2aa, f8ddcff2, f56df17b. 299 tests pass. Decimal precision (4q25.1) and ROUNDED (4q25.4/.30) remain out of scope.

title: Lever 2: parametrize copy-paste test families (preserve @covers)
description:
Highest line savings (~6,000 lines) but most care needed (@covers preservation). Only 4 of 388 files use @pytest.mark.parametrize despite ~6,862 hand-written test functions, many in copy-paste families differing only in literal inputs/expected values.

Targets (from frontend + COBOL + type-inference audits):
- Per-language tree-sitter frontend unit files (java/csharp/kotlin/php/pascal/ruby/javascript): ~200-235 functions collapsible, ~2,000-2,450 lines. Highest-value, lowest-risk same-member families first: Java+Kotlin GenericTypeSeeding (13->2), PHP include/require (8->1-2), Kotlin AnonymousFunction (7->2-3), Pascal binary+bitwise operators (6->2), Ruby modifier classes (~11->4-5, parametrize WITHIN each class since 4 distinct members).
- COBOL (test_cobol_programs.py / test_cobol_e2e.py / test_cobol_frontend.py): ~130 functions, ~3,500 lines. Biggest: ON_SIZE_ERROR (14->1), ref-mod families (~33->6), e2e TestNumericValueVerification (11->1). Keep e2e (JAR-free layer) — slim, don't merge into integration. Drop ~3 misplaced IR-shape tests from e2e (covered by frontend).
- tests/integration/test_type_inference.py: ~29 near-identical single-language *ReturnType/*FuncSignatures classes -> parametrized SOURCES-dict style (matching existing TestBinopIntPlusInt), ~300-400 lines. No unit/integration merge (unit file has 0 @covers; merging would risk the 6 @covers in integration).

CRITICAL @covers handling:
- Coverage is a static set-scan of @covers members. Collapsing N tests that share ONE member into a parametrized test loses nothing if the decorator is retained.
- Mixed-member families MUST stack the union: Java integer literals (HEX/OCTAL/BINARY + INTEGER_LITERALS), Ruby modifiers (IF/UNLESS/WHILE/UNTIL_MODIFIER), PHP Interface/Trait/Enum (distinct members). Either parametrize within a single member or stack all @covers.
- Run feature_coverage_audit.py after every file; it must stay at 0 uncovered for all 15 languages.

Do file-by-file with the coverage audit + full suite as gates. True deletes are rare; this is mechanical count/line reduction preserving coverage.
acceptance_criteria: 1. High-value same-member families parametrized first (GenericTypeSeeding, PHP include/require, ON_SIZE_ERROR, type_inference per-language classes). 2. Mixed-member families either parametrized within a member or stack the union of @covers. 3. feature_coverage_audit.py shows 0 uncovered for all 15 languages after each file. 4. Full suite green after each file. 5. No distinct edge case deleted; e2e JAR-free layer preserved (slimmed, not merged). 6. Net reduction reported (functions + lines per file).

title: Lever 3: hoist rosetta/exercism boilerplate into shared base class
description:
~180 byte-identical test-method bodies repeated across ~45 files in tests/unit/rosetta/ (30 files) and tests/unit/exercism/ (20 files), which already share a conftest.py.

Duplicated boilerplate methods (body counts rosetta/exercism):
- test_zero_llm_calls — 27/18 — identical 5-line body (assert stats.llm_calls == 0)
- test_correct_result — 26/18 — near-identical assert on execution_result
- test_all_languages_covered — 24/18 — identical (assert set(PROGRAMS.keys()) == set(SUPPORTED_DETERMINISTIC_LANGUAGES))
- test_cross_language_consistency — 25/18 — identical (assert_cross_language_consistency(...))
- test_clean_lowering — 28/18 — calls shared assert_clean_lowering(...)

These are NOT false positives (truly duplicated bodies) and must NOT be deleted — they assert real per-language behavior via parametrized fixtures. Fix is structural: hoist the 4-5 boilerplate methods into a shared mixin/base class in the existing conftest.py (rosetta/conftest.py already has assert_clean_lowering / assert_cross_language_consistency helpers — extend that). Per-file data (PROGRAMS, REQUIRED_OPCODES) stays local; each test class inherits the base and supplies the data.

CRITICAL: preserve every @covers(...) decorator and the parametrization. The coverage audit is a static scan — moving a method to a base class must keep the @covers members discoverable (verify the audit still counts them; if base-class methods are not scanned, keep thin per-file overrides or ensure the audit sees inherited @covers). Validate feature_coverage_audit.py stays at 0 uncovered before/after.

A change to the LLM-call or consistency assertion currently touches 45 files.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The 4-5 boilerplate methods hoisted into a shared base/mixin in tests/unit/rosetta/conftest.py (and exercism equivalent). 2. Per-file classes inherit and supply only their data (PROGRAMS/REQUIRED_OPCODES). 3. @covers coverage preserved — feature_coverage_audit.py shows 0 uncovered for all 15 languages (verify the audit scans inherited/base @covers; adjust approach if not). 4. Full suite green; same test count behavior (parametrization preserved). 5. No per-language behavior assertion changed.
notes:
DEEP ANALYSIS (2026-06-05): ZERO COVERAGE RISK confirmed. feature_coverage_audit.py (scan_test_dirs, line 165) only rglobs test_*.py — conftest.py is NEVER scanned — AND the rosetta/exercism boilerplate methods carry NO @covers decorators at all (verified in test_rosetta_bubble_sort.py). So moving them anywhere cannot lose coverage. CORRECTION to initial audit: only THREE methods are truly identical boilerplate — test_zero_llm_calls (pure, depends only on the execution_result fixture), test_all_languages_covered, test_cross_language_consistency. test_correct_result is FILE-SPECIFIC (different expected values/extraction per algorithm) — do NOT hoist it. WIRING CONSTRAINT: each file's 3 fixtures (language_ir/all_results/execution_result) are @pytest.fixture(params=sorted(PROGRAMS.keys())) evaluated at class-body time against module-level PROGRAMS; methods also read module-level REQUIRED_OPCODES/MIN_INSTRUCTIONS. Cleanest safe refactor: (a) hoist test_zero_llm_calls into a mixin (depends only on execution_result fixture — trivial, removes ~45 copies); (b) to hoist test_all_languages_covered + test_cross_language_consistency, convert per-file PROGRAMS/REQUIRED_OPCODES to class attributes so mixin methods read self.X (or pass via fixture). Keep fixtures per-file (params eval at class-body time) unless using pytest_generate_tests indirection (more invasive — defer). exercism/ mirrors rosetta/.
REFINED ANALYSIS (2026-06-05): the boilerplate is LESS uniform than first thought. test_zero_llm_calls (45 copies) varies: most use fixture execution_result -> (lang,_vm,stats); exercism uses a 5-tuple (lang,_vm,stats,_expected,desc); some files use differently-NAMED fixtures (scoping_result, leaky_result). A mixin method must read stats positionally (result[2], length-agnostic) AND every consuming class must name its fixture execution_result — the outliers (scoping_result/leaky_result, and any non-execution_result fixture) need renaming or exclusion. So Lever 3 is materially more invasive than Lever 1 (touches pytest fixture/class-inheritance semantics across ~45 files with per-file variation), for a modest line saving (~135 lines for this one method). Recommend a dedicated plan + subagent-driven execution with review, scoped to the uniform-fixture subset first, NOT an ad-hoc sweep. Zero coverage risk still holds (no @covers on these methods; conftest not scanned by the audit).

title: VM: LoadRegion out-of-bounds read returns zero-padded bytes
description:
In standard COBOL, CALL USING passes raw memory by position. If the callee's LINKAGE field is declared larger than the caller's USING argument (size mismatch), the callee reads bytes beyond what the caller wrote. Real COBOL runtimes return whatever happens to be in memory; our interpreter should return zeroes for the overrun portion (safe, predictable, matches OS zero-page semantics).

Currently the LoadRegion handler in interpreter/vm/executor.py (or wherever LoadRegion is dispatched) raises an index error if offset + length exceeds the region's allocated size.

**Fix:** In the LoadRegion execution handler, clamp the actual read to min(length, region_size - offset). For any bytes beyond the region end, append zero bytes to the result. Total returned bytes = length (zero-padded to requested size).

This is a VM-level invariant: AllocRegion zero-initialises (all bytes start as 0x00). LoadRegion out-of-bounds reads return 0x00 for the overrun.

This handles the case where LINKAGE SECTION fields are wider than the USING argument passed by the caller — consistent with COBOL 'undefined but safe' semantics.
acceptance_criteria:
1. LoadRegion on a region of size N with offset=0, length=N+4 returns N real bytes followed by 4 zero bytes (no exception).
2. Unit test in tests/unit/ for the LoadRegion handler covering the padding case.
3. Existing LoadRegion tests remain green (in-bounds reads unchanged).

title: COBOL: CALL USING BY VALUE / BY CONTENT — no copy-back after CallWithMemory
description:
Once CALL USING BY REFERENCE is implemented (red-dragon-q7r1), BY VALUE and BY CONTENT need a small additional change in lower_call.py.

BY VALUE (param_type == 'VALUE') and BY CONTENT (param_type == 'CONTENT') both pass a copy to the callee. The callee cannot modify the caller's storage. The only difference from BY REFERENCE is: skip the copy-back step (step 5 in red-dragon-q7r1).

Standard COBOL semantics: BY CONTENT is identical to BY VALUE at the machine level — a copy is made, the callee operates on the copy, and no write-back occurs.

**Change in lower_call.py:** In the copy-back loop (post-CallWithMemory), only emit WriteRegion for params where param_type == 'REFERENCE'. Params with param_type in ('VALUE', 'CONTENT') are skipped.

This is a one-line conditional around the existing copy-back emission from red-dragon-q7r1.
acceptance_criteria:
1. CALL 'SUBPROG' USING BY VALUE WS-A: callee modifies its LINKAGE copy; caller's WS-A is unchanged after return.
2. Same for BY CONTENT.
3. Unit test: lower_call emits no copy-back WriteRegion for BY VALUE / BY CONTENT params.
4. Integration test: SUBPROG doubles a BY VALUE field; caller verifies WS-A is unchanged.
5. @covers(CobolFeature.CALL_USING_BY_VALUE) or equivalent feature enum member.

title: WORKING-STORAGE not persistent across subprogram calls
description:
WORKING-STORAGE SECTION is re-allocated on every call to a COBOL subprogram, wiping its state. Real COBOL semantics require WS to persist across calls (allocated once at program load, not re-initialised on re-entry).

This is a pre-existing gap not introduced by the LINKAGE/LOCAL-STORAGE implementation. It means:
- Subprograms that accumulate state in WS across multiple calls will not work correctly
- Recursive subprograms that mutate WS will lose state on re-entry

The root cause is that AllocRegion for WS is emitted at the callee's entry label, which re-executes on every call. The fix requires a separate program-load phase that allocates WS regions once, independent of the call entry path.

Related spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-cobol-linkage-local-storage-design.md (Known Limitations section)

comments.author: avishek-sen-gupta
comments.text: Already fixed by the singleton init architecture (commits 55510b0c / 201d12ff). WS is now allocated once in the program-load init block (lower_program_init.py:68), stored in the singleton ws_handle field; the procedure division only loads the handle via lower_ws_from_singleton and never re-allocates. Verified by test_ws_counter_survives_two_calls — SUBPROG accumulates WS-COUNTER to 2 across two calls, which would read 1 if WS were re-allocated per call.

title: C frontend: Implement ternary operator
description: Implement the ternary operator (conditional_expression) for the C language frontend to close the TERNARY_OPERATOR coverage gap.
close_reason: Implemented ternary operator test coverage

title: STRING/UNSTRING destination ref_mod: INTO WS-DEST(start:length) partial write not supported
description: COBOL allows reference modification on the destination of STRING/UNSTRING: STRING x DELIMITED BY SIZE INTO WS-DEST(2:5). Currently the bridge serializes the INTO target as a plain string, and lower_string.py writes the full result field. A partial destination write requires: (1) Java bridge: detect ref_mod on INTO target in serializeString/serializeUnstring; (2) Python AST: update StringStatement/UnstringStatement destination model to RefModOperand; (3) lower_string.py / lower_unstring.py: when destination has ref_mod, write only into the slice bytes of the target field. Lower priority than MOVE target ref_mod (red-dragon-q0gr) and COMPUTE ref_mod (red-dragon-59y3) as STRING/UNSTRING destination ref_mod is less common in practice. Files: proleap-bridge/src/main/java/org/reddragon/bridge/StatementSerializer.java, interpreter/cobol/cobol_statements.py (StringStatement, UnstringStatement), interpreter/cobol/lower_string.py.

title: Delete fallback coercion in apply_update — red-dragon-132/rrb cleanup not completed
description:
Both red-dragon-132 (migrate all handlers to TypedValue) and red-dragon-rrb (simplify apply_update) are marked CLOSED, but the defensive fallback coercion block in apply_update (interpreter/vm/vm.py ~lines 244-252) is still present.

The block:
  # Register writes — coerce to declared type if needed
  for reg, val in update.register_writes.items():
      tv = val
      declared = type_env.register_types.get(reg, UNKNOWN)
      if declared and tv.type != declared:
          coerced = _coerce_value(tv.value, reg, type_env, conversion_rules)
          frame.registers[reg] = typed(coerced, declared)
      else:
          frame.registers[reg] = tv

This was documented as a 'transition measure' while callers still passed raw values. If red-dragon-132 is genuinely complete, all register_writes values should already be TypedValue with correct types, making this coercion redundant.

Steps:
1. Audit all callers of apply_update (direct + via StateUpdate.register_writes) to confirm every write site produces a correctly-typed TypedValue.
2. If confirmed, delete the fallback coercion branch and replace with a direct store: frame.registers[reg] = val
3. Add a type assertion or pyright annotation to enforce TypedValue at the write site.
4. Run full test suite to verify no regressions.

title: COBOL ref_mod: arithmetic source operands — WS-FIELD(start:length) in ADD/SUBTRACT/MULTIPLY/DIVIDE/COMPUTE
description: Reference modification on arithmetic source operands is not implemented. COBOL allows ADD WS-FIELD(1:3) TO WS-TOTAL or COMPUTE WS-RESULT = WS-SRC(2:4) + 1 where the substring is treated as a numeric value. The Java bridge arithmetic serialization methods do not call serializeRefMod(). The Python ArithmeticStatement operand lists hold plain strings. The lowering reads the full field value without extracting the specified substring first.
acceptance_criteria: Java bridge serializes ref_mod on arithmetic source operands. ArithmeticStatement operands gain ref_mod_start/ref_mod_length. Lowering calls __string_slice before using the value in BINOP. Integration test: ADD WS-FIELD(1:3) TO WS-TOTAL where WS-FIELD contains '123ABC', assert WS-TOTAL incremented by 123.
close_reason: Implemented: arithmetic source ref_mod for ADD/SUBTRACT/MULTIPLY/DIVIDE completed alongside COMPUTE ref_mod. Tests in TestArithmeticRefMod passing.

title: COBOL ref_mod: DISPLAY items — WS-FIELD(start:length) in DISPLAY statement

acceptance_criteria: Java bridge serializes ref_mod on each DISPLAY item. DisplayStatement items gain ref_mod fields. Lowering calls __string_slice when ref_mod is present before the display operation. Integration test: DISPLAY WS-FIELD(1:3), assert output matches the substring.

The Java bridge serializeInspect() uses extractCallName() for the source, which does not capture ref_mod. The Python InspectStatement.source is a plain str. The lowering inspects the full field without slicing first.

Lowering calls __string_slice on the source when ref_mod is present. Integration test: INSPECT WS-FIELD(2:5) TALLYING WS-COUNT FOR ALL 'A', assert count matches substring only.

title: COBOL ref_mod: UNSTRING source — WS-FIELD(start:length) as subject of UNSTRING statement
description: Reference modification on the UNSTRING source field is not implemented. COBOL allows UNSTRING WS-FIELD(start:length) DELIMITED ... INTO .... The Java bridge serializeUnstring() uses extractCallName() for the source, which does not capture ref_mod. The Python UnstringStatement.source is a plain str with no ref_mod fields. The lowering reads the full field value without applying substring extraction first.

Lowering calls __string_slice on the source before splitting. Integration test: UNSTRING WS-SRC(3:4) DELIMITED ',' INTO WS-A WS-B, assert correct splits.

title: COBOL ref_mod: STRING sendings — WS-FIELD(start:length) as source operand in STRING statement
description: Reference modification on STRING statement sending operands is not implemented. COBOL allows WS-FIELD(start:length) as a source in STRING ... DELIMITED ... INTO .... The Java bridge serializeString() uses extractValueStmtText() and does not call serializeRefMod(). The Python StringSending dataclass has no ref_mod fields. The lowering in lower_string.py does not extract substrings before concatenating.
acceptance_criteria: Java bridge serializes ref_mod on each sending operand. StringSending dataclass gains ref_mod_start/ref_mod_length fields. Lowering calls __string_slice when ref_mod is present. Integration test: STRING WS-SRC(2:3) DELIMITED SIZE INTO WS-DST, assert correct bytes.
close_reason: Closed

title: Leaky Execution Loop: run.py imperatively patches stack frames after StateUpdate apply
description:
## Summary

The \"pure handler → StateUpdate → apply_update\" abstraction leaks because `StackFramePush` is structurally incomplete at handler time. After `apply_update` creates a new stack frame, `run.py` must imperatively patch three fields that only the call site knows.

## Evidence

`interpreter/run.py:249-268` — `_handle_call_dispatch_setup`:

```python
apply_update(vm, update, type_env=type_env, conversion_rules=conversion_rules)

new_frame = vm.current_frame
new_frame.return_label = call_return_label   # patched after the fact
new_frame.return_ip = call_return_ip         # patched after the fact
new_frame.result_reg = call_result_reg       # patched after the fact
```

The frame is in a half-initialized state between `apply_update` (line 263) and the final patch (line 268). Nothing in the `StateUpdate` contract enforces or documents this two-step initialization.

## Root Cause

Call handlers (in `interpreter/handlers/`) emit a `StackFramePush` with `return_label=None`, `return_ip` unset, `result_reg` unset — they cannot know the call-site's instruction pointer or result register. Only `run.py` has that context. The abstraction boundary is therefore wrong: the caller must reach inside the VM to complete what `apply_update` left incomplete.

## Affected Files

- `interpreter/run.py` — patch site at lines 249-268
- `interpreter/vm/vm_types.py` — `StateUpdate`, `StackFramePush` schema
- `interpreter/vm/vm.py` — `apply_update` (creates incomplete frame)
- `interpreter/handlers/` — call handlers returning incomplete `StackFramePush`

## Impact

- Frame is transiently invalid (half-initialized) between `apply_update` and the imperative patch — any code that runs in that window sees broken state.
- The "pure handler" design principle is silently violated; reviewers cannot tell from the handler contract that callers must patch the frame.
- Makes it harder to reason about or test the call-dispatch path in isolation.
design:
## Chosen Approach: Option B — Augment before apply

Complete the StackFramePush *before* calling apply_update, so the frame is fully initialized on creation and the VM never sees half-initialized state.

### Changes (3 files)

1. interpreter/vm/vm_types.py — add return_ip: int | None = None and result_reg: Register = NO_REGISTER to StackFramePush

2. interpreter/vm/vm.py — pass return_ip and result_reg from StackFramePush into the StackFrame constructor in apply_update (lines 265-277)

3. interpreter/run.py — rewrite _handle_call_dispatch_setup to augment the update before applying:
   - model_copy the call_push with return_label=current_label, return_ip=ip+1, result_reg=instruction.result_reg
   - call apply_update with the augmented update
   - remove the three post-patch lines (265-268)

Both call sites (run.py:424, run.py:577) are unchanged. Handler contract is unchanged. apply_update remains the single atomic state transition.

title: COMPUTE ON SIZE ERROR: overflow detection not implemented
description: COMPUTE statement has no ON SIZE ERROR / NOT ON SIZE ERROR support. The lower_compute function in interpreter/cobol/lower_arithmetic.py emits a Binop directly without any overflow check or clause handling. Design: same three-label pattern as ArithmeticStatement (ADD/SUBTRACT/MULTIPLY/DIVIDE), but COMPUTE expressions can involve complex nested arithmetic — the overflow check must be applied to the final result register against the target field's type descriptor. StatementSerializer.java serializeCompute() also needs onSizeErrorPhrase/notOnSizeErrorPhrase serialization. Out of scope: red-dragon-4q25.5 (which covers ArithmeticStatement only). Related: red-dragon-4q25.5 (implemented pattern to follow).
close_reason: Implemented: ON SIZE ERROR for COMPUTE statement in lower_compute, with overflow detection and branching. Integration tests in TestComputeOnSizeError all passing.

title: COMPUTE ON SIZE ERROR not implemented
description: lower_compute in interpreter/cobol/lower_arithmetic.py has no overflow detection. COMPUTE target(s) = expr should support ON SIZE ERROR / NOT ON SIZE ERROR clauses per COBOL standard. The ProLeap ASG ComputeStatement exposes getOnSizeErrorPhrase()/getNotOnSizeErrorPhrase() but StatementSerializer does not serialize them and ComputeStatement dataclass has no on_size_error/not_on_size_error fields. Separate from red-dragon-4q25.5 which covers ADD/SUBTRACT/MULTIPLY/DIVIDE only. Implementation requires: (1) Java bridge serialization of ON SIZE ERROR phrases for COMPUTE, (2) ComputeStatement dataclass fields, (3) lower_compute restructure with emit_overflow_check calls.
close_reason: Duplicate of red-dragon-zdac. COMPUTE ON SIZE ERROR is implemented — closes with zdac.

title: Test assertion mismatch: test_caller_callee_chain_has_nested_nodes in test_dataflow_graph_panel.py
description: Test name claims: caller-callee chain has nested subtree structure (double calls add, so add should appear as a child of double). Assertions actually check: any(len(n.children) > 0 for n in nodes). Only verifies that at least one node has children, but does not verify the nesting represents the correct call structure (double → add) or that add specifically appears as a child. File: tests/unit/test_dataflow_graph_panel.py:182

title: Test assertion mismatch: test_multi_function_finds_outermost_call in test_dataflow_graph_panel.py
description: Test name claims: finds the outermost call in a multi-function scenario (double calls add; double is outermost). Assertions actually check: len(top_calls) >= 1 and any('double' in c.callee_label for c in top_calls). Does not verify that inner call (add) is excluded from top_calls, so the test would pass even if both double and add were returned. File: tests/unit/test_dataflow_graph_panel.py:131

title: Test assertion mismatch: test_function_source_span_computation in test_dataflow_tui.py
description: Test name claims: verifies function block prefix matching finds the right source lines. Assertions actually check: len(func_labels) >= 1 and max_line > 0. Only checks that some source line was found (max_line > 0), not that the span matches expected line numbers or that min_line is in the right range. File: tests/integration/test_dataflow_tui.py:108

title: COBOL: VALUE clause on REDEFINES field — initialization correctness untested
description: AC4 of red-dragon-4q25.9: a VALUE clause on a REDEFINES field should initialize the shared bytes according to the redefining field's VALUE. Current initialization iterates all_leaves(); it is unclear whether REDEFINES fields are included or skipped, and there is no integration test that reads the initialized bytes through the redefining field.
acceptance_criteria: An integration test declares WS-BASE PIC X(4) VALUE 'ABCD' and WS-NUM REDEFINES WS-BASE PIC 9(4). At startup (no MOVE), reading WS-BASE gives 'ABCD'. If WS-NUM has its own VALUE, reading WS-NUM gives that value.
close_reason: Closed

title: COBOL: PIC 9 field with no VALUE clause not tested for zero-fill at startup
description: AC3 of red-dragon-4q25.9: a PIC 9(3) field with no VALUE clause should contain 000 at program start (COBOL default zero-fill). No integration test verifies this default initialization path.
acceptance_criteria: An integration test declares WS-N PIC 9(3) with no VALUE clause, reads from the region without MOVE, and asserts the zoned-decimal bytes are all 0xF0 (zero).
close_reason: Closed

title: COBOL: VALUE clause on group item — child field initialization untested

The lower_data_division.py iterates all_leaves() which should cover this, but there is no integration test that reads multiple group-child fields at startup without any MOVE.
acceptance_criteria: An integration test declares a 01-level group with 05-level children each having VALUE clauses. At startup (no MOVE), each child field reads as its declared VALUE.
close_reason: Closed

title: COBOL: PIC X VALUE 'literal' initial value not tested — read-without-MOVE
