title: Java frontend missing unsigned right shift operator (>>>)
description: The Java frontend's operator mapping in interpreter/frontends/java/ does not handle the unsigned right shift operator (>>>). This causes compile_module() to fail with 'Unknown binary operator: >>>' for files that use this operator. Three files in a real multi-module project failed due to this. The fix is to add >>> to the Java operator mapping, likely mapping to a new BinopKind or a builtin function.
close_reason: Added UNSIGNED_RSHIFT (>>>) to BinopKind enum

title: Multi-module Java linking: label namespacing for merged IR from multiple files
description: When compiling multiple Java files from a multi-module Maven project and merging their IR, labels like func_main_2 collide across files. The current linker in interpreter/project/linker.py handles this for the normal compile_project() pipeline, but the Java import resolver (JavaImportResolver) only searches 4 fixed roots under a single project root, so it cannot resolve imports across Maven modules with separate source trees. Two sub-problems: (1) JavaImportResolver needs multi-root support for multi-module Maven projects, (2) wildcard imports (import com.example.*) have no resolution logic. The linker's link_modules() already handles label namespacing, but requires a properly constructed import graph which the resolver can't build for multi-module project structures.
close_reason: Multi-module Java linking implemented: MavenSourceRootDiscovery, multi-root JavaImportResolver with wildcard support, list[ResolvedImport] return type, NO_PATH null object. Integration test verifies val==43 and label=='result' across 4 modules.

title: Interprocedural analysis produces 0 flows for C pointer-passing programs
description:
Test program: void set_val(int *p) { *p = 99; } int x = 10; set_val(&x);
Call graph incorrectly shows set_val calling itself instead of top-level calling set_val. Summaries produce 0 flows. Whole-program graph is empty. The param→field flow (p → *p = 99) is not captured, and the call-site arg mapping (&x → p) is not established. Discovered during ADDRESS_OF.reads() investigation. Likely related to how C pointer parameters are modeled in the interprocedural summary builder — the STORE_INDIRECT *p = 99 may not be recognized as a field write on param p.
close_reason: Interprocedural pointer flow analysis fixed — DereferenceEndpoint type, STORE_INDIRECT/LOAD_INDIRECT summary extraction, ADDRESS_OF argument tracing. C pointer-passing programs now produce correct flows. 13,162 tests passing.

title: Migrate all Register-typed instruction fields from bare str to Register
description: Multiple instruction fields typed as Register actually hold bare strings at runtime (e.g., StoreField.obj_reg='%3', DeclVar.value_reg='%0'). This blocks the reads()/writes() methods from working with StorageIdentifier, because they call .is_present() which fails on str. The value_reg fields on DeclVar/StoreVar were fixed in the cv82 commit, but obj_reg, left, right, ptr_reg, arr_reg, index_reg, cond_reg, operand, size_reg, region_reg, offset_reg, value_reg (on StoreField/StoreIndex/StoreIndirect/WriteRegion) remain bare strings in some construction paths. Estimated ~50-100 sites across 18 frontend files. Same mechanical pattern as s5jh (result_reg migration).
close_reason: Removed 88 str() wrappings from Register-typed fields across 9 frontend files. All direct typed-instruction construction sites now pass Register objects directly. Fixed StorageIdentifier protocol to not require .value property (Register has .name, VarName has .value — they share is_present/str/hash). Verified reads()/writes() work correctly across 6 languages. 1 false positive fixed (FuncName(str(dec_reg)) in Python decorator lowering). 13,108 tests passing.

title: emit_inst(Label_) does not populate func_symbol_table
description: Discovered during audit #16 fix (e2zf). emit_inst(Label_(...)) does NOT register the label in ctx.func_symbol_table — the table stays empty. The original test_tracks_label had 'or True' hiding this. Either: (a) Label_ emission should register func labels in func_symbol_table (bug — fix the emit path), or (b) func_symbol_table is intentionally populated only by emit_func_ref (by design — the original test was always wrong). Needs investigation of TreeSitterEmitContext to determine which. File: interpreter/frontends/context.py, method emit_inst or related.
close_reason: By design — func_symbol_table is populated exclusively by emit_func_ref(), not by emit_inst(Label_). Label_ is a control-flow marker; emit_func_ref registers the name→label mapping and emits a CONST for callable resolution. The original test was always wrong; the or True hid it. Current test correctly asserts instruction recording.

title: VarName Phase 1b: change field types and cascade through VM/handlers/tests
description: Change instruction field types to VarName. Cascade through VM scope chain (local_vars, captured_var_names, var_heap_aliases), handlers, and ~400 test assertions. Remove __eq__(str) bridge. Same pattern as BinopKind Tasks 3-4.
close_reason: Field types changed, VM cascaded, bridge removed, ~600 test assertions updated, production code fixed (dataflow, summaries, field_fallback, MCP). 13,017 tests passing.

title: VarName Phase 1a: wrap all frontend/COBOL construction sites with VarName
description: Wrap ~450 DeclVar/StoreVar/LoadVar/AddressOf name= sites across 50 frontend and 3 COBOL files with VarName(). Field type stays str — bridge __eq__(str) ensures compatibility. Add import, wrap name argument. Same pattern as BinopKind Task 2.
close_reason: All ~430 frontend/COBOL construction sites wrapped with VarName

title: VarName simple wrapper: define type and migrate all sites
description: Phase 1: Define VarName(value: str) as a simple wrapper (like CodeLabel). Migrate ~450 frontend construction sites, 4 instruction field types, ~26 VM/handler scope chain uses, and ~400 test assertions. Bridge-first with __eq__(str) and hash compat. Remove bridge after full migration.
close_reason: VarName type defined with tests, __post_init__ guard, domain methods

title: Introduce FieldName domain type for field/property name fields
description: Replace str with FieldName for LoadField.field_name and StoreField.field_name. Used for heap object field access. 2 fields across 2 instruction classes. Needs brainstorming: how does this interact with HeapObject.fields dict keys? Should it be the same type as VarName or distinct?
close_reason: FieldName domain type complete: tagged FieldKind (PROPERTY/INDEX/SPECIAL), kind as identity, no str bridge, all instruction/heap/handler/test sites migrated. 13,034 tests passing.

title: Introduce VarName domain type for variable name fields
description: Replace str with VarName for LoadVar.name, DeclVar.name, StoreVar.name, AddressOf.var_name. These flow through scope chains, dataflow analysis, and the linker. 4 fields across 4 instruction classes. Needs brainstorming: should mangled names (x$1) be a variant? How does this interact with scope chain lookup (dict[str, ...] keyed by variable name)?
close_reason: All sub-issues closed: VarName type defined (b9cd), frontend sites wrapped (jdx0), field types + VM cascade (90z9), type_inference push (d3gp), structured decomposition (kozu), ClosureEnvironment bindings (ss6g). 13,106 tests passing.

title: Introduce Operator enum for Binop.operator and Unop.operator
description: Replace str with an Operator enum for the fixed set of arithmetic/comparison/logical/unary operators. Makes invalid operators a construction-time error. 2 fields across 2 instruction classes. Needs brainstorming: should binop and unop share one enum or have separate enums? How does this interact with the VM's operator dispatch?
close_reason: All 5 tasks complete: BinopKind/UnopKind enums defined, all ~95 operator= sites wrapped, field types strict, resolve functions reject unknown operators, per-language lint pass with 16 tests. 13,000 tests passing.

title: Capture parameterized types from statically-typed frontends
description: Capture parameterized types from Go frontend (slice_type, map_type). Parse tree-sitter type nodes into ParameterizedType instead of passing raw text strings. Other statically-typed frontends (Java, Kotlin, Scala, C#, Rust, C++, TypeScript) would require deeper changes since their constructors go through CallFunction, not NewObject directly — filed separately.
close_reason: Go frontend emits Array[T] for slices, Map[K,V] for maps. Other languages deferred to red-dragon-wkv1 (requires constructor dispatch changes).

title: Add EnumType, AnnotationType, StructPatternType to TypeExpr ADT
description: Add new TypeExpr ADT variants: EnumType(name), AnnotationType(name), StructPatternType(name). Replace scalar('enum:Foo') with EnumType('Foo') at ~12 frontend emit sites. Remove string-encoded prefix convention.
close_reason: EnumType, AnnotationType, StructPatternType added to TypeExpr ADT. All 10 string-encoded prefix sites replaced.

title: Migrate 3 remaining opcode== comparisons in registry.py
description: registry.py has 3 opcode== comparisons left because the instruction list is mixed-type (IRInstruction + typed). Needs normalization at the entry point or isinstance checks with to_typed() fallback. Discovered during Layer 3e.
close_reason: opcode comparisons replaced with isinstance

title: Migrate 59 COBOL frontend emit(Opcode.) calls to emit_inst()
description: COBOL frontend files lower_search.py (16), lower_perform.py (23), lower_call.py (2), lower_procedure.py (3), lower_arithmetic.py (12), condition_lowering.py (2), cobol_frontend.py (1) still use flat emit(Opcode.). Convert to emit_inst() with typed instructions. Discovered during Layer 4 migration.
close_reason: All COBOL frontend emit() calls migrated to emit_inst() with typed instructions. Literal args in emit_context.py CallFunction calls also fixed.

title: Fix COBOL ir_encoders.py to use const_to_reg for literal operands
description: ir_encoders.py places literal ints/floats directly in CALL_FUNCTION operands (e.g. BuiltinName.MAKE_LIST, 1, 0). This forces _as_register() workaround in to_typed converters. Fix ir_encoders.py to emit CONST instructions for literals, then remove _as_register(). Also: StoreIndex.value_reg type should be Register | SpreadArguments to match actual JS frontend usage.
close_reason: Literal operands in ir_encoders.py now go through CONST instructions into registers. StoreIndex.value_reg typed as Register | SpreadArguments. _as_register() retained until oczk migrates emit_context.py.

title: Phase 4 remaining: migrate emit() calls and pipeline types to Instruction
description: 2,481 emit() calls across frontends still create flat IRInstruction. 18 files use list[IRInstruction] type annotations. Typed instructions now have full interface compat (operands, result_reg, label, branch_targets, opcode, source_location), so the migration is mechanical: change emit() to construct typed classes, change type annotations to list[Instruction]. This is a bulk refactor best done per-frontend.
close_reason: Superseded by Layer 3 (red-dragon-1fws) and Layer 4 (red-dragon-jbgx) sub-issues under the revised 5-layer elimination plan.

title: Refactor: BRANCH_IF/TRY_PUSH targets should be structured, not comma-separated string in label
description: BRANCH_IF stores two targets as 'true_label,false_label' in the label field. TRY_PUSH stores catch/finally labels the same way. This encodes structured data (multiple targets) in a string, requiring .split(',') at every consumer. Should be separate fields (e.g., true_target/false_target) or a list[str] targets field. Separate from the CodeLabel refactoring.
close_reason: BRANCH_IF uses structured branch_targets: list[CodeLabel] field. No more comma-separated label hack. CodeLabel.branch_targets() removed. 63 files, 12850 tests passing.

title: Refactor: IRInstruction.result_reg — replace str | None with Register / NoRegister
description: IRInstruction.result_reg is str | None. 48% of instructions have result_reg=None, causing pyright reportArgumentType errors. Replace with null object pattern: Register(name: str) for real registers, NoRegister singleton for absent registers. ~144 read sites, ~315 construction sites. Heavy refactoring — same phased approach as the label refactoring. Should be done after the label refactoring since the pattern will be established.
close_reason: Migrated 669 test construction sites from result_reg=str to result_reg=Register(). Fixed LLM frontend JSON deserialization to wrap with Register(). Fixed chunked_llm_frontend._renumber_reg to return Register. Bridge in _Flat.__init__ retained as canonical conversion layer for _to_typed pipeline. 8 JSON fixture strings intentionally kept as strings. 42 test files modified. 13,108 tests passing.

title: Refactor: IRInstruction.label — replace str | None with CodeLabel / NoCodeLabel
description: IRInstruction.label is str | None. 72% of instructions have label=None, causing 83+ pyright reportArgumentType errors at call sites that assume non-None. Replace with null object pattern: CodeLabel(value: str) for real labels, NoCodeLabel singleton for absent labels. Both implement a common protocol. ~35 read sites, ~250 construction sites. Heavy refactoring — break into phases: (1) define types, (2) update IRInstruction, (3) update frontends, (4) update CFG/registry/VM, (5) update tests.
close_reason: CodeLabel/NoCodeLabel null object pattern with domain methods. 49 files updated, 12,850 tests passing.

title: Pyright: fix reportCallIssue (4) — stale API calls and missing arguments
description: 4 pyright errors: api.py calls execute_cfg_traced with kwargs that don't exist (func_symbol_table, class_symbol_table), expressions.py missing param_index argument. These may be real bugs.
close_reason: Fixed 2 real bugs: stale kwargs in execute_traced, missing param_index in lower_js_param. 1 false positive (MappingProxyType.get).

title: Add pyright type checking — report-only
description: Install pyright, add pyrightconfig.json with basic mode, run as report-only to see baseline type error count. Add to CI analysis job (continue-on-error). No enforcement yet.
close_reason: Pyright installed, basic mode, 197 errors baseline. Report-only in CI.

title: TS: import_alias (import Foo = Bar.Baz) produces SYMBOLIC
description: TypeScript namespace import 'import Foo = Bar.Baz' produces SYMBOLIC unsupported:import_alias. Should lower to LOAD_VAR Bar + LOAD_FIELD Baz + STORE_VAR Foo. Rare in modern TS (namespaces discouraged) but seen in legacy codebases and .d.ts files. Light fix — ~10 lines in the TS frontend dispatch.
close_reason: lower_import_alias handler + _lower_nested_identifier for dotted paths. 3 tests.

title: Pascal: apostrophe escape roundtrip in _parse_const
description: _parse_const strips outer quotes but does not un-escape doubled Pascal apostrophes ('can''t' → 'can't'). xfail in test_exercism_reverse_string.py::TestPascalApostropheLimitation.

title: Scala: case class instanceof not wired for match
description: Scala match on case classes (case Circle(r) => ...) doesn't dispatch isinstance correctly. xfail in test_scala_pattern_matching.py::TestScalaCaseClassPattern::test_case_class_match.

title: Scala: tree-sitter grammar lacks operator precedence
description: tree-sitter Scala grammar parses 'a - b > c' as '(a - b) > c' correctly but 'a > b - c' as 'a > (b - c)' — comparison and arithmetic have the same precedence. Two xfail tests in test_scala_frontend.py.

title: compile_directory: compile all source files in a directory tree
description: New function compile_directory(directory, language, entry_file) that scans a directory tree for all source files of the given language, compiles each one, and links them. Unlike compile_project which discovers files via import BFS, this compiles everything — catching orphaned modules, test files, and files not reachable via imports. The entry_file parameter determines which module's code runs first. Builds on compile_module and link_modules.
close_reason: compile_directory implemented with directory scanning, extension mapping for 16 languages, flat import graph. 6 tests.

title: Remove backwards-compat PythonFrontend re-export from interpreter.frontend
description: interpreter/frontend.py has a __getattr__ shim that lazily imports PythonFrontend from interpreter.frontends.python for backwards compatibility. This creates a transitive dependency from every frontend to interpreter.frontends.python via the _base → frontend chain, which import-linter flags. Only one consumer: tests/unit/test_frontend_factory.py. Fix: update the test to import from interpreter.frontends.python directly, remove the __getattr__ shim.
close_reason: Shim removed. Test updated. Transitive dependency eliminated. 1013 deps, 5 contracts passing.

title: Add import-linter contracts: project isolation, frontend independence, COBOL isolation
description: Three new architectural contracts: (1) interpreter.project should not import interpreter.vm or interpreter.handlers, (2) individual frontend packages should not import each other, (3) interpreter.cobol should not be imported by non-COBOL code except interpreter.frontend.
close_reason: 3 new contracts: project isolation, frontend independence, COBOL isolation. 5 total, all passing.

title: CI: make import-linter a gate, keep radon/pylint report-only
description: Move lint-imports from the report-only analysis job to the lint job so it fails CI on architectural contract violations. Radon and pylint stay report-only (too many pre-existing warnings to gate on).
close_reason: lint-imports moved to lint job as a gate. Radon/pylint stay report-only.

title: Java switch guard: guard condition on pattern-bound variable evaluates as symbolic
description: The two-stage branch (pattern test → bind → guard → branch) is correct. The binding 's = "hi"' happens before the guard. But 'hi'.length() returns symbolic because the VM doesn't dispatch Java's .length() method on raw Python strings. The guard comparison (symbolic > 3) is then symbolic, and branch_if assumes true. Fix: add string .length() to the VM's method builtin table.
close_reason: Root cause was missing .length() in METHOD_TABLE, not pattern binding ordering. Two-stage branch was already correct. Fixed by red-dragon-pubu.

title: Java: instanceof with record_pattern does not destructure components
description: 'o instanceof Point(int a, int b)' emits isinstance check but does not bind a or b. The lower_instanceof handler only handles simple type+binding (String s), not record_pattern with positional components. Switch expressions handle record_pattern correctly via parse_java_pattern. The instanceof handler would need to detect record_pattern child, call parse_java_pattern, and emit compile_pattern_bindings inside the if-body.
close_reason: lower_instanceof now detects record_pattern and delegates to parse_java_pattern + compile_pattern_bindings.

title: C# tuple pattern: positional_pattern_clause not handled in recursive_pattern
description: Tree-sitter parses tuple patterns like (1, _) as recursive_pattern with positional_pattern_clause. The current handler only processes property_pattern_clause (named fields like { X: 5 }), not positional_pattern_clause (indexed elements). Literal tuples (1, 2) work as constant_pattern. Wildcard-only tuples (_, _) work as recursive_pattern without checks. But mixed tuples like (1, _) compare the constant against the whole tuple instead of indexing into it. Needs LOAD_INDEX to access tuple elements by position.

title: ImportRef.kind is a raw string, should be an enum
description: kind: str = 'import' accepts any string. Valid set is well-known: import, include, use, require, mod, using. Per CLAUDE.md, use enums for fixed string sets.
close_reason: ImportKind enum with 6 values. All construction and comparison sites updated. Regular Enum, not StrEnum.

title: LinkedProgram.func_symbol_table and class_symbol_table use untyped dict
description: Both fields typed as bare dict instead of dict[str, FuncRef] and dict[str, ClassRef]. Loses type safety.
close_reason: Added type annotations: dict[CodeLabel, FuncRef] and dict[CodeLabel, ClassRef]. Annotation-only change, zero runtime impact.

title: COBOL multi-file integration test (skip when ProLeap unavailable)
description: test_all_languages_execution.py covers all 15 tree-sitter languages but not COBOL. Add a TestCobolMultiFile class that tests COPY copybook inclusion and CALL subprogram invocation through the full compile_project pipeline. Mark with pytest.importorskip or skip when PROLEAP_BRIDGE_JAR is not available, consistent with existing COBOL integration tests.
close_reason: COBOL multi-file integration test implemented in test_all_languages_execution.py::TestCobolMultiFile. Asserts WS-RESULT = 42 via zoned decimal decode.

title: Linker complexity: simplify import stub rewriting and module chaining
description: The current linker has accumulated patches to work around VM dispatch semantics: import stub rewriting (replacing CALL_FUNCTION 'import' with CONST label bindings), variable import dropping (removing import stubs for variable-only imports so dependency module STORE_VARs aren't overwritten), module entry chaining (inserting BRANCH instructions to connect dependency modules' top-level code), and symbol table name/label separation (bare names for dispatch, namespaced labels for CFG). These should be consolidated into a cleaner design — potentially a single-pass IR transform or a dedicated 'module preamble' block that runs all dependency init code before the entry module.
close_reason: Rewrote linker from scratch. 499→311 lines (38% reduction). Eliminated 4 separate patches (import stub rewriting, variable import dropping, module entry chaining, _chain_module_entries) with a single clean design: strip per-module entry labels, concatenate deps-first with one shared entry label, drop import stubs for resolved names. The merged IR now looks exactly like single-file compilation output.

title: Multi-file: COBOL extract_imports (COPY/CALL)
description: Parse COBOL COPY statements (copybook inclusion) and CALL statements (inter-program calls). COPY 'CUSTOMER-RECORD'. CALL 'SUBPROG' USING WS-DATA. COBOL uses the ProLeap frontend, not tree-sitter — extract_imports needs to work with the COBOL-specific AST. Copybooks are like C #include (textual inclusion). CALL is runtime linking but we need to discover the target programs statically.
close_reason: COBOL extract_imports (COPY/CALL) via regex + CobolImportResolver for .cpy/.cbl resolution

title: Multi-file: Go extract_imports (import declarations)

Handles: transitive imports, cycle detection (error on circular imports or break cycles), file-not-found errors. Returns a topologically sorted list of files to compile.

title: Multi-file: Per-module compiler
description: Compile a single file into a ModuleUnit. Wraps the existing pipeline (frontend.lower → build_cfg → build_registry) and adds export table extraction. The export table maps exported names (functions, classes) to internal labels. Each frontend needs an extract_exports() method or the compiler infers exports from the registry.

title: Multi-file project support: import resolution, per-module compilation, linking
description: Support analyzing and executing multi-file projects across all 15 languages. Three new components: (1) Import Resolver — parse import/include/require statements, discover transitive dependencies as a DAG. (2) Per-Module Compiler — compile each file independently into a ModuleUnit (IR, CFG, registry, export table). (3) Linker — resolve cross-module references via import tables, namespace labels, merge into a LinkedProgram. Architecture: per-module compilation with two-phase linking (build import tables, then rewrite references). No incremental caching initially. See spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-22-multi-file-project-design.md

title: Synthetic IR instructions missing source locations
description: 42% of IR instructions (16/38 in a simple 3-function program) have no source location mapping. Three categories: (1) decl_var for params — should inherit from the SYMBOLIC param instruction above, (2) const func_* + decl_var for function bindings — should point to the def/function line, (3) const None + return fallthrough — should point to the function's closing line. All are compiler-generated in the Python frontend's lowering pass.

title: Expose RedDragon VM and analysis as an MCP server
description: Create an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that exposes RedDragon's compilation pipeline, VM execution, and interprocedural dataflow analysis as tools for LLMs. This would allow an LLM to lower source code, execute it step-by-step, inspect VM state, run dataflow analysis, and query call chains — enabling AI-assisted code analysis, debugging, and program understanding workflows.

title: TUI footer truncates bindings on initial mount
description: The Footer widget doesn't show all key bindings (space Play/Pause, home First, end Last) on initial mount. They appear after toggling dataflow mode and back, which triggers a CSS re-layout. Likely a Textual Footer width calculation issue — adding the 'd Dataflow' binding pushed total width past the threshold. Cosmetic only — all bindings still work.

title: TUI traced VM returns SymbolicValue for function calls instead of executing them
description: run_pipeline() in viz/pipeline.py uses execute_cfg_traced which doesn't properly propagate return values across function calls. The traced VM returns SymbolicValue (e.g., sym_0 with type_hint='quadruple(5)') instead of the actual computed value (20). The non-traced run() function works correctly. This means the VM State panel in the TUI shows symbolic placeholders for any variable assigned from a function call result.

title: Interprocedural: build_summary produces empty flows — param→return chain not traced
description: build_summary runs intraprocedural analyze() on a function's sub-CFG but the resulting DataflowResult.dependency_graph doesn't connect SYMBOLIC param declarations to RETURN operands. The register chain from param:x → STORE_VAR x → LOAD_VAR x → BINOP → RETURN doesn't produce a dependency_graph entry linking 'x' to the return value. Root cause likely in how _build_raw_dependency_graph traces through register chains for function-scoped sub-CFGs. This means FunctionSummary.flows is empty for all functions, so the whole-program graph has no cross-function edges. Blocks test_program_graphs_non_empty in test_interprocedural_integration.py.

title: Fix: interprocedural propagation uses register names instead of traced variable names
description: apply_summary_at_call_site substitutes formal params with raw register names (%3, %result_reg) instead of tracing them to named variables. This produces orphaned edges in the whole-program graph that don't connect to source-level variables. Fix: use dataflow._trace_to_named_vars() or the caller's dependency_graph to resolve registers to named variables during substitution.
close_reason: Partial fix: register→variable tracing added. Remaining empty-summary issue needs separate investigation.

title: Fix: interprocedural call graph callee resolution — function name vs CFG label mismatch
description: CALL_FUNCTION operands use bare function names (e.g., 'add') but build_function_entries indexes by CFG labels (e.g., 'func_add_0'). Resolution fails, producing empty callees. Fix: build name→label lookup from FuncRef objects in registry, or strip prefix/suffix from labels. 3 integration tests xfailed pending this fix (red-dragon-5iez duplicate).
close_reason: Fixed: func_refs on FunctionRegistry + name→label lookup in call graph builder.

title: Call graph: CALL_FUNCTION name-to-label resolution
description: CALL_FUNCTION operands use function names (e.g. 'countdown') but build_function_entries keys by CFG label (e.g. 'func_countdown_0'). Need a name-to-label mapping in call_graph.py so callee resolution works for direct function calls. Three integration tests are xfailed waiting on this fix.
close_reason: Fixed via red-dragon-pa25.

title: Interprocedural dataflow analysis — call graph, summaries, propagation, queries

Call graph (CHA), function summaries (1-CFA), whole-program fixpoint propagation, query interface (impact analysis, taint tracking, program slicing). Depth-1 field-sensitive. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-22-interprocedural-dataflow-design.md

title: Refactor: extract executor handler families into grouped modules
description:
## Summary

Split executor.py (1,855 lines, 31 handlers) into handler family modules. Prerequisite HandlerContext (red-dragon-w358) is done.

## Proposed Structure

```
interpreter/
  executor.py           → slim orchestrator (~200 lines): LocalExecutor, dispatch table, execute_cfg
  handlers/
    __init__.py
    variables.py        → _handle_const, _handle_load_var, _handle_store_var, _handle_decl_var, _handle_symbolic (~200 lines)
    memory.py           → _handle_load_field, _handle_store_field, _handle_load_index, _handle_store_index, _handle_load_indirect, _handle_store_indirect, _handle_address_of, _handle_load_field_indirect (~400 lines)
    control_flow.py     → _handle_branch, _handle_branch_if, _handle_return, _handle_throw, _handle_try_push, _handle_try_pop, _handle_set_continuation, _handle_resume_continuation (~200 lines)
    calls.py            → _handle_call_function, _handle_call_method, _handle_call_unknown (~500 lines)
    arithmetic.py       → _handle_binop, _handle_unop (~200 lines)
    objects.py          → _handle_new_object, _handle_new_array (~100 lines)
    regions.py          → _handle_alloc_region, _handle_write_region, _handle_load_region (~150 lines)
```

## Benefits
- Each family testable in isolation
- Easier to navigate (find call dispatch? → handlers/calls.py)
- Parallel development on different handler families
- Clear ownership of helper functions per family

## Approach
- Pure file reorganization — zero behavior change
- Move functions + their helpers to family modules
- executor.py becomes slim: imports handlers, builds dispatch dict, defines LocalExecutor + execute_cfg
- All tests continue to work (they import from executor.py which re-exports or tests call handlers directly)
close_reason: Done: executor.py split from 1855→224 lines into 8 handler family modules. Zero behavior change.

title: Refactor: replace executor **kwargs with HandlerContext dataclass
description:
## Summary

All 31 executor handler functions receive parameters via `**kwargs`, losing type safety, IDE autocomplete, and explicit dependency declaration. Replace with a `HandlerContext` dataclass.

## Problem

Every handler in `interpreter/executor.py` does this:

```python
def _handle_load_field(inst, vm, **kwargs):
    symbol_table = kwargs.get("symbol_table", SymbolTable.empty())
    type_env = kwargs.get("type_env", _EMPTY_TYPE_ENV)
    registry = kwargs["registry"]
    cfg = kwargs["cfg"]
    ...
```

This pattern repeats across 31 handlers. The call site in `LocalExecutor._step()` unpacks and passes 10+ parameters every time. Problems:
- No type hints on handler parameters (kwargs are `Any`)
- IDE autocomplete can't help inside handlers
- Refactoring "remove type_env from all handlers" requires auditing 31 functions
- Handler signature doesn't declare what it actually uses

## Proposed Fix

Create a `HandlerContext` dataclass:

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class HandlerContext:
    cfg: CFG
    registry: FunctionRegistry
    current_label: str
    ip: int
    type_env: TypeEnvironment
    symbol_table: SymbolTable
    binop_coercion: BinopCoercionStrategy
    unop_coercion: UnopCoercionStrategy
    conversion_rules: ConversionRules
    overload_resolver: OverloadResolver | None
    field_fallback: FieldFallback | None
```

Handler signature becomes:
```python
def _handle_load_field(inst: IRInstruction, vm: VMState, ctx: HandlerContext) -> ExecutionResult:
    symbol_table = ctx.symbol_table  # typed, autocomplete works
```

## Scope
- Replace **kwargs with HandlerContext in all 31 handlers
- Create HandlerContext in LocalExecutor._step() once per step
- Zero behavior change — pure mechanical refactor
- Prerequisite for future handler family extraction

## Files
- `interpreter/executor.py` (1,855 lines, 31 handlers)
close_reason: Done: HandlerContext dataclass replaces **kwargs in all 31 handlers. Zero behavior change.

title: COBOL: PIC X VALUE with digit-only strings stores zeros
description: VALUE clause for PIC X fields with digit-only content (e.g., VALUE '1234') stores all zeros instead of EBCDIC-encoded digits. Likely the lowering misinterprets digit-only alphanumeric literals as numeric zero. Discovered during REDEFINES testing.
close_reason: Fixed: emit_encode_alphanumeric now wraps values in quotes (value=f'"{value}"') so _parse_const returns str not int. MOVE literal path also fixed to quote unquoted digit-only sources. Regression tests in TestPicXDigitOnlyValue cover both paths. 13,633 tests pass.

title: COBOL: REDEFINES fields get sequential offsets instead of shared offsets
description: data_layout.py assigns REDEFINES fields their own sequential offsets instead of sharing the offset of the field they redefine. This breaks MOVE from REDEFINES fields since IR lowering reads/writes at the wrong byte position. Discovered via integration tests: test_simple_redefines_data_layout_offset, test_multiple_redefines_data_layout_offsets, test_group_redefines_move_composite.
close_reason: Fixed: REDEFINES offset sharing in _flatten_field + _fix_redefines_offsets. All 9 integration tests pass.

title: Rust: *r dereference returns SymbolicValue instead of concrete value
description: When a variable holds a Pointer (from &x), dereferencing with *r emits LOAD_FIELD '__boxed__' which returns a SymbolicValue instead of the concrete value. The actual value is stored at heap field '0', not '__boxed__'. Affects: *r in expressions, match body deref (*val), any explicit pointer dereference. Related xfails: test_read_through_reference, test_modify_original_visible_through_reference in test_pointer_aliasing_execution.py. Workaround: use &val reference patterns in match arms instead of *val in body.
close_reason: Fixed: unified Box and reference dereference on LOAD_INDIRECT. BOXED_FIELD changed from __boxed__ to 0. lower_deref_expr emits LOAD_INDIRECT. 2 xfails now pass.

title: Scala 3: parameterized enum variants (case Circle(radius: Double))
description: Parameterized enum variants like case Circle(radius: Double) need lightweight classes with constructors, similar to Rust custom enum variants (red-dragon-vwqd). Follow-up from red-dragon-416 which only covers simple enums.

title: Ruby: guard/if patterns in case/in
description: Guard clauses in Ruby case/in: in pattern if condition. Follow-up from red-dragon-6n0u.

title: Ruby: pin patterns (^variable) in case/in
description: Pin patterns like ^x in Ruby case/in. Match against existing variable value. Follow-up from red-dragon-6n0u.

title: Ruby: find_pattern in case/in
description: Find patterns like [*, elem, *] in Ruby case/in. Special sequence search pattern. Follow-up from red-dragon-6n0u.

title: Ruby: hash_pattern destructuring in case/in
description: Hash patterns like {x:, y:} in Ruby case/in. Maps to MappingPattern in Pattern ADT. Follow-up from red-dragon-6n0u.

title: Refactor: extract unified match expression lowering framework (match_expr.py)
description:
## Summary

Extract the duplicated match/switch expression lowering skeleton from 4 language frontends into a shared framework in `interpreter/frontends/common/match_expr.py`.

## Problem

Rust, Scala, Kotlin, and C# each implement ~80-120 lines of identical IR-emitting logic for expression-style match:
- result_var / end_label allocation
- irrefutability shortcut (WildcardPattern/CapturePattern skip test)
- pre-guard binding protocol (_needs_pre_guard_bindings)
- guard folding (BINOP && test_reg guard_reg)
- BRANCH_IF / LABEL / BRANCH / DECL_VAR skeleton per arm
- final LABEL + LOAD_VAR epilogue

~320 lines of duplicated control-flow logic across 4 files.

## Proposed Design (Design 1: Minimal Interface)

**New file:** `interpreter/frontends/common/match_expr.py` (~80 lines)

**Interface:**
```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MatchArmSpec:
    extract_arms: Callable[[object], list[object]]
    pattern_of: Callable[[TreeSitterEmitContext, object], Pattern]
    guard_of: Callable[[TreeSitterEmitContext, object], object | None]
    body_of: Callable[[TreeSitterEmitContext, object], str]

def lower_match_as_expr(ctx, subject_reg, body_node, spec: MatchArmSpec) -> str
```

**Per-language usage:** Define a `_LANG_SPEC` constant with 4 callbacks, call `lower_match_as_expr`.

**What gets eliminated per language:**
- Rust: `_lower_match_arm` + `_extract_arm_body` (~80 lines → ~30 lines of extractors)
- Scala: `_lower_case_clause` (~80 lines → ~20 lines of extractors)
- Kotlin: `_lower_when_entry` + `_lower_when_body` (~80 lines → ~25 lines of extractors)
- C#: inline switch_expr logic (~55 lines → ~10 lines of extractors)

**Excluded by design:**
- Kotlin subjectless `when` (not pattern matching — stays in caller)
- C# switch statement (uses break_target_stack — stays separate)
- Python statement-style match (uses compile_match with lower_block)

## References
- ADR-117 (Rust), ADR-118 (Scala), ADR-119 (Kotlin) established the pattern
- Spec comparison of all 4 implementations done in architecture review
close_reason: Closed

title: Kotlin: range patterns (in 1..10) in when expression
description: Kotlin when supports range checks: when(x) { in 1..10 -> ... }. Needs RangePattern or desugaring to comparison operators. Follow-up from red-dragon-y0df.

title: Kotlin: destructuring patterns in when expression
description: Kotlin when with destructuring: when(val (a, b) = pair) { ... }. Needs componentN-based destructuring infrastructure. Follow-up from red-dragon-y0df.

title: Kotlin: smart casts after is-check in when
description: Kotlin smart casts: after 'is String' check in when arm, variable is auto-cast to String type. Needs type narrowing in VM/type system. Follow-up from red-dragon-y0df.

title: Go: migrate type switch to Pattern ADT
description: Go type switches (switch x.(type)) currently use hand-rolled type checks. Could benefit from Pattern ADT ClassPattern + isinstance for cleaner type dispatch. lower_expression_switch and lower_type_switch in go/control_flow.py.

title: Java: migrate switch to Pattern ADT for sealed/record patterns (Java 17+)
description: Java switch currently uses hand-rolled == comparisons. Java 17+ introduces pattern matching in switch (sealed classes, record patterns, guarded patterns). Migrate lower_java_switch/lower_java_switch_expr to use parse_java_pattern + compile_pattern_test/compile_pattern_bindings.

title: Scala: extractor patterns (custom unapply) in match
description: Custom destructuring via unapply methods. Stretch goal for Scala pattern matching. Follow-up from red-dragon-hgfq.

title: Scala: as-patterns (x @ Pattern) in match
description: Variable binding with inner pattern: x @ Circle(r) binds x to subject while also destructuring. Maps to AsPattern in Pattern ADT. Follow-up from red-dragon-hgfq.

title: Scala: infix patterns (head :: tail) in match
description: Cons-list destructuring via infix patterns like head :: tail. Requires cons-list infrastructure in VM or desugaring to ClassPattern. Follow-up from red-dragon-hgfq.

title: Rust: while let pattern matching
description: while let Some(x) = expr { body } — deferred from red-dragon-r06p. Needs detect let_condition in while handler, similar to if-let implementation in lower_if_expr.
close_reason: Implemented: while let with pattern test/bind loop. 2 integration tests.

title: Rust: Some(v) match destructuring — LOAD_INDEX on Option returns symbolic value
description: ClassPattern isinstance check works correctly for Option/Some(v), but compile_pattern_bindings uses LOAD_INDEX positionally which returns a symbolic value instead of the concrete wrapped value. The Option prelude class stores the value in a 'value' field (LOAD_FIELD), not positionally (LOAD_INDEX). Need to either: (a) teach ClassPattern to use LOAD_FIELD for keyword access when positional args map to named fields, or (b) add __match_args__ to Option prelude class. Discovered during red-dragon-r06p Task 4.
close_reason: Fixed via match_args: Option/Box registered in symbol table with match_args=("value",)/("__boxed__",). Pattern parsers convert positional→keyword via _resolve_positional_via_match_args. 3 Rust xfails now pass.

title: Rust: ref/mut bindings and @ patterns in match
description: Rust ref/mut bindings (ref x, ref mut x) and @ bindings (x @ 1..=5) in match arms. Low priority stretch goal. Follow-up from red-dragon-r06p.

title: Rust: range patterns in match (1..=5)
description: Range patterns like 1..=5 in Rust match arms. Would need a new RangePattern type in the common Pattern ADT or lowering to OrPattern of literals for small ranges. Follow-up from red-dragon-r06p.

title: Rust: add enum variants to symbol table extraction
description: extract_rust_symbols does not extract enum_item nodes. Enum variants and their associated data are invisible to the symbol table, making pattern matching type resolution harder. Need to add enum handling to _collect_rust_structs_and_impls or a parallel collector.

title: Rust: emit lightweight variant classes for custom enums
description: Custom enum variants like Shape::Circle(r) need proper runtime classes for destructuring in pattern matching. Currently lower_enum_item creates a flat object with variant names as fields but no associated data. Need to emit a class per variant with __init__ storing positional fields, so ClassPattern + isinstance works.

title: Symbol table Phase 4: forward references and cross-class field awareness

title: Symbol extraction pre-pass: catalog classes, fields, functions, and constants before IR lowering
description: A pre-pass over the tree-sitter AST that builds a symbol table (classes with their fields, functions with signatures, constants) before IR lowering begins. Would eliminate ad-hoc workarounds like _class_field_names, _resolve_match_args AST walking, _resolve_class_static_field IR scanning. Enables forward references, cross-class field awareness, and better type inference. Significant architectural change — needs careful brainstorming.

title: TypedValue type lost through _resolve_reg unwrapping in DECL_VAR
description:
When DECL_VAR copies a register value to local vars, it calls _resolve_reg() which unwraps TypedValue, discarding the type information. The value is then re-wrapped via typed_from_runtime() which produces UNKNOWN type.

This means pointer(scalar('Point')) set on a register by NEW_OBJECT degrades to UNKNOWN (or scalar('Point') at best) when stored in local vars. The Pointer value itself flows correctly — only the type annotation is lost.

Impact: integration tests cannot assert tv.type == pointer(scalar('Dog')) on local vars. Type information is only correct in registers, not in the variable store.

Root cause: _resolve_reg() at vm.py:327 unconditionally unwraps TypedValue via .value, then downstream code re-wraps with typed_from_runtime() which cannot recover the original parameterized type.

Fix options:
1. Make _resolve_reg return TypedValue instead of bare values (broad change, many callsites)
2. Make DECL_VAR/STORE_VAR preserve TypedValue from registers without unwrapping
3. Thread TypedValue through the register→variable pipeline end-to-end

Discovered during Pointer migration (red-dragon-lr9y).

title: Pascal: _lower_pascal_method stub missing DECL_VAR self alias
description: _lower_pascal_method (class body forward declaration stub) emits DECL_VAR this but not DECL_VAR self. The defProc body emits both. If Pascal code in a stub body ever references self, it would fail. Low practical risk since stubs have empty bodies, but an asymmetry worth fixing for consistency.

description: lower_pascal_proc handles both kFunction and kProcedure via genericDot detection, but only procedure TFoo.SetName is tested. Need a test for function TFoo.GetName: string to verify return type extraction works with qualified names.

title: C#: ref to array element (ref arr[i]) not supported
description: ref int x = ref arr[i]; requires constructing Pointer with computed offset. Follow-up to red-dragon-c4v.

title: C#: ref to object field (ref obj.field) not supported
description: ref int x = ref obj.field; requires constructing Pointer with field name offset. Follow-up to red-dragon-c4v.

title: C#: ref returns (return ref arr[0]) not supported
description: Returning a reference from a method. Follow-up to red-dragon-c4v (ref locals). Requires ref local support first.

title: C#: ref reassignment (x = ref arr[1]) not supported
description: Rebinding a ref local to point at a different location. Follow-up to red-dragon-c4v (ref locals). Requires ref local support first.

title: C++: structured binding struct member decomposition via LOAD_FIELD
description: auto [x, y] = point; where point is a struct — decompose into LOAD_FIELD per public member instead of LOAD_INDEX. Requires knowing field names at compile time. Follow-up to red-dragon-cq8 which only supports positional (LOAD_INDEX) decomposition.

title: C#: ref return and ref local not supported
description: C# 7.0 ref returns (ref int Find(...) => return ref arr[i]) and ref locals (ref int slot = ref Find(...)). Requires managed pointers that survive across function returns. Much more complex than out/ref/in params. Low priority unless real-world code exercises this.

title: Introduce LOAD_INDIRECT / STORE_INDIRECT opcodes to replace LOAD_FIELD/STORE_FIELD with magic "*" field
description: ADR-099 uses LOAD_FIELD ptr "*" and STORE_FIELD ptr "*" val to dereference pointers. The "*" field name is a magic string overloading field access semantics. Introduce dedicated LOAD_INDIRECT / STORE_INDIRECT opcodes with clear intent. Migrate C and Rust frontends, then C# byref params. Blocked by: red-dragon-ia8 (C# out/ref/in must land first using the existing "*" convention).

title: VM: pass-by-reference semantics for out/ref parameters
description: C# out/ref parameters require pass-by-reference: callee assigns to the parameter and the caller sees the updated value. Currently the VM passes all arguments by value, so out params keep their default (0) in the caller scope. Needs a ref-cell or address-of mechanism in the VM to support this.

title: tree-sitter-c-sharp: v * scale parsed as declaration_expression in constructor initializer args
description: tree-sitter-c-sharp 0.23.1 (latest) parses 'this(v * scale)' inside a constructor_initializer as declaration_expression instead of binary_expression (multiplication). This is a grammar bug — constructor initializer argument lists should not permit declaration_expression. Workaround: use addition or explicit this.field assignment in the constructor body. May be worth filing upstream at the tree-sitter-c-sharp repo.

title: Scala auxiliary constructor this() chaining
description: Scala auxiliary constructors call this(args) for delegation. Currently not handled. Should follow the Java/Kotlin pattern for constructor delegation.

title: C# constructor chaining via :this() — lower as CALL_METHOD like Java
description: C# supports constructor chaining with :this(args) syntax. Currently not handled. Should follow the Java pattern: emit CALL_METHOD on this with __init__ as method name.

title: Kotlin secondary constructor delegation should inline full primary constructor body (including field initializers)
description: Currently _emit_constructor_delegation only maps delegation arguments to primary constructor params via STORE_FIELD. It doesn't replay field initializer stores (e.g. var doubled: Int = 0). This means fields initialized in the class body don't exist on the heap object when the secondary constructor body runs, breaking implicit this.field resolution for those fields. Fix: inline the full primary constructor body (param stores + field initializer stores) during delegation. xfail: test_secondary_constructor_body_reads_field in test_kotlin_frontend_execution.py
close_reason: Tasks 5-6 done: Builtins.TABLE and METHOD_TABLE now FuncName-keyed, str() removed from lookup_builtin and lookup_method_builtin

title: Replace regex-based CLASS_REF_PATTERN with structured class references
description: Class references are stringly-typed (e.g. '<class:Dog@class_Dog_0:Animal>') and parsed back with CLASS_REF_PATTERN regex. Same problem as red-dragon-fdc but for classes. Replace with structured data type. Do after red-dragon-fdc is complete.

title: Replace regex-based FUNC_REF_PATTERN with structured function references
description: Function references are currently stringly-typed (e.g. '<function:new@func_new_0>') and parsed back with FUNC_REF_PATTERN regex. This is fragile — e.g. dotted names like 'Counter.new' break \w+ matching. Replace with a structured data type (dataclass or similar) that carries function name, label, and optional class directly, eliminating regex parsing entirely.

title: Lua: colon syntax (:) with implicit self for method dispatch
description: Lua colon syntax (obj:method()) implies implicit self as first argument. Currently only dot syntax is supported (ADR-104). Colon syntax should emit CALL_METHOD with implicit self injection, while dot syntax continues to use LOAD_FIELD + CALL_UNKNOWN with explicit self.

title: Pascal: unit/module system not supported
description: Pascal unit declarations (unit MyUnit; interface ... implementation ... end.) are the module system. UNIT node not dispatched. Required for multi-file Pascal analysis.

title: Java: string templates (Java 21) not supported
description: Java 21 string templates (STR."Hello \\{name}") are a new core language feature. TEMPLATE_EXPRESSION not dispatched.

title: Ruby: string interpolation node not dispatched
description: Ruby INTERPOLATION node (the \#{expr} inside double-quoted strings) is not explicitly dispatched. May be handled via parent traversal, but worth verifying.

title: C++: coroutines (co_await/co_return/co_yield) not supported
description: C++20 coroutines are increasingly common in async C++ code. CO_AWAIT_EXPRESSION, CO_RETURN_STATEMENT, CO_YIELD_STATEMENT not dispatched.

title: Rust Result/panic\! error handling not supported
description: Rust's error handling uses Result<T, E> with ? operator and panic\!/catch_unwind for unrecoverable errors. These are ubiquitous in real-world Rust code. Our VM returns SymbolicValue for these patterns. The Rust frontend should handle Result as a type (similar to Option) and support the ? operator for early error return.

title: Add string concat builtin to VM for languages with native concat functions
description: C has strcat(), Rust has format\!(), and other languages have native string concat functions beyond the + operator. The VM should support a concat builtin that these can lower to, enabling string concatenation in languages where + doesn't naturally apply to strings. Currently C is excluded from the Rosetta string_concat test because it has no expression-level concat.

title: Pascal TCounter.Create (Delphi OOP constructor) returns symbolic
description: Pascal class constructors via TCounter.Create return SymbolicValue. The class is recognized but .Create (inherited Delphi constructor) is not wired up as a constructor call. This blocks method chaining and other OOP patterns in Pascal.

title: Go defer/recover error handling not supported
description: Go's error handling uses defer/recover for panic recovery. This is a common real-world Go pattern. Our VM doesn't handle defer statements or recover() calls. The Go frontend should lower defer as a deferred function call and recover() as a builtin.

title: Lua pcall/xpcall error handling not supported
description: Lua's error handling mechanism is pcall(fn)/xpcall(fn, handler). These are real-world Lua patterns for error recovery. Our VM returns SymbolicValue for pcall calls. The Lua frontend should handle pcall as a builtin that calls the function and returns (true, result) or (false, error).

title: Java hex float literals stored as strings instead of parsed to float
description: 0x1.0p10 should parse to 1024.0 during lowering, but it's stored as the raw string '0x1.0p10'. The Java frontend's literal handler needs to recognize hex_floating_point_literal nodes and convert them via float.fromhex() or equivalent.
close_reason: Closed

title: PHP print expression should return 1
description: PHP's print is an expression that always returns 1. Our VM treats it as a statement, so $r = print 'hi' stores None instead of 1. The PHP frontend should lower print_intrinsic to emit CONST 1 as the result register after the print side-effect.

title: Register method signatures for prelude classes (Box, Option) in TypeEnvironmentBuilder
description:
The prelude classes (Box, Option) emitted by the Rust frontend bypass the normal type inference pipeline. The TypeEnvironmentBuilder never sees their method signatures, so the executor's overload resolver always hits the 'sig/label count mismatch' warning and falls back to index 0.

This works for now because prelude methods have no overloads, but it's fragile. The fix should register method signatures for prelude classes either:
1. During prelude emission (the frontend knows the signatures)
2. During TypeEnvironmentBuilder scanning (teach it to recognize prelude_class_ labels)
3. Via a prelude-specific method signature registration in the TypeEnvironment

Related: red-dragon-62g (Box/Option implementation)

title: Rust frontend: Box/Option unwrap in recursive linked list traversal returns SymbolicValue
description: Simple struct field access works (Point { x: 42 }; p.x → 42). But recursive traversal through Option<Box<Node>> (node.next_node.as_ref().unwrap()) returns SymbolicValue. The .as_ref().unwrap() chain is not resolved by the VM. Affects Rosetta linked list test — Rust is one of 2/15 languages still symbolic.

title: Pascal frontend: array-of-records index-based traversal returns SymbolicValue
description: Simple record field access works (p.x := 42; answer := p.x → 42). But array-of-records traversal (nodes[idx].value, nodes[idx].nextIdx) returns SymbolicValue. The combination of array indexing + field access on the result is not resolved. Affects Rosetta linked list test — Pascal is one of 2/15 languages still symbolic.

title: Class/struct type metadata: ClassTypeDescriptor with TypeExpr fields
description: Add first-class type metadata for class/struct definitions. Store ClassTypeDescriptor (name, fields as dict[str, TypeExpr], parent types) on the context during lowering, replacing the current approach where struct field info exists only as IR instructions. Support multi-pass resolution for recursive types (struct Node { Node* next }). Integrate with TypeEnvironmentBuilder. This enables struct-aware initializer lists, better type inference, and moves toward JVM/CLR-style type metadata. Supersedes the IR-scan approach used for the initializer list fix.

title: Pascal frontend: record field access through pointer deref returns SymbolicValue
description: Pascal record field access via pointer dereference (node^.value) returns SymbolicValue. Direct record field access (n1.value := 3; answer := n1.value) works for simple dot notation. The pointer-based traversal pattern needed for linked lists doesn't resolve. Also: the @ (address-of) operator is unsupported (lowered as 'symbolic unsupported:kAt').
notes: Pascal record field access works for direct dot notation (answer=42). Pointer deref is separate.

title: Scala frontend: primary constructor val params not lowered to STORE_FIELD
description: Scala class with primary constructor val params (class Node(val value: Int, val nextNode: Node)) generates an empty class body — no __init__ method, no STORE_FIELD instructions. The VM creates the object but fields are never set, so LOAD_FIELD returns SymbolicValue. Same issue as Kotlin (red-dragon-6cb).

title: C frontend: struct field access through pointers returns SymbolicValue
description: C struct field access works with direct dot notation (p.x = 42; answer = p.x → 42). But accessing fields through pointer dereference (node->value, node->next_node) returns SymbolicValue. Initializer-list construction ({3, 0}) also doesn't persist field values for later LOAD_FIELD resolution. Affects linked list traversal patterns.

title: Rust frontend: struct literal field access returns SymbolicValue
description: Rust struct literals (Node { value: 3, next_node: None }) create objects via call_function, but subsequent LOAD_FIELD on the object returns SymbolicValue. The struct literal initialization doesn't store fields in a way the VM can resolve. Simple field access like n1.value after construction returns sym_X instead of the concrete value.
notes: Rust struct literals already produce concrete field values (answer=42). No fix needed.

title: C++ frontend: explicit 'this' param in __init__ breaks field storage
description: C++ constructor lowering emits 'symbolic param:this' as the first parameter of __init__, but the VM injects 'this' implicitly when calling constructors. This causes parameter binding mismatch — the constructor args get shifted, and STORE_FIELD operates on a symbolic 'this' instead of the actual object reference. Result: LOAD_FIELD on C++ objects returns SymbolicValue. Java/C#/Python/JS/etc. do NOT include 'this' as a parameter and work correctly.

title: Kotlin frontend: secondary constructor syntax not lowered
description: Kotlin secondary constructors (constructor(v: Int) { this.value = v }) are lowered as 'symbolic unsupported:secondary_constructor'. Primary constructor val params now work correctly (class Node(val x: Int) generates __init__ with STORE_FIELD). Only secondary constructors remain unsupported.

title: P2: Rosetta pattern_matching uses if/else, not actual pattern matching
description: test_rosetta_pattern_matching.py: All 15 programs use if/else-if chains, not language-specific pattern matching (match/switch/when). Name implies the pattern matching feature.

title: P2: Python test_future_import_with_class has no class definition
description: test_python_frontend_execution.py::test_future_import_with_class: Name and docstring mention 'class definition' but source is just future import + arithmetic.

title: P2: test_for_loop_destructuring_execution.py is IR-only, not execution
description: File is in tests/integration/ and named 'execution' but only checks IR generation (opcodes), no VM execution via run().

title: P2: 3 Lua method tests check existence only, not correctness
description: test_lua_method_index_execution.py: test_method_call_with_args_executes, test_method_call_chained, test_method_call_executes — all only check 'r in local_vars', not that r has the expected value.

title: P2: test_type_hint_propagation.py is lowering-only, not integration
description: File is in tests/integration/ but only checks frontend lowering and type_env_builder state. No VM execution occurs.

title: Explore unifying TypedValue.type with TypeEnvironment register_types/var_types
description: TypedValue now carries runtime type alongside every value, but TypeEnvironment.register_types and var_types still store static/declared types separately. These overlap: write-time coercion (Layer 2) exists to reconcile mismatches between the two (e.g. Python float from int division when declared type is Int). If Layer 1 coercion strategies were made TypeEnvironment-aware (so result_type() returns the declared type directly), write-time coercion would become a no-op for locally-executed instructions. It would only remain necessary for LLM-produced updates. This could simplify the two-layer architecture significantly. Investigation needed: (1) how many write-time coercions actually fire in practice, (2) what it would take to thread TypeEnvironment into BinopCoercionStrategy/UnopCoercionStrategy, (3) whether the LLM path can be handled differently.

title: BinopCoercionStrategy.coerce should return tuple[TypedValue, TypedValue] not tuple[Any, Any]
description: coerce() currently strips TypedValue and returns raw values. It should preserve TypedValue wrappers so type information flows through the binop pipeline cleanly.

title: Wrap bare values in LLMPlausibleResolver._parse_llm_response with TypedValue
Refactor builtins to emit StateUpdate with heap_writes instead of direct vm.heap mutation
description: Builtins like _builtin_array_of and _builtin_object_rest currently create HeapObjects with raw field dicts directly, bypassing StateUpdate/apply_update. Refactor them to return ExecutionResult with new_objects + heap_writes, giving a uniform write path. Prerequisite: red-dragon-x2t (HeapObject.fields stores TypedValue).

title: Migrate HeapObject.fields to store TypedValue (heap Phase 2)
Replace constructor result_reg=None hack with is_ctor flag on StackFrame
description:
Current approach: _handle_call_dispatch_setup re-detects constructor calls via scope chain walk and sets result_reg=None to prevent __init__'s return value from clobbering the object address. This is fragile (had a scope chain bug) and redundant (_try_class_constructor_call already knows).

Clean approach:
1. Add is_ctor: bool = False to StackFramePush and StackFrame
2. _try_class_constructor_call sets is_ctor=True
3. _handle_return checks vm.current_frame.is_ctor — if true, produces typed(None, scalar(Void)) regardless of operands (constructors are void)
4. _handle_return_flow: skip write if return value is Void, write if None or concrete
5. Remove constructor detection from _handle_call_dispatch_setup entirely
6. Remove result_reg=None hack

Note: JS constructor explicit object return edge case tracked separately in red-dragon-4hz.

title: JS constructor explicit object return: new Foo() can return different object
description: JavaScript constructors can return a different object than 'this' if they explicitly return an object (not a primitive). This is a pathological edge case that no other supported language has. Currently not modeled — the is_ctor flag on StackFrame makes _handle_return produce Void for all constructors, swallowing any explicit return. If ever needed, the JS frontend could emit different IR for this pattern (e.g. bypass the constructor call path). Low priority — genuinely rare in real-world code.

title: Handle TypedValue in _format_val verbose logging
description: After red-dragon-132, register_writes/var_writes contain TypedValue objects. _format_val in run.py needs to handle TypedValue for verbose logging output.

title: Simplify apply_update after all handlers produce TypedValue
description: Final cleanup: once red-dragon-132 is complete and all handlers produce TypedValue directly, apply_update becomes a thin store-directly function. Remove the legacy deserialize/coerce/wrap path.

title: Migrate heap_writes values to TypedValue
description: Extend TypedValue wrapping to HeapObject.fields and heap_writes in StateUpdate. Depends on red-dragon-132 (handlers produce TypedValue) completing first. Previously tracked as red-dragon-f6i.

title: Migrate return_value in StateUpdate to TypedValue
description: After red-dragon-132 completes (handlers produce TypedValue in register_writes/var_writes), migrate return_value to TypedValue as well. Currently consumed raw by _handle_return_flow in run.py.

description: Optional: resolve overloads at lowering time when frontend has full visibility (intra-class calls). Emit CALL_METHOD targeting a specific label. Avoids runtime resolution for the common case. Depends on execution-time resolution being complete and tested.

title: VM builtin: string/array slicing for Rust (a[1..3])

title: VM builtin: string/array slicing for Python (a[1:3], a[::2])
description: Python frontend emits CALL_FUNCTION('slice', start, stop, step) for subscript slices. Currently goes symbolic. Need VM builtin handling start/stop/step for strings, lists, and heap arrays.

title: VM builtin: string/list slicing for Ruby (a[1..3], a[1,2])
description: Ruby supports range-based slicing (a[1..3]) and index+length slicing (a[1,2]). Check if frontend emits slice calls and add VM support.

title: VM builtin: string/list slicing for Kotlin (substring, subList)
description: Kotlin uses method calls for slicing (substring, subList, slice). Check if these are lowered and add VM builtin support.

title: VM builtin: slice expression for Go (a[low:high])
description: Go frontend emits CALL_FUNCTION('slice', obj, low, high) for slice expressions. Currently goes symbolic. Need VM builtin for 2-arg slice (start, end) on strings, slices, and heap arrays.

title: Method chaining fully concrete across all OOP-capable languages
description:
Track remaining languages where method chaining (obj.method().method()) still produces symbolic results instead of concrete values. Currently 11/14 class-capable languages work. Remaining 3 are blocked by independent issues:
- C++: stack construction doesn't trigger constructor (red-dragon-ng8)
- Lua: table-based OOP not tracked by VM (red-dragon-0vp)  
- Scala: implicit return from expression blocks (red-dragon-2j7)

This issue is resolved when all three blockers are fixed and the _CHAINING_SYMBOLIC_LANGUAGES skip list in test_rosetta_method_chaining.py is empty.

title: Lua table-based OOP method chaining produces symbolic results
description: Lua has no native class system. The Rosetta method chaining test uses a table-based OOP pattern (Counter.new(), Counter.increment(self)) with explicit self-passing. This pattern produces symbolic results because the VM doesn't recognise the table-as-object convention. The Lua frontend would need to detect self-returning table methods and track the table identity through the call chain. This may be a lower priority since Lua's OOP is purely conventional, not language-level.

title: C++ stack-constructed objects don't trigger implicit constructor call
description: When C++ declares an object on the stack (e.g. `Counter counter;`), no constructor is invoked. Unlike `new Counter()` which emits NEW_OBJECT + CALL_METHOD __init__, stack construction has no equivalent lowering. This means field initializers are never applied and method chaining on stack-constructed objects produces symbolic results. The frontend needs to detect variable declarations whose type is a known class and emit an implicit constructor call.
close_reason: Closed

title: Scala array indexing emits CALL_FUNCTION instead of LOAD_INDEX
description: Scala array access (e.g. arr(0)) is lowered as CALL_FUNCTION instead of LOAD_INDEX, because Scala's tree-sitter grammar represents array indexing as a function call. This causes the Rosetta array_accumulate test to skip for Scala. The frontend should detect the array-access pattern and emit LOAD_INDEX instead.

title: PHP lower_php_enum_case uses bare constants.PARAM_SELF in STORE_FIELD
description: In interpreter/frontends/php/declarations.py, lower_php_enum_case emits STORE_FIELD with constants.PARAM_SELF ('self') as the object operand instead of using a register from LOAD_VAR. This is the same bare-string pattern that was fixed for property declarations in the B2 field initializer work. The enum case should either LOAD_VAR the enum object or use the class-level register.

title: Lua method chaining: dynamic table initialization not tracked through VM
description: Lua models objects as tables created in factory functions (local self = {count = 0}), lowered as NEW_OBJECT + STORE_INDEX. Method calls are positional (Counter.increment(counter)) not method syntax. The VM can't track heap objects through dynamic table initialization and function-based dispatch. This is an architectural limitation — no class/constructor mechanism for Lua to hook into. Unlike the C++/PHP bugs, this requires deeper VM support for table-based OOP patterns. See _CHAINING_SYMBOLIC_LANGUAGES in test_rosetta_method_chaining.py.

title: Wrap remaining raw class_name strings in ScalarType() at seed call sites
description: ~25 call sites across 10 OOP frontends still pass raw class_name strings (from ctx.node_text()) to seed_register_type, seed_param_type, seed_var_type. These should be wrapped in ScalarType(class_name) to complete the string→TypeExpr migration. Covers this/self seeding and cast expression handlers in JS, Java, C#, C++, Kotlin, Scala, PHP, and expression files.

title: Migrate existing seed call sites from string to TypeExpr
description: ~100 existing call sites across 15 frontends pass raw strings to seed_register_type, seed_var_type, and seed_param_type. Migrate them to pass TypeExpr objects directly (ScalarType, metatype, etc.) instead of going through parse_type() roundtrips. The seed methods already accept str | TypeExpr, so this is incremental.

title: Method overload resolution via subtype matching
Assignment compatibility for reference types
description:
TypeResolver.resolve_assignment() currently only handles primitive coercions (Int→Float, Bool→Int). For reference types, Animal a = new Dog() should be a no-op (no coercion needed) when Dog <: Animal via is_subtype_expr().

Currently this case is handled implicitly (VM doesn't coerce objects), but explicit subtype checking would enable:
- Compile-time warnings for incompatible assignments
- Covariant container checks (List<Dog> assignable to List<Animal> with proper variance)

Lower priority than type narrowing — the VM works without this today.

title: Method chaining: return-this produces symbolic for 7 languages
description: Method chaining (return this/self) produces symbolic results for C++, C#, Java, Kotlin, Lua, PHP, Scala. The VM doesn't resolve 'return this' back to the object reference correctly in these frontends. Discovered during method chaining Rosetta test.

title: Rosetta: string length test
description: Dropped: VM can't resolve len()/strlen()/.length/.len()/#s/Length() to concrete values for most languages (only Python, Go, Lua work). Needs built-in function resolution in VM executor.

description: Skipped by user. Default parameter binding produces symbolic results for most languages during VM execution. Needs VM-level support for default param resolution.
