Also ~120 defensive None checks (systemic/low) and magic strings (incremental). See memory/code_review_audit1.md.

title: COBOL: SET <88-condition-name> TO FALSE unsupported (warns and skips)
description:
SET <88-condition-name> TO FALSE is not implemented: _set_condition_name in interpreter/cobol/lower_arithmetic.py (~L1341) logs 'SET %s TO FALSE unsupported (no FALSE value captured) — skipping' and emits nothing, leaving the parent field unchanged.

SEMANTICS: SET cond TO FALSE writes the level-88's FALSE phrase value (88 NAME VALUE x WHEN SET TO FALSE IS y) into the parent. Without a captured FALSE value the writeback target is genuinely ambiguous, hence the skip.

REMEDIATION:
1. Capture the 88 'WHEN SET TO FALSE IS' value in the ConditionNameIndex/ConditionValue (bridge + Python) when present.
2. Lower SET cond TO FALSE to write that value into the parent (mirror of the TO TRUE path in _set_condition_name).
3. If no FALSE value is declared, keep the warn-and-skip (that is the correct conservative behavior — never guess).
4. Integration test through run(): 88 with explicit WHEN SET TO FALSE value, SET TO FALSE flips the parent.

Found in COBOL language-coverage audit 2026-06-22. Sibling of the (now closed) red-dragon-0sq2 SET TO TRUE work.
notes:
INVESTIGATION 2026-06-23 — NOT an easy win; gated on a ProLeap grammar fork + ~0 real-world demand.

KEY FINDINGS:
1. The STATEMENT 'SET <88> TO FALSE' parses fine (bridge emits {type:SET, set_type:TO, values:[FALSE]}) and reaches _set_condition_name, which correctly warn-skips because there is no false-value to write.
2. The VALUE SOURCE for a false write is the level-88 'WHEN SET TO FALSE IS <lit>' phrase. ProLeap's grammar does NOT parse it — verified hard syntax error 'mismatched input WHEN' in dataDescriptionEntryFormat3 (the 88 rule only allows conditionName + dataValueClause; ValueClause exposes only getValueIntervals(), ValueInterval only from/to). No ASG metamodel element for the FALSE phrase either.
3. Standard COBOL forbids SET <88> TO FALSE unless the 88 declares the FALSE phrase, so without it there is no conformant value — the current warn-skip is correct (and per project policy we do NOT guess a value, e.g. a sibling 88 or 'any non-matching value').

SO: TO FALSE is NOT symmetric with TO TRUE. TO TRUE's source (the VALUE clause) is always present; TO FALSE's source (the FALSE phrase) is grammar-blocked.

PREVALENCE (scanned 7745 COBOL files locally — CardDemo, LASer, SPICS, NIST-85, GnuCOBOL tests, etc.):
- SET ... TO TRUE: 4279 occurrences (baseline, ubiquitous)
- SET ... TO FALSE statement: 0
- WHEN SET TO FALSE IS declaration: 0

SCOPE IF EVER DONE (medium/large, in order):
A. Fork proleap-cobol-parser grammar to accept 'WHEN SET TO FALSE IS literal' on format-3 (88) entries; regenerate ANTLR; add an ASG element exposing the false value.
B. Bridge: serialize the false value onto the condition (e.g. condObj 'false_value').
C. Python: add false_val to ConditionName/ConditionValue (condition_name.py); 
D. Lowering: _set_condition_name FALSE branch writes false_val into the parent (mirror of the TO TRUE path). Easy once A-C exist; dead until then.

RECOMMENDATION: keep P3 / won't-fix-cheaply. Demand is nil. Re-scoped from the original 'just capture the FALSE value' framing, which assumed the parser already surfaced it.

title: Regression coverage for broad name=[null] recovery (fail-loud on unmodelled operands)
description: Follow-up to red-dragon-o8uq. The bridge fix (StatementSerializer.extractCallName) now recovers the verbatim source name for ALL operands where ProLeap's Call.getName() is null (previously all collapsed to the 'name=[null]' placeholder, which lower_arithmetic._SPECIAL_REGISTER_NAMES dropped). Decision (2026-06-20, user): keep the BROAD recovery (fail-loud) rather than narrowing to RETURN-CODE only. Consequence: a previously-silently-dropped nameless MOVE target that now surfaces by a real name which is neither a DATA DIVISION field nor in the skip-list will raise KeyError in resolve_field_ref at lowering time (program fails to compile) instead of being dropped. Known-safe today: RETURN-CODE resolves to its region; SORT-RETURN/TALLY still drop by real name; full COBOL sweep green. Work: (1) add a characterization/regression test documenting the fail-loud behavior for an unmodelled nameless MOVE target (assert a clear lowering error), and confirm RETURN-CODE/SORT-RETURN/TALLY behave as expected; (2) optionally sweep the COBOL corpus for any program that now fails to lower due to a newly-surfaced nameless operand, and either model or skip-list it; (3) consider whether the 'name=[null]' skip-list entry is now dead and should be removed or documented as a fallback; (4) minor cleanup: tests/integration/test_cobol_return_code.py reads WS via list(vm.region_keys())[0] (positional/brittle vs alloc order) — make it robust via a stable handle. Multi-token nameless constructs surface only their first token (ctx.getStart().getText()) — note as a known limitation.
acceptance_criteria: A regression test pins the fail-loud behavior for an unmodelled nameless MOVE target; RETURN-CODE/SORT-RETURN/TALLY paths covered; the brittle positional WS read in test_cobol_return_code.py is replaced with a stable lookup.

title: DISPLAY of signed zoned numeric (S9(n)V99) emits stripped value, not zoned digits
description: Found by spike running real batch CBACT01C. DISPLAY ACCT-CURR-BAL (PIC S9(10)V99, value 100.00) emits '100' instead of the zoned USAGE DISPLAY representation real mainframes emit ('0000000100' with implied V / sign overpunch). Affects batch report/log fidelity where DISPLAY output is compared or captured. Lower priority than write-path bugs.
acceptance_criteria: DISPLAY of a DISPLAY-usage signed numeric emits the zoned digit string (leading zeros, implied decimal, sign handling) matching mainframe behavior.

title: Implement USE FOR DEBUGGING declaratives
description: Out-of-scope sibling of red-dragon-m0oa.4 (USE AFTER ERROR/EXCEPTION). The NIST corpus uses USE FOR DEBUGGING (~40 occurrences) — a distinct debug-line declarative with different trigger semantics (debug lines / DECLARATIVES on procedure references), accessed via ProLeap UseStatement.getUseDebugStatement(). The bridge currently SKIPS it explicitly (AsgSerializer gates on getUseDebugStatement()==null) and the Python side ignores it. Implementing it requires: bridge serialization of the debug targets, ASG modelling, and a runtime trigger tied to debug-line execution (likely gated on a WITH DEBUGGING MODE / debug switch). Separate trigger model from error/exception declaratives.
acceptance_criteria: USE FOR DEBUGGING declaratives serialized, modelled, and fired per COBOL-85 debug semantics; gated by debugging mode; does not affect USE AFTER ERROR/EXCEPTION behavior.

title: NIST COBOL programs raise OverflowError deep in execution (>150k steps)
description:
Surfaced by the NIST CCVS tracer (scripts/nist_ccvs_tracer.py) at its 400k step cap, 2026-06-18: 4 programs aborted with '<run aborted: OverflowError>'.

CHARACTERIZATION:
- Does NOT occur within 150,000 steps (a full-corpus runtime scan at max_steps=150_000 found zero OverflowError / zero exceptions). It appears only deep in long-running execution (between ~150k and 400k steps).
- Strongly correlated with the heavy/runaway programs (the ones that never reach END OF TEST and write huge record counts, e.g. IX102A, SQ201M/208M/209M/210M). Likely the same non-terminating loops driving a counter/field value upward until a numeric op exceeds Python's float range.

LIKELY CAUSE: COBOL numeric handling converts a reconstructed value to float (e.g. digit*10**power summation in the zoned/display decode, or an arithmetic op) and OverflowError fires when the value (or 10**power for a large/garbage field width) exceeds ~1e308. A runaway loop feeds it an ever-growing or corrupted magnitude.

REPRO: run scripts/nist_ccvs_tracer.py over the corpus at max_steps>=400_000 (the trace() helper catches and records the abort); or run the implicated heavy programs directly at 400k+ and catch OverflowError to get the exact traceback location.

FIX DIRECTION: (a) identify the exact arithmetic/decode site and guard against out-of-range magnitudes (COBOL fields are bounded by their PIC; a value exceeding the field width indicates an upstream bug — fail or truncate per COBOL size-error semantics rather than building an unbounded Python int->float). (b) the runaway loops themselves are tracked separately (red-dragon-m0oa.7 incomplete bucket).

Related: red-dragon-m0oa / m0oa.7 (NIST conformance, runaway-output programs).

title: PERFORM VARYING over an OCCURS DEPENDING index (ODO-IX) not registered in the data layout
description:
Surfaced by the NIST CCVS tracer lowering scan, 2026-06-18.

SYMPTOM: lowering SQ214A logs:
  'unresolvable field reference ODO-IX — emitting sentinel'
  'VARYING variable ODO-IX not found in layout'
A PERFORM VARYING uses ODO-IX as its loop/index variable, but ODO-IX is not present in the materialised data layout, so the VARYING lowering can't resolve it (emits a sentinel) — the varying loop then won't iterate correctly.

PROGRAM: SQ214A (sequential, OCCURS DEPENDING ON area).

ROOT CAUSE (hypothesis): ODO-IX is an index/subscript associated with an OCCURS DEPENDING ON table; the data-layout build doesn't register it as an addressable field (indexes/ODO control vars may be omitted from the layout), so PERFORM VARYING ODO-IX FROM ... can't find it. Confirm whether ODO-IX is declared as an index (INDEXED BY) or a WS counter; either way the VARYING lowering needs it resolvable.

FIX DIRECTION: ensure VARYING loop variables (incl. OCCURS index/ODO control names) are registered in the layout / resolvable by the VARYING lowering. Add a lowering+exec test: PERFORM VARYING <idx> over an OCCURS DEPENDING table iterates the right number of times.

Related: red-dragon-m0oa (NIST file I/O), CobolFeature.PERFORM_VARYING_AFTER / OCCURS_DEPENDING_ON.

title: COBOL bridge serializes an unresolved field reference as the literal 'name=[null]'
description:
Surfaced by the NIST CCVS tracer (scripts/nist_ccvs_tracer.py) lowering scan, 2026-06-18.

SYMPTOM: lowering these programs logs (repeatedly):
  'unresolvable field reference name=[null] — emitting sentinel'
i.e. a field-reference operand arrives at condition/expression lowering with the name literally 'name=[null]' — the Java bridge's toString of an object whose name field is null. The Python side (interpreter/cobol/condition_lowering.py _lower_expr_dict, ~L733) then can't resolve it and emits a sentinel.

PROGRAMS (lowering-time, independent of runtime input): SQ201M, SQ208M, SQ209M, SQ210M (the SQ2xxM sequential M-stub series).

ROOT CAUSE (hypothesis): a bridge serializer path (StatementSerializer) emits a reference whose data-name Call is null/unresolved and stringifies the object instead of producing a proper {'kind':'ref','name':...} (or emitting a literal/sentinel). Need to find which construct in SQ201M triggers it (likely a specific I/O or data-name form in the M-stub driver code).

NOTE: distinct from red-dragon-m0oa.5 (M-stubs need external input data) — this is a lowering/serialization defect that fires before execution. May contribute to the SQ2xxM runaway-output behavior.

FIX DIRECTION: in the bridge, when a referenced data-name Call is null/unresolvable, emit a proper sentinel/text node (not the object toString); on the Python side, the sentinel handling already exists. Add a lowering test from the offending SQ201M construct once isolated.

title: Add BYTES to FoundationTypeName for binary-buffer IR constants
description:
## Why this issue exists

Cicada's CICS lowering emits `Const` IR nodes carrying Python `bytes` payloads for COMMAREA fallbacks (`b""`) and VSAM key/record fallbacks. These payloads flow through the CICS builtins as `bytes(args[i].value)` and into the dispatcher as `bytearray(context.commarea)` — both calls require actual bytes objects, not strings.

The typed-IR API introduced in 763fc20c requires `type_expr` on every `Const`. Because `FoundationTypeName` has no BYTES entry (only STRING, INT, FLOAT, BOOL, NULL, OBJECT, ARRAY…), the only honest option right now is `type_expr=UNKNOWN`, which is a gap rather than a real type.

## What needs to be done

Add `BYTES = TypeName("Bytes")` to `FoundationTypeName` and a corresponding `Const.bytes_` factory classmethod, so binary-buffer constants can carry a proper static type instead of UNKNOWN.

## How to apply

Once available, cicada's `cics/strategy.py` can replace `Const(result_reg=r, value=b"", type_expr=UNKNOWN)` with `Const.bytes_(r, b"")` at the four COMMAREA/VSAM-key/record fallback sites.

title: Class-scope attribution relies on current_class_name bleed + flattened-method IR (Java/C#/Scala)
description:
Discovered during red-dragon-b4j6 (scope-stack inference).

PROBLEM: In type inference (interpreter/types/type_inference.py), method->class attribution depends on current_class_name being set at a class-entry label and NEVER reset (a 'bleed'). Two coupled issues:

1. Bleed onto following code: after a class, current_class_name still points at that class, so a top-level function emitted after a class body can be mis-attributed as a method of that class.

2. Flattened-method IR: Java, C#, and Scala frontends emit an EMPTY class body and lower methods FLATTENED AFTER end_class (i.e. class_M, end_class_M, then func_add...end_add at top level). Python (and others) nest methods INSIDE the class label range (class_C, func_m...end_m, end_class_C). The flattened frontends RELY on the bleed to attribute their methods, because positional nesting would place those methods outside the class.

WHY b4j6 left it: the scope-stack work fixed function scope (branched returns + variable bleed) by making current_func_label a stack-derived property. Unifying class scope into the same stack (pop at end_class) breaks flattened-method attribution for Java/C#/Scala (14 regressions). So current_class_name was kept as the original bled field; class behavior is byte-for-byte unchanged from before. See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-18-scope-stack-inference-design.md (status note) and the b4j6 merge (main 763fc20c).

PRINCIPLED FIX: normalize the Java/C#/Scala (and audit all other) frontends so methods are lowered NESTED inside the class label range (class_X ... func_method ... end_method ... end_class_X), like Python. Then class scope can join the scope stack (push on class entry, pop on end_class), eliminating the bleed and fixing the top-level-after-class mis-attribution. This touches frontend lowering (declarations) for those languages + likely VM/linker validation, and will have its own expectation churn. Alternative (less principled): derive method->owning-class from the symbol table rather than positional nesting.

Scope check needed: confirm whether the bleed-driven mis-attribution (issue 1) actually affects any real analysis output today, to prioritize.

title: Branched-return functions are orphaned from return-type inference (_infer_label clears current_func_label on control-flow labels)
description:
Discovered during red-dragon-x78r (visible Union[T,Null] nullability).

PROBLEM: _infer_label (interpreter/types/type_inference.py) sets current_func_label only on the func_<name> entry label and clears it to "" on EVERY subsequent non-function label — including intra-body control-flow labels (if_true_*, if_end_*, loop labels). It is never restored. So as soon as any if/for/while appears in a function body, current_func_label becomes "" for the rest of the body, and _infer_return bails at its first guard (if not ctx.current_func_label: return).

CONSEQUENCE: functions with branched/multiple returns get NO inferred return type (UnknownType). This bounds nullability visibility: e.g. 'def f(c):\n if c: return 1\n return None' should infer Union[Int, Null] but infers nothing. Only single-return functions with no preceding control-flow label get a return type.

WHY NOT FIXED IN x78r: current_func_label is shared variable-scoping infrastructure (store_var_type / lookup_var_type at ~line 229/239 use it as the scope key; param-type collection in _infer_symbolic at ~514-525 also uses it). Changing when it is cleared would silently alter variable scoping and param inference repo-wide — too risky/broad for the nullability feature. Deferred deliberately.

FIX DIRECTION: track function scope correctly through control flow (e.g. set on is_function() entry, clear only at the matching function end label / class boundary — NOT on ordinary control-flow labels), then re-validate the whole type-inference + var-scoping test surface and absorb the (likely large) expectation churn. Consider a scope stack rather than a single current_func_label string.

Once fixed, branched-return functions will correctly infer Union[...] return types, completing the return-type half of the union-based nullability model.

comments.author: avishek-sen-gupta
comments.text:
FIXED (function-scope) — merged to main 2026-06-18 (merge 763fc20c, branch scope-stack-inference).

current_func_label is now a computed property over a scope_stack that pushes on function/class entry and pops on the matching end (asserting _pop_frame, no defensive no-op), leaving control-flow labels alone, reset per fixpoint pass. Result across all 15 frontends:
- Branched-return functions now infer return types (incl. Union[T,Null]) — completes the return-type half of the union-based nullability model (red-dragon-x78r).
- Local variables no longer bleed to global scope; same-named locals in different functions stay independent.
Suite: 14255 passed, 66 skipped, 15 xfailed (independently verified green).

SCOPE NOTE: This ticket's stated bug (current_func_label cleared on control-flow labels → branched-return orphaning + var bleed) is fully resolved. The parallel CLASS-scope issue (current_class_name bleed + Java/C#/Scala flattened-method IR) turned out to be load-bearing for those frontends' method attribution, so class behavior was kept unchanged. Filed separately as red-dragon-adam.

Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-18-scope-stack-inference-design.md. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-18-scope-stack-inference.md.

title: Explore moving nullability to option-style (Option<T>/Maybe) vs union-based Null
description:
DISCUSSION/SPIKE (requested 2026-06-16). After the typed-Const refactor
(red-dragon-v0l2) lands, discuss the ramifications of moving the type system from
union-based nullability (current: Optional[T] = Union[T, Null], type_expr.py
optional_of) toward an option-style model (Option<T>/Maybe<T> sum type, no bare
null value) as in Rust/Haskell/ML/Swift.

To cover in the discussion:
- What changes in TypeExpr (a parameterized Option type vs Union[T, Null]).
- VM/runtime representation of None and how _handle_const's Null literal maps
  (Some/None constructors vs a Null value).
- Blast radius across 15 frontends (most have null/nil literals) and coercion.
- Migration/compat: can it be incremental, or is it a hard cutover?
- Whether it's worth it vs the mainstream union-based model we already have.

This is a DISCUSSION first (brainstorm), not yet committed work. Prerequisite:
red-dragon-v0l2 (typed Const) done, since it makes the null literal's type explicit.

comments.author: avishek-sen-gupta
comments.text:
DONE — Model A implemented & merged to main (merge 6dbef4c0, branch option1-nullability), 2026-06-18.

Decision: union-based nullability via the general type system (null = ordinary scalar; nullable T = Union[T, Null]); option-style (Option<T>) rejected as it mismatches 14/15 source languages and is the model that risks LOSING the variable's type. Decision memo: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-17-nullability-model-decision.md. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-17-option1-nullability.md.

Shipped (7 staged tasks, TDD, ~19 commits, all via real pre-commit hooks):
- Return_.implicit flag + emit_implicit_return helper; all synthetic fall-off-the-end returns across all 15 frontends marked implicit.
- _infer_return: skip implicit returns; union explicit return types; annotation-wins preserved via new _seeded_func_return_labels (mirrors _seeded_var_names).
- Removed the null->UNKNOWN shim in _infer_const: nullability now visible. Verified: inferred 'x="hi"; x=None' -> Union[Null, String]; annotated 'String s="hi"; s=null' -> String (type retained either way — the user's original concern).
- Deleted dead is_optional/unwrap_optional helpers (kept optional()).
Final suite: 14249 passed, 66 skipped, 15 xfailed (CardDemo CICS e2e green).

Known limitation filed as red-dragon-b4j6: branched-return functions are orphaned from return-type inference (pre-existing _infer_label var-scoping behaviour; intentionally not changed here).

title: Wire USE AFTER STANDARD ERROR/EXCEPTION procedures to fire on I/O errors
description:
Follow-up to the DECLARATIVES entry-point fix (red-dragon-m0oa.3). Once declaratives
sections are identified and excluded from normal flow, implement their actual semantics:
a USE AFTER STANDARD ERROR/EXCEPTION PROCEDURE ON <file> must be invoked when a matching
I/O error occurs on that file, then control returns per COBOL rules.

Requires:
- Bridge to serialize the USE clause (mode: ERROR/EXCEPTION, GIVING/file targets).
- Runtime dispatch: on an I/O verb returning a non-success status for a file with a
  registered USE procedure, perform that procedure before continuing.

This is the fuller correctness story; genuine error-recovery NIST tests depend on it.
Not needed for the 9 entry-point-only programs in red-dragon-m0oa.3.

Related: red-dragon-m0oa (epic), red-dragon-m0oa.3 (prerequisite),
red-dragon-m0oa.1 (SQ152A/SQ155A check status directly; may not need USE).
notes: DONE 2026-06-19 (branch use-declaratives, 7 commits 8644f06d..231bdf3b). Implemented USE AFTER STANDARD ERROR/EXCEPTION via lowering-time injection, no core-VM change: bridge serializes the use clause; ASG UseClause on CobolSection; EmitContext registry (use_by_file/use_by_mode/use_global) built in lower_procedure; emit_use_trigger in lower_io fires the matching declarative section (named-file -> GLOBAL -> open-mode) when an I/O verb returns a non-0x status and the statement has no explicit AT END/INVALID KEY clause; open-mode resolved at runtime via new builtin __cobol_file_open_mode + RealFileIOProvider mode tracking. 6 integration tests (named-file/GLOBAL/open-mode/multi-file fire, explicit-AT-END suppresses, no-USE no-op) + bridge+ASG unit tests; full suite + 262-test COBOL sweep green; final whole-branch review READY TO MERGE. CONFORMANCE: NIST -m nist = 36 pass (flat vs ~37 baseline at program level); SQ103A SEQ-TEST-GF-10 'DECLARATIVE NOT EXECUTED' resolved (verified via nist_ccvs_tracer) but SQ103A still fails on CLOSE-status assertions. Follow-ups: m0oa.8 (wire USE to CLOSE errors), m0oa.9 (USE FOR DEBUGGING, out of scope).

title: COBOL: parse_pic raises ValueError on B / 0 / slash insertion edit pictures
description:
interpreter/cobol/pic_parser.parse_pic raises 'ValueError: Cannot parse PIC clause' for numeric edit pictures whose only editing symbols are simple insertion B (blank), 0 (zero), or / (slash) — e.g. a date edit '99/99/99' or '9(5)BB9'.

Mechanism:
- edit_picture.is_numeric_edited() only treats Z, +, -, ',', '.' as edit symbols. A picture like 99/99/99 or 9(5)BB9 contains none of those, so is_numeric_edited() returns False.
- parse_pic therefore falls through to the Lark grammar, which %ignores only space, ',', '.', '-', '+' — NOT '/', 'B', or '0'. The grammar hits UnexpectedInput and parse_pic re-raises as ValueError.

So instead of producing a NUMERIC_EDITED descriptor, the whole field (and program) fails to parse — a hard crash, not a wrong value.

Impact: latent. AWS CardDemo stores dates as X(08) (e.g. WS-TRAN-DATE PIC X(08) VALUE '00/00/00'), not as edited 99/99/99, and uses no B/0/slash numeric-edit pictures — confirmed by grep of cpy/cbl/cpy-bms. Correctness gap for general COBOL only.

Fix: recognise B / 0 / / as edit symbols in is_numeric_edited (so these route through edit_picture, sized by full char width), and handle them in format_edited (B -> space, 0 -> '0', / -> '/'; all simple insertion). Alternatively, teach the Lark grammar to size them. Prefer the edit_picture route for consistency with the other insertion handling.

Introduced/exposed by the numeric edit-picture feature (commit 42ab57a0); the pre-existing Lark grammar already could not parse these, but they now also escape is_numeric_edited detection.

title: COBOL: numeric edit-picture formatter mis-formats unsupported symbols ($ * CR DB floating sign)
description:
interpreter/cobol/edit_picture.py format_edited implements only the AWS CardDemo subset of numeric editing: fixed leading/trailing sign (+/-), Z zero-suppression, comma and decimal-point insertion. Pictures containing OTHER edit symbols are SIZED correctly but FORMATTED wrong.

Mechanism:
- is_numeric_edited() returns True for these pictures because they contain '.' and/or ',' (e.g. $$$,$$9.99, ****9.99, ZZ9.99CR), so parse_pic produces a NUMERIC_EDITED descriptor with the correct character width (countEditedWidth in the bridge / parse_edit_picture().width count every position).
- But format_edited()'s template walk has no case for floating/fixed currency ($), check-protection (*), or CR/DB. They hit the 'else: out.append(sym)' fallback and are emitted VERBATIM. Worse, $ and * are NOT counted as digit positions (only 9/Z are in parse_edit_picture), so the numeric value is also mis-placed.

Example: PIC $$$,$$9.99 with value 1.23 -> '$$$,$$1.23' instead of the correct floating '    $1.23'.

Impact: latent. No current consumer (AWS CardDemo) uses these symbols as numeric-edited PICs — they appear only in VALUE literals (VALUE '$', VALUE ALL '*') and X(n) fields. Correctness gap for general COBOL only.

Fix: extend format_edited to handle floating sign/currency ($$$, ++++, ----), * check-protection, and CR/DB trailing sign; count $/*/floating-sign positions as digit positions in parse_edit_picture. See the 'NOT supported' docstring list in edit_picture.py.

Introduced by the numeric edit-picture feature (commit 42ab57a0).

title: Copybook-not-found error scrapes ProLeap message with regex; wrap and raise instead
description: interpreter/cobol/cobol_parser.py:69 uses re.search(r'Could not find copy book (\S+)', msg) to extract the missing copybook name from ProLeap's error string for an enriched CobolParseError. No need to scrape the external tool's message: just wrap the underlying error and raise (include the copybook search dirs + the raw cause), without regex-extracting a name out of a string we don't own. Retire the re.search. Small.
close_reason: Closed

title: PIC re-parsed from string in Python+Java; emit structured picture from the bridge
description: Structure->string->reparse for PICTURE clauses. ProLeap has the structured picture (PictureString); proleap-bridge DataFieldSerializer.extractPic flattens it to a string (e.g. 'S9(8)'), which is then re-parsed TWICE: in Java (DataFieldSerializer.computePicByteLength) and in Python (interpreter/cobol/pic_parser.py via an ANTLR CobolDataTypes grammar PLUS a regex _NUMBEROF_PATTERN = \((\d+)\) for repeat counts). Desired end state: the bridge emits structured picture fields (digit count, decimal places, sign, editing, usage) directly from ProLeap's picture model; Python consumes structure; retire _NUMBEROF_PATTERN and the PIC-string re-parse. Same anti-pattern as red-dragon-6ddr (field refs), different construct.
close_reason: Closed

title: Structure subscript interiors (recursive subscript expressions, retire remaining string form)
description: Follow-on to the structured-subscripts work (red-dragon-6ddr partial / spec 2026-06-09-structured-subscripts-design.md). That change carries subscripts as a structured LIST but each subscript ELEMENT remains a string (e.g. 'WS-IDX', '5', 'WS-I + 1', nested 'B(C)'), resolved by the existing per-subscript path. This issue: model each subscript interior structurally (an expression/reference node, recursively, like ref_mod.py's RefModExpr union), so there is NO string form anywhere in a subscript: literal vs data-name vs arithmetic (WS-I + 1) vs nested subscript (B(C)) carried as typed nodes from the ProLeap bridge + CICS parser through resolve_field_ref. Bridge: serialize each Subscript's value-statement as a structured node instead of extractValueStmtText. CICS parser: vnested already structured; hand over nodes. Depends on the structured-subscripts list change landing first.
close_reason: Closed

title: COMPUTE arithmetic serialized as text, re-parsed by _TOKEN_RE (structure->string->reparse)
description: The ProLeap bridge serializes COMPUTE expressions as a flat text formula (e.g. 'WS-A + WS-B * 2'); interpreter/cobol/cobol_expression.py re-tokenizes it with _TOKEN_RE (line 26) and a recursive-descent parser to rebuild the expression tree the bridge already parsed. This is the structure->string->regex re-parse anti-pattern for arithmetic, parallel to (but distinct from) red-dragon-6ddr which is about field references. _TOKEN_RE also special-cases subscripted refs WS-TBL(IDX) as single tokens. Fix: bridge emits a structured arithmetic expression tree (operands+operators); cobol_expression consumes structure; retire _TOKEN_RE. Distinct subsystem from 6ddr; larger; filed separately.
close_reason: Closed

title: COMPUTE arithmetic emitted as text string, re-parsed by regex (structure->string->reparse)
description: The ProLeap bridge serializes COMPUTE expressions as a flat text formula (e.g. 'WS-A + WS-B * 2'); interpreter/cobol/cobol_expression.py then re-tokenizes it with _TOKEN_RE and a recursive-descent parser to rebuild the expression tree the bridge already had. This is the structure->string->regex re-parse anti-pattern for arithmetic (parallel to the subscript-flattening one). _TOKEN_RE also special-cases subscripted refs WS-TBL(IDX) as single tokens. Fix: have the bridge emit a structured arithmetic expression tree (operands + operators) instead of a string; consume structure in cobol_expression; retire _TOKEN_RE. Larger than the subscript work; filed separately as agreed.

title: Re-enable gaps-doc + beads-export as verify-not-mutate pre-commit hooks
description:
The feature-coverage-gaps and beads-backup pre-commit hooks were disabled (commented out in .pre-commit-config.yaml, commit de06e9b7) because they rewrite tracked files mid-commit (docs/frontend-lowering-gaps.md datestamp and issues/issues.jsonl), which the pre-commit framework treats as 'files were modified by this hook' and aborts the commit intermittently.

Re-enable them using the verify-don't-mutate pattern (Option 1 from the 2026-06-06 discussion) so they are mandatory but never mutate the tree:
- gaps-doc: run feature_coverage_audit.py in a --check mode that exits non-zero if docs/frontend-lowering-gaps.md is stale vs freshly-generated (do NOT regenerate+git add in the hook).
- beads: 'bd export --all' to a temp file, diff against issues/issues.jsonl, fail if they differ (do NOT overwrite+git add in the hook).
- Add a 'make regen' (or equivalent) target that performs both regenerations + git add, so the one-step fix when a check fails is trivial.

Alternatives considered: (2) a commit wrapper script outside the framework — easy to bypass; (3) a core.hooksPath shim that runs generators then delegates to the framework — fully automatic but most moving parts.
acceptance_criteria: Both hooks re-enabled in .pre-commit-config.yaml as validators that fail on stale/diverged generated artifacts and never modify the working tree. A 'make regen' (or documented one-liner) regenerates+stages both. A normal 'git commit' with up-to-date artifacts succeeds without abort; a commit with stale artifacts fails with a clear message pointing to 'make regen'.
notes: Discovered 2026-06-06 while committing the 4q25.42 design; the mutating hooks repeatedly aborted commits. feature_coverage_audit.py may already support a check mode — verify before adding one.

title: COBOL ref_mod: UNSTRING INTO targets — WS-FIELD(start:length) as receiving field in UNSTRING
description: Reference modification on UNSTRING INTO receiving fields is not implemented. COBOL allows UNSTRING WS-SRC DELIMITED SPACE INTO WS-A(2:3) WS-B, writing each split part into a substring of the target field (write-back via __string_splice). The Java bridge serializeUnstring() serializes INTO targets as plain strings. The Python UnstringStatement.into is a list[str]. The lowering writes the full field unconditionally.
acceptance_criteria: Java bridge serializes each INTO target as a ref-mod-aware operand object. UnstringStatement.into becomes list[RefModOperand]. Lowering applies __string_splice when ref_mod is present on an INTO target. Integration test: UNSTRING WS-SRC DELIMITED SPACE INTO WS-A(2:3), assert correct bytes written into substring range of WS-A.

title: SUBTRACT CORRESPONDING ON SIZE ERROR: overflow detection not implemented
description: SUBTRACT CORRESPONDING (COBOL SUBTRACT ... CORRESPONDING ... FROM ...) has no ON SIZE ERROR / NOT ON SIZE ERROR support. SUBTRACT CORRESPONDING is not yet implemented in the lowering layer; when it is, overflow detection must be included. Each corresponding field pair must be checked against its target type descriptor (signed fields check both bounds; unsigned only upper bound). Overflow OR'd across all pairs before a single BranchIf. StatementSerializer.java serializeSubtract() needs onSizeErrorPhrase/notOnSizeErrorPhrase for the CORRESPONDING variant. Files: interpreter/cobol/lower_arithmetic.py (lower_subtract_corresponding, to be created), proleap-bridge/src/main/java/org/reddragon/bridge/StatementSerializer.java. Related: red-dragon-4q25.5 (ArithmeticStatement ON SIZE ERROR pattern), red-dragon-d1w8 (ADD CORRESPONDING ON SIZE ERROR).

title: ADD CORRESPONDING ON SIZE ERROR: overflow detection not implemented
description: ADD CORRESPONDING (COBOL ADD ... CORRESPONDING ... TO ...) has no ON SIZE ERROR / NOT ON SIZE ERROR support. ADD CORRESPONDING is not yet implemented in the lowering layer; when it is, overflow detection must be included. Each corresponding field pair must be checked independently against its target type descriptor, with overflow OR'd across all pairs before branching. StatementSerializer.java serializeAdd() also needs onSizeErrorPhrase/notOnSizeErrorPhrase wired for the CORRESPONDING variant. Files: interpreter/cobol/lower_arithmetic.py (lower_arithmetic_corresponding, to be created), proleap-bridge/src/main/java/org/reddragon/bridge/StatementSerializer.java. Related: red-dragon-4q25.5 (ArithmeticStatement ON SIZE ERROR pattern).

title: SUBTRACT CORRESPONDING ON SIZE ERROR not implemented
description: SUBTRACT CORRESPONDING is not yet implemented in RedDragon. When it is implemented, it must support ON SIZE ERROR / NOT ON SIZE ERROR clauses per COBOL standard. Filed as a tracking issue so the overflow clause is not forgotten when SUBTRACT CORRESPONDING is eventually implemented. Related: red-dragon-4q25.5 (ADD/SUBTRACT/MULTIPLY/DIVIDE ON SIZE ERROR).

title: ADD CORRESPONDING ON SIZE ERROR not implemented
description: ADD CORRESPONDING is not yet implemented in RedDragon. When it is implemented, it must support ON SIZE ERROR / NOT ON SIZE ERROR clauses per COBOL standard. Filed as a tracking issue so the overflow clause is not forgotten when ADD CORRESPONDING is eventually implemented. Related: red-dragon-4q25.5 (ADD/SUBTRACT/MULTIPLY/DIVIDE ON SIZE ERROR).

title: instructions.py: remove redundant result_reg redeclarations in subclasses
description:
File: interpreter/instructions.py
Lines: ~203, 219, 282, 301, 330, 353, 376, 402, 426, 453, 496, 519, 575, 616, 638, 654, 824, 843

After red-dragon-4ei7 (result_reg promoted to InstructionBase), 18 subclasses still explicitly redeclare result_reg: Register = NO_REGISTER. These are now redundant — Python dataclass inheritance deduplicates them. They compile correctly but mislead readers into thinking result_reg is subclass-specific for some classes and not others.

Fix: remove the standalone result_reg: Register = NO_REGISTER line from the ~18 subclasses that have it (Const, LoadVar, Binop, Unop, CallFunction, CallMethod, etc.). Keep result_reg fields that are semantically different (different default or different type).

Note: do NOT remove result_reg from subclasses where it appears inside the compat block — those were already removed in red-dragon-4ei7.

Epic: red-dragon-r32l

title: Phase 3 gate upgrade: typeCheckingMode standard + strip per-file pragmas

title: llm_client: Any in run.py — Optional LLM client injection

title: dict[VarName, Any] in calls.py — value type annotation as TypedValue
description:
File: interpreter/handlers/calls.py:173,235,597
Symbol: new_vars, captured, bindings dict values
Context: These dicts had dict[VarName, Any] replaced with dict[VarName, TypedValue]. The annotation appears correct based on assignments, but the original Any suggests callers may pass raw values. Filed during type annotation pass to confirm TypedValue is the correct bound.

title: idx_val: Any in _infer_index_kind — str | int union
description:
File: interpreter/handlers/memory.py:53
Symbol: idx_val in _infer_index_kind
Context: Parameter accepts str (field name) or int (array index) depending on caller. The str | int annotation is correct for the known cases but TypedValue.value is Any at the call site, so the actual type might be broader. Filed during type annotation pass.

title: pre_triage: Any in TypedValue.value and typed_value.py helpers — raw VM runtime values
description:
File: interpreter/types/typed_value.py
Symbols:
  - TypedValue.value: Any (line 34)
  - runtime_type_name(val: Any) (line 20)
  - typed(value: Any, ...) (line 38)
  - unwrap(val: Any) -> Any (line 43)
  - unwrap_locals(local_vars: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any] (line 50)
  - typed_from_runtime(value: Any) (line 55)
Context: All Any uses here represent raw Python objects flowing through the VM at runtime: int, str, float, bool, list, dict, custom VM objects (Pointer, SymbolicValue, etc.). The union of all possible VM values is very wide. A domain type like VmValue = Union[int, str, float, bool, list, dict, object] could be explored, but the heterogeneous nature makes it a significant effort.
Epic: red-dragon-r32l

title: pre_triage: Callable[[Any], Any] in NullTypeResolver.resolve_assignment — coercion function over raw VM values
description:
File: interpreter/types/null_type_resolver.py
Symbol: NullTypeResolver.resolve_assignment() -> Callable[[Any], Any]
Context: Inherits from TypeResolver. Returns _identity (the identity function), which applies no coercion. Both input and output are raw VM runtime values (int, str, list, etc.). Parallel to red-dragon-isoc in TypeResolver.
Epic: red-dragon-r32l

title: pre_triage: Callable[[Any], Any] in TypeResolver.resolve_assignment — coercion function over raw VM values
description:
File: interpreter/types/type_resolver.py
Symbol: TypeResolver.resolve_assignment() -> Callable[[Any], Any]
Context: The return type is a coercion function applied to raw VM runtime values. Both the input and output are untyped Python objects (int, str, list, custom VM objects). A domain type like CoercionFn = Callable[[object], object] or a Protocol could replace Any here.
Epic: red-dragon-r32l

title: What is the type of a coercer argument? Define RuntimeValue TypeAlias?
description:
File: interpreter/types/coercion/conversion_result.py, interpreter/types/coercion/conversion_rules.py, interpreter/types/coercion/default_conversion_rules.py, interpreter/types/coercion/identity_conversion_rules.py
Symbol: Callable[[Any], Any] — _identity, _to_float, _to_int, _truncate_to_int, left_coercer, right_coercer, coerce_assignment return type
Context: All coercer callables accept and return heterogeneous runtime values (int, float, bool, str, etc.) dispatched at runtime based on type ontology. Currently typed as Callable[[Any], Any]. A RuntimeValue TypeAlias (Union of all concrete runtime primitives, or a Protocol) could replace the Any parameters, making the boundary explicit and enabling stricter checking at call sites.
Epic: red-dragon-r32l

title: Migrate Definition.variable / Use.variable to StorageIdentifier
description: Change Definition.variable and Use.variable from str to StorageIdentifier. Clean up all downstream dicts (produced_from, defs_by_var) to use StorageIdentifier keys. Simplify _trace_to_named_vars: isinstance(reg, VarName) for leaves, isinstance(reg, Register) for intermediates — eliminates _is_temporary_register string heuristic. Fix interprocedural/summaries.py comparison sites. Update tests in test_dataflow.py and test_interprocedural_types.py. Depends on the StorageIdentifier + used_storage/defined_storage issue.
close_reason: Definition.variable and Use.variable changed from str to StorageIdentifier. _defs_of and _uses_of now delegate to instruction.writes()/reads(). Removed _VALUE_PRODUCERS, _VAR_DEFINERS constants (dead code). Removed _is_temporary_register string heuristic — isinstance(id, VarName) for leaves, isinstance(id, Register) for intermediates. All internal dicts (produced_from, defs_by_var, gen_map, local_defs) use StorageIdentifier keys. Added LoadVar.reads() override. 2 test files updated. 13,108 tests passing.

title: StorageIdentifier protocol + used_storage/defined_storage on instruction classes
description:
1. Create interpreter/storage_identifier.py with @runtime_checkable StorageIdentifier protocol (value: str, is_present() -> bool, __str__, __hash__). Both VarName and Register already satisfy it structurally.
2. Add used_storage() -> list[StorageIdentifier] method to InstructionBase (default empty) and override on every typed instruction class to return the typed Register/VarName fields that represent storage reads.
3. Add defined_storage() -> StorageIdentifier | None method — returns result_reg (Register) for value producers, name (VarName) for STORE_VAR/DECL_VAR, None for instructions that don't define storage.
4. Rewrite _defs_of and _uses_of in dataflow.py to call these methods instead of parsing operands.
This decouples dataflow analysis from the stringified operands property.
close_reason: Created StorageIdentifier protocol (interpreter/storage_identifier.py). Added reads() and writes() methods to all 30 instruction classes in instructions.py. Fixed 33 bare-string value_reg sites across 18 frontend files. Reverted _defs_of/_uses_of rewrite — blocked by v11s (remaining bare-string Register fields on obj_reg, left, right, etc.). 13,108 tests passing.

title: Introduce ModulePath domain type for ImportRef.module_path

Flows through 57 sites in resolver.py and imports.py. Language-agnostic opaque wrapper — no parsing, just identity and display. Prevents accidental comparison with file paths.

- FunctionInfo.name: str → FuncName
- ClassInfo.name: str → ClassName
- ClassInfo.parents: tuple[str, ...] → tuple[ClassName, ...]

title: Design null-object pattern for failed function lookups across all dispatch strategies
description:
Currently, function lookup failures are handled inconsistently across dispatch strategies:

1. Builtins: _try_builtin_call returns ExecutionResult.not_handled() when func_name not in TABLE (calls.py:76-77)
2. Scope chain: returns empty string func_val when name not in any frame's local_vars (calls.py:309-314)
3. Class methods: returns empty list from registry.class_methods.get(class_name, {}).get(method_name, []) (calls.py:464-465)
4. Method builtins: returns None from METHOD_TABLE.get(method_name) (calls.py:477)
5. Parent chain: falls through with empty func_label (calls.py:534-554)
6. Final fallback: call_resolver.resolve_call / resolve_method — LLM/symbolic (calls.py:317, 517)

Each failure is checked differently (not in dict, empty string, empty list, None, not_handled). A unified null-object pattern would:
- Define a NullCallable / NOT_A_BUILTIN sentinel distinct from UNCOMPUTABLE
- Make every lookup return a callable (null or real) instead of None/empty
- Unify the three-way dispatch: not-found → try-next-strategy, found-but-uncomputable → symbolic, found-and-computed → concrete

Files: interpreter/handlers/calls.py (primary), interpreter/vm/builtins.py (TABLE/METHOD_TABLE), interpreter/registry.py (class_methods, func_refs).

This is a dispatch architecture improvement, not blocking FuncName migration. FuncName accessors use Optional for now.

title: Rust frontend: migrate Box/Option from CALL_FUNCTION with embedded type params to CALL_CTOR
description:
Rust frontend emits CALL_FUNCTION 'Box[Node]' with generic type params embedded in func_name string. The handler in calls.py:272 has to strip them with split('[')[0]. This should use CALL_CTOR (CallCtorFunction) instead, which has a dedicated type_hint field for the parameterized type.

Current: CallFunction(func_name='Box[Node]', ...) → handler does func_name.split('[')[0] to get 'Box'
Target: CallCtorFunction(func_name='Box', type_hint=ParameterizedType('Box', [scalar('Node')]), ...)

Files: interpreter/frontends/rust/declarations.py (Box::new and Some emission), interpreter/handlers/calls.py:272 (remove split hack).

Blocking FuncName clean-name invariant — FuncName must store clean names only, no embedded type parameters.
close_reason: Dead split hack removed. Frontend already emits clean FuncName. Filed 0tsw for CallCtorFunction follow-up.

title: Push VarName into type_inference.py internal dicts
description:
type_inference.py uses str(name) to key into internal dict[str, ...] dicts when name is now VarName. 8 sites across _infer_load_var (lines 581-594) and _infer_store_var (lines 610-617):

- register_source_var: dict[str, str] (line 198) — maps register name to source variable name. str(name) at line 581.
- var_array_element_types: dict[str, TypeExpr] (line 191) — maps variable name to array element type. str(name) at lines 586-588, 614.
- var_tuple_element_types: dict[str, dict[int, TypeExpr]] (line 195) — maps variable name to tuple element types. str(name) at lines 591-593, 617.
- store_var_type / lookup_var_type: methods on _InferenceContext (lines 207, 224) that take str name param. str(name) at lines 582, 610.

All 4 dicts should change key type from str to VarName. The store_var_type/lookup_var_type method signatures should accept VarName. The scoped_var_types (line 188) which underlies lookup_var_type is dict[str, dict[str, TypeExpr]] — outer key is scope, inner key is var name — inner key should become VarName.

No external consumers of these dicts — they are internal to _InferenceContext. Low risk, self-contained change. No Pydantic models involved (pure dataclass).

Constraint: the TypeEnvironment.scoped_var_types in type_environment.py also uses str var name keys and feeds into lookup_var_type. That may need updating too — check during implementation.

Related: red-dragon-w667 (VarName epic), completed issues b9cd/jdx0/90z9.
close_reason: All str(name) calls replaced with native VarName keys in type_inference.py, type_environment.py, and type_environment_builder.py. var_types also cascaded to VarName keys. 13,017 tests passing.

title: Migrate ClosureEnvironment.bindings keys from str to VarName
description: ClosureEnvironment.bindings: dict[str, TypedValue] in vm_types.py uses variable names as keys. Should be dict[VarName, TypedValue] for consistency after VarName Phase 1b.
close_reason: bindings keys migrated to VarName, bridge wrapping removed. 13,039 tests passing.

title: VarName structured decomposition: add base/scope_id for block-scope mangling
description: Phase 2: Extend VarName from simple wrapper to structured type with base: str and scope_id: int | None. Add from_str() parsing (x$1 → base=x, scope_id=1), is_mangled property, and domain methods (starts_with, etc). Implement when block-scope mangling feature is needed. Depends on b9cd (Phase 1) being complete.
close_reason: YAGNI — VarScopeInfo already provides structured decomposition of mangled names. declare_block_var() produces 'x$1' mangled strings and stores VarScopeInfo(original_name='x', scope_depth=1) as separate metadata. Adding base/scope_id to VarName would duplicate this. No consumer needs to parse mangled names from VarName.value — they use var_scope_metadata to recover the original name. Reopen if a concrete use case emerges where VarName-level decomposition is needed and VarScopeInfo doesn't suffice.

title: Introduce ContinuationName domain type for COBOL continuation fields

title: Introduce FuncName domain type for function/method/class name fields
description: Replace str with FuncName for CallFunction.func_name, CallMethod.method_name, CallCtorFunction.func_name. Used for scope lookup and registry dispatch. 3 fields across 3 instruction classes. Needs brainstorming: should method_name and func_name share a type? How does this interact with builtins table lookup and registry.func_params?

title: NIST M-stub programs need external input data files (9 programs)
description:
Nine NIST "*M" programs skip because they read external input files that are not present
on disk; they never open PRINT-FILE:
  SQ302M, SQ303M, SQ401M, IX301M, IX302M, IX401M, RL301M, RL302M, RL401M

These are multi-program test drivers expecting data produced by a prior program/step in
the NIST harness. Cannot pass without providing those input files.

Options:
- Locate/generate the required input files and seed them via make_provider path_overrides.
- Or accept as won't-fix and document (mark xfail/skip with explicit reason).

Lowest priority. Related: red-dragon-m0oa (epic).

Mixed-kind — would need a union type VarName | Register or a common LocalIdentifier wrapper. Lower priority due to the mixed semantics.

title: Investigate LLMPlausibleResolver gaps: orphan HeapWrites, missing NewObject, type_hint inference
description:
LLMPlausibleResolver (interpreter/vm/unresolved_call.py:185-204) emits HeapWrite without companion NewObject entries. This triggers the defensive heap_ensure fallback in apply_update (vm.py:251-252) which creates untyped HeapObject(type_hint=UNKNOWN).

Known gaps:
1. HeapWrite without NewObject — LLM JSON response doesn't include object creation, only field writes. The address may not exist in the heap.
2. No type_hint inference — LLM doesn't provide type info for heap objects it references.
3. obj_addr values from LLM are arbitrary strings — no validation against existing heap state.
4. The LLM prompt template (lines 145-170) doesn't instruct the LLM to distinguish new objects from field updates on existing objects.

Potential fixes:
- Add NewObject entries to the LLM response schema
- Infer type_hint from context (e.g., the function return type or argument types)
- Validate obj_addr against existing heap before creating orphan objects

Files: interpreter/vm/unresolved_call.py (LLMPlausibleResolver._parse_llm_response, _build_prompt), interpreter/vm/vm.py (apply_update heap_ensure fallback).

Low urgency — the defensive fallback works correctly, just creates untyped objects.

title: Introduce ClosureId domain type for closure environment identifiers
description:
vm.closures uses str keys ('closure_42', 'env_3') for closure environment lookup. These are distinct from heap addresses (obj_/arr_/mem_/sym_) and from other string identifiers. Should be a ClosureId(value: str) domain type.

Fields: VMState.closures dict key, StackFrame.closure_env_id, BoundFuncRef.closure_id. Small scope — COBOL continuations pattern. Depends on v217 (Address) being complete to avoid overlapping migrations.

Files: interpreter/vm/vm_types.py (VMState.closures, StackFrame.closure_env_id), interpreter/refs/func_ref.py (BoundFuncRef.closure_id), interpreter/handlers/calls.py, interpreter/handlers/variables.py.
close_reason: Duplicate of red-dragon-ynpw, closed together

title: Rust frontend: migrate Box/Option from CallFunction to CallCtorFunction for typed constructor dispatch
description:
Box::new and Some are lowered as CallFunction in interpreter/frontends/rust/expressions.py (lines 85, 107). They should use CallCtorFunction with a type_hint field for proper parameterized type propagation.

Current: CallFunction(func_name=FuncName('Box'), args=...) — handler infers type via parse_type(str(base_name)) which returns scalar('Box')
Target: CallCtorFunction(func_name=FuncName('Box'), type_hint=ParameterizedType('Box', [inferred_element_type]), args=...)

The split('[') hack was already removed (n9tr). This is about using the right opcode for constructor dispatch, enabling typed Box[T] and Option[T] propagation through the type system.

Files: interpreter/frontends/rust/expressions.py (_lower_box_new, _lower_some). Low urgency — current approach works, just uses the wrong opcode.

title: Investigate: runtime string → FieldName impedance mismatch at indirect handlers
description:
LoadFieldIndirect and StoreIndirect handlers convert runtime string values (from registers) to FieldName for heap lookups. This is a boundary crossing between the runtime value domain (str in registers) and the heap key domain (FieldName).

Current flow (post-FieldName migration):
1. StoreField: frontend produces FieldName → handler writes to heap with FieldName key
2. LoadFieldIndirect: register holds runtime str → handler wraps FieldName(str) → looks up heap
3. StoreIndirect: Pointer.offset (int) → str(offset) → FieldName(str, INDEX) → HeapWrite
4. _common.py spread: range(len(fields)) → str(i) → FieldName(str(i), INDEX) → fields lookup

The wrapping at step 2-4 is a necessary impedance mismatch — runtime values are strings, heap keys are FieldName. Not a bug, but worth investigating whether:
- CONST instructions could produce FieldName values directly (eliminates wrap at LoadFieldIndirect)
- Pointer.offset could store FieldName instead of int (eliminates double str() conversion)
- Both would mean FieldName becomes a runtime value type, which has architectural implications

Files: interpreter/handlers/memory.py (LoadFieldIndirect ~line 237, StoreIndirect ~line 289), interpreter/handlers/_common.py (spread args ~line 27), interpreter/vm/vm.py (alias write ~line 283).

This may not need fixing — the current wrap-at-boundary pattern is correct and explicit. Filed for future analysis only.

title: Introduce Address domain type for HeapWrite.obj_addr and heap address fields
description:
HeapWrite.obj_addr is str representing a heap object address (e.g. 'obj_0', 'arr_3', 'mem_0'). Also used as keys in vm.heap: dict[str, HeapObject], Pointer.base: str, and NewObject.addr: str. Should be a domain type Address(value: str) to prevent interchange with other strings.

Files: interpreter/vm/vm_types.py (HeapWrite.obj_addr, NewObject.addr, Pointer.base), interpreter/vm/vm.py (vm.heap keys). ~50 construction sites in handlers/builtins + tests.

Low urgency — addresses are already structurally distinct (prefixed with obj_/arr_/mem_/sym_) and not easily confused with other string types.
close_reason: Address domain type complete: VMState accessors (heap_get/set/contains/ensure/items/keys, region_get/set) as permanent API. vm._heap: dict[Address, HeapObject], vm._regions: dict[Address, bytearray] — both private. Pointer.base, HeapWrite.obj_addr, NewObject.addr, RegionWrite.region_addr all Address. NullHeapObject for miss returns. No str bridge. 13,106 tests passing.

title: Migrate SymbolTable and FunctionRegistry to domain type keys
description:
SymbolTable and FunctionRegistry use raw str keys for all name types:

- ClassInfo.fields: dict[str, FieldInfo] — should be dict[FieldName, FieldInfo]
- ClassInfo.methods: dict[str, FunctionInfo] — should be dict[FuncName, FunctionInfo] (depends on cnz9)
- ClassInfo.constants: dict[str, str] — constant names, may stay str
- ClassInfo.name: str — should be class name domain type
- ClassInfo.parents: tuple[str, ...] — class names
- SymbolTable.classes: dict[str, ClassInfo] — class names
- SymbolTable.functions: dict[str, FunctionInfo] — function names
- SymbolTable.resolve_field(class_name: str, field_name: str) — both params need domain types
- FunctionRegistry.func_params: dict[CodeLabel, list[str]] — param names should be list[VarName]
- FunctionRegistry.class_methods: dict[str, dict[str, list[CodeLabel]]] — class name → method name
- FunctionRegistry.classes: dict[str, CodeLabel] — class names
- FunctionRegistry.class_parents: dict[str, list[str]] — class names
- FunctionRegistry.func_refs: dict[str, FuncRef] — function names

Files: interpreter/frontends/symbol_table.py, interpreter/registry.py.

Currently the boundary between domain-typed instructions and str-keyed symbol tables requires str() unwrapping. This is the general pattern — not specific to any one domain type. Depends on j0h1 (FieldName), cnz9 (FuncName), w667 (VarName) being complete.

Low urgency — current str() unwrapping at the boundary is correct and explicit.

title: Review Symbolic.hint representation
description: Symbolic.hint is currently str, holding freeform descriptive text ('__param__x', 'unknown_import'). May benefit from a structured hint type if patterns emerge, or may be fine as freeform str. Placeholder for future review.

title: Review LoadRegion/WriteRegion.length representation
description: LoadRegion.length and WriteRegion.length are currently int, holding byte counts for COBOL byte-addressed memory. May benefit from a ByteLength type for clarity, or may be fine as plain int given COBOL-only usage. Placeholder for future review.

title: Review Const.value representation
description: Const.value is currently str, holding polymorphic literal values ('42', 'True', '"hello"', '<function:fib@func_fib_0>'). May benefit from a typed ConstValue ADT, or may be fine as str since parsing happens at VM runtime. Placeholder for future review.
